# SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL

This section contains the parameter descriptions for the devices listed below. The parameter descriptions for the various devices can differ. If this is the case, then in the topic under "Object", the Control Unit is listed for which the description applies. In the table of contents, you will then see a separate entry for each parameter number. The following Control Units are taken into account in the online help:

- SINAMICS G120

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 00002 - 00203

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 00002 - 00203

### r0002 Drive operating display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 250 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Operating display for the drive.

**Value:**
  
0:
Operation - everything enabled  
10:
Operation - set "enable setpoint" = "1" (p1142)  
11:
Operation - set "enable speed controller" = "1" (p0856)  
12:
Operation - RFG frozen, set "RFG start" = "1" (p1141)  
13:
Operation - set "enable RFG" = "1" (p1140)  
21:
Ready for operation - set "Operation enable" = "1" (p0852)  
30:
Ready for operation- "wait for feedback signals"  
31:
Ready for switching on - set "ON/OFF1" = "0/1" (p0840)  
41:
Switching on inhibited - set "ON/OFF1" = "0" (p0840)  
42:
Switching on inhibited - set "OC/OFF2" = "1" (p0844, p0845)  
43:
Switching on inhibited - set "OC/OFF3" = "1" (p0848, p0849)  
45:
Switching on inhibited - remove fault cause, acknowledge fault  
46:
Switching on inhibited - exit commissioning mode (p0009, p0010)  
47:
Switching on inhibited - refer to r0046  
70:
Initialization  
200:
Wait for booting/partial booting  
250:
Device signals a topology error

**Notice:**
  
For several missing enable signals, the corresponding value with the highest number
is displayed.

**Note:**
  
OC: Operating condition  
EP: Enable Pulses (pulse enable)  
RFG: Ramp-function generator  
COMM: Commissioning

### p0010 Drive commissioning parameter filter

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2800, 2818 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 30 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the parameter filter to commission a drive.  
Setting this parameter filters out the parameters that can be written into in the
various commissioning steps.

**Value:**
  
0:
Ready  
1:
Quick commissioning  
2:
Power unit commissioning  
3:
Motor commissioning  
4:
Encoder commissioning  
5:
Technological application/units  
15:
Data sets  
17:
Basic positioner commissioning  
25:
Position control commissioning  
29:
Only Siemens internal  
30:
Parameter reset

**Note:**
  
The drive can only be switched on outside the drive commissioning (inverter enable).
To realize this, this parameter must be set to 0.  
By setting p3900 to a value other than 0, the quick commissioning is completed, and
this parameter is automatically reset to 0.  
Procedure for "Reset parameter": Set p0010 to 30 and p0970 to 1.

### r0020 CO: Speed setpoint smoothed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6799 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [rpm] | - [rpm] | [ ] - [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the currently smoothed speed setpoint at the input of the speed controller
or U/f characteristic (after the interpolator).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0060

**Note:**
  
Smoothing time constant = 100 ms  
The signal is not suitable as a process quantity and may only be used as a display
quantity.  
The speed setpoint is available smoothed (r0020) and unsmoothed (r0060).

| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

### r0022 Speed actual value rpm smoothed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |

quantity.  
The speed actual value is available smoothed (r0021, r0022) and unsmoothed (r0063).

### r0026 CO: MSC DC link voltage smoothed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6799 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**5_3 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [V] | - [V] | [ ] - [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the smoothed actual value of the DC link voltage of the motor-side converter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0070

**Notice:**
  
This smoothed signal is not suitable for diagnostics or evaluation of dynamic operations.
In this case, the unsmoothed value should be used.

**Note:**
  
Smoothing time constant = 500 ms  
The signal is not suitable as a process quantity and may only be used as a display
quantity.  
The DC link voltage is available smoothed (r0026) and unsmoothed (r0070).

### r0032 CO: Active power actual value smoothed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |

### r0033 CO: Torque utilization smoothed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8012 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the smoothed torque utilization as a percentage.  
The torque utilization is obtained from the required smoothed torque in reference
to the torque limit, scaled using p2196.

**Note:**
  
Smoothing time constant = 100 ms  
The signal is not suitable as a process quantity and may only be used as a display
quantity.  
The torque utilization is available smoothed (r0033) and unsmoothed (r0081).  
For M_set total (r0079) > 0, the following applies:  
- Required torque = M_set total  
- Actual torque limit = M_max upper effective (r1538)  
For M_set total (r0079) <= 0, the following applies:  
- Required torque = - M_set total  
- Actual torque limit = - M_max lower effective (r1539)  
For the actual torque limit = 0, the following applies: r0033 = 100 %  
For the actual torque limit < 0, the following applies: r0033 = 0 %

### r0034 CO: Motor utilization thermal

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the thermal motor utilization. The utilization is
calculated based on an I2t model, which emulates the energy entered resulting from
the stator current. The ambient temperature is not taken into consideration.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F07011

**Note:**
  
Smoothing time constant = 100 ms

### r0037[0...19] CO: Power unit temperatures

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9796 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**21_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2006 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°C] | - [°C] | [ ] - [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the temperature in the power unit.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Cooling liquid module stack maximum value  
[
1]:
Cooling liquid DC link reactor maximum value  
[
2]:
Inner cabinet temperature maximum value  
[
3]:
Electronics module power unit maximum value  
[
4]:
Cooling liquid module stack PSA1  
[
5]:
Cooling liquid module stack PSA2  
[
6]:
Cooling liquid module stack PSA3  
[
7]:
Cooling liquid module stack PSA4  
[
8]:
Cooling liquid DC link reactor PSA1  
[
9]:
Cooling liquid DC link reactor PSA2  
[
10]:
Cooling liquid DC link reactor PSA3  
[
11]:
Cooling liquid DC link reactor PSA4  
[
12]:
Inner cabinet temperature PSA1  
[
13]:
Inner cabinet temperature PSA2  
[
14]:
Inner cabinet temperature PSA3  
[
15]:
Inner cabinet temperature PSA4  
[
16]:
Electronics module power unit PSA1  
[
17]:
Electronics module power unit PSA2  
[
18]:
Electronics module power unit PSA3  
[
19]:
Electronics module power unit PSA4

**Notice:**
  
Only for internal Siemens troubleshooting.

**Note:**
  
The value of -200 indicates that there is no measuring signal (sensor not connected
or the sensor has a fault condition).

### r0038 MSC power factor smoothed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6799 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the smoothed actual power factor of the motor-side converter.

**Notice:**
  
For infeed units, the following applies:  
For active powers < 25 % of the rated power, this does not provide any useful information.

**Note:**
  
Smoothing time constant = 300 ms  
The signal is not suitable as a process quantity and may only be used as a display
quantity.

### r0042[0...2] CO: Process energy display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Wh] | - [Wh] | [ ] - [Wh] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the energy values at the output terminals of the
power unit.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Energy balance (sum)  
[
1]:
Energy drawn  
[
2]:
Energy fed back

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0043

**Note:**
  
The signal can be displayed as process variable (scaling: 1 = 1 Wh).  
This is enabled in p0043.  
The display is also reset with p0040 = 1.  
If an enable is present in r0043 when the Control Unit powers up, then the value from
r0039 is transferred into r0042.  
As r0039 serves as a reference signal for r0042, due to format reasons, the process
energy display can only process values of r0039 up to 2147483 kWh. r0039 should also
be reset using this value.

### r0046.0...31 CO/BO: Missing enable sig

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2634 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the missing enable signals that prevent operation.  
To exit the "switching on inhibited" state, bits 0, 1, 2, 4 ... 7, 14 ... 22, 24 ...
31 must all have a "0".  
To change into the state "Operation" (r3402), bits 0 ... 9, 13 ... 31 must all have
a "0".  
A "1" in bits 10 ... 12 has no effect.  
Drives without redundant power unit:  
The value in bits 16, 21, 28 ... 31 is obtained from the logical OR operation of the
same bits of all indices of r6225.  
Drives with redundant power unit:  
A minimum of a "1" in the bits 16, 20, 21, 26, 28 ... 31 of all indices of r6225 prevents
the complete (total) system from being switched on. Bits 16, 20, 21, 26, 28 ... 31
show the result of the logical OR operation of the same bits of all indices of r6225.  
At least one "1" in bits 8, 9 in all indices of r6225 is preventing the complete (total)
system from being enabled for operation. Bits 8, 9 then indicate the result of the
logical OR combination of the same bits of all indices of r6225.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | ON command still active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | OFF2 enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | OFF3 enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Operation enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | 2-channel off enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | External switching on inhibited active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Commissioning active | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Drive converter cooling not ready for switching on | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Drive converter cooling not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Line PLL not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Ramp-function generator enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Ramp-function generator start missing | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Setpoint enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Transformer inrush incoming transformer active | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Output transformer de-magnetizing active | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Output transformer not de-magnetized | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Ext. enable signals missing | No | Yes | 2635 |
| 17 | Excitation equipment not ready or line operation | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Parallel switching unit fault present | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | Subsystem ON command still active | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | Reactor, I2T load too high | No | Yes | 8038 |
| 22 | Safety circuit open | Closed | Open | - |
| 23 | Cooling-down time after turn mode active | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Motor data set changeover active | No | Yes | - |
| 25 | Switching on inhibited motor sequence control active | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | Drive passive or subsystem not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 27 | Internal output disconnector not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 28 | Subsystem off enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | Fault in the power unit | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | Circuit breaker control blocked | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | Circuit breaker not ready | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0002, r0878, r0898, r3402, r6225, p6230, p6265, p6318, r6592

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
This bit indicates whether, in the "switching on inhibited" state (r3402), an ON command
(r0898.0 = 1) is still present.  
The "switching on inhibited" state is only exited if the ON command is withdrawn (r0898.0
= 0) and the cause of the missing enable signals has been resolved.  
For p6265:  
Depending on the operating mode set in p6265, the following "missing enable signals"
have no effect, but they are always still displayed:  
p6265 = 0, 100: Normal operation, semi-manual operation  
- all missing enable signals are evaluated.  
p6265 = 1: Test operation rotor position sensing  
- bits 7 ... 9, 18, 21, 30, 31 are not evaluated.  
p6265 = 2, 10: (current controlled operation), circulating operation  
- bits 17, 18, 23 are not evaluated  
p6265 = 3: Determination offset angle line-side converter  
- bits 17, 18, 21, 23, 27 are not evaluated.  
p6265 = 110: Manual operation  
- bits 7, 8, 17, 18, 23, 27, 30, 31 are not evaluated.  
For p6318:  
p6318 = 0: VSM voltage sensing upstream of the incoming circuit breaker  
- bit 9 is also evaluated to exit "switching on inhibited'.  
p6318 = 1: VSM voltage sensing downstream of the incoming circuit breaker  
- bit 9 is evaluated to change into the run operating state. Bit 9 is not evaluated
to exit "switching on inhibited".

### r0063[0...2] CO: Speed actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 5019, 4700, 4710 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [rpm] | - [rpm] | [ ] - [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the speed actual value.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Unsmoothed  
[
1]:
Smoothed with p0045  
[
2]:
Calculated from f_set - f_slip (unsmoothed)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0021, r0022

**Note:**
  
The speed actual value is calculated in encoderless operation and for U/f control.  
For operation with encoder, r0063[0] is smoothed with p1441.  
The speed actual value r0063[0] – smoothed with p0045 – is additionally displayed
in r0063[1]. r0063[1] can be used as process variable for the appropriate smoothing
time constant p0045.  
The speed (r0063[2]) calculated from the output frequency and slip can only be compared
with the speed actual value (r0063[0]) in the steady-state.  
The actual speed (r0063[0]) is available as a display quantity with additional smoothing
in r0021.

[
5]:
Phase W derivation

**Note:**
  
For several systems, the total value is displayed.  
The derivations of the currents of 3 phases are displayed in indices 3 ... 5.

### r0081 CO: Torque utilization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8012 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the torque utilization as a percentage.  
The torque utilization is obtained from the required smoothed torque referred to the
torque limit.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0033

**Note:**
  
The torque utilization is available smoothed (r0033) and unsmoothed (r0081).  
The torque utilization is obtained from the required torque referred to the torque
limit as follows:  
- Positive torque: r0081 = (r0079 / r1538) * 100 %  
- Negative torque: r0081 = (-r0079 / -r1539) * 100 %

0]:

[
1]:
Smoothed with p0045  
[
2]:
Power drawn  
[
3]:
From setpoint and smoothed with p0045

r0032

**Note:**
  
The active power is available smoothed (r0032 with 500 ms, r0082[1] with p0045) and
unsmoothed (r0082[0]). The unsmoothed electric motor active power is displayed in
r0082[2] without taking into account the motor losses.  
The smoothed electrical motor active power calculated from the Isq setpoint is displayed
in r0082[3].

excited synchronous motors. In the following cases, the unsmoothed flux actual value
is also displayed:

- during the pole position identification.  
- for I/f control.  
- for a stalled drive.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0093, p0431, r1778

**Note:**
  
The transformation angle corresponds to the electrical commutation angle.  
If no pole position identification is carried out (p1982), and the encoder is adjusted,
the following applies:  
The encoder supplies the value and indicates the electrical angle of the flux position
(d axis).

### p0100 IEC/NEMA standards

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**SESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Defines whether the converter and motor power settings (e.g. rated motor power, p0307)
are expressed in [kW] or [hp].  
Depending on the selection, the rated motor frequency (p0310) is either set to 50
Hz or 60 Hz.  
For p0100 = 0, the following applies: The power factor (p0308) should be parameterized.  
For p0100 = 1, the following applies: The efficiency (p0309) should be parameterized.

**Value:**
  
0:
IEC (50 Hz line, SI units)  
1:
NEMA (60 Hz line, US units)

**Dependency:**
  
If p0100 is changed, all of the rated motor parameters are reset. Only then are possible
unit changeovers made.  
The units of all motor parameters are changed that are involved in the selection of
IEC or NEMA (e.g. r0206, p0307, p0316, r0333, r0334, p0341, p0344, r1493, r1969).  
  
Refer to:
r0206, p0210, r0210, p0300, p0304, p0305, p0307, p0308, p0309, p0310, p0311, p0312,
p0314, p0320, p0322, p0323, p0335, r0336, r0337

**Note:**
  
The parameter can only be changed for vector control (p0107).  
The parameter value is not reset when the factory setting is restored (p0010 = 30,
p0970).

### p0105 Activate/deactivate drive object

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate a drive object.

**Value:**
  
0:
Deactivate drive object  
1:
Activate drive object  
2:
Drive object deactivate and not present

**Dependency:**
  
When activating drive objects with the safety functions enabled, the following applies:  
After reactivating, a warm restart (p0009 = 30, p0976 = 2, 3) or POWER ON should be
carried out.  
  
Refer to:
r0106  
Refer to:
A01314

**Notice:**
  
The following applies when activating:  
If components are inserted for the first time and the appropriate drive object is
activated, then the drive system is automatically booted. To do this, the pulses of
all of the drive objects must be cancelled.

**Note:**
  
For value = 0, 2:  
When a drive object is deactivated it no longer outputs any errors.  
For value = 0:  
All components of the drive object were completely commissioned and are deactivated
using this value. They can be removed from the DRIVE-CLiQ without any error.  
If a component has been deactivated, only the component with the correct serial number
may be inserted, or none at all.  
For value = 1:  
All components of the drive object must be available for error-free operation.  
For value = 2:  
Components of a drive object in a project generated offline and set to this value
must never be inserted in the actual topology from the very start. This means that
the components are marked to be bypassed in the DRIVE-CLiQ line.  
For components that comprise several individual components (e.g. Double Motor Modules),
it is not permissible to set just one subset to this value.

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 02 | Closed-loop speed/torque control / n/M | Not activated | Activated | - |
| 08 | Extended setpoint channel / Ext setp | Not activated | Activated | - |
| 18 | Free function blocks / FBLOCKS | Not activated | Activated | - |
| 27 | Fan / Fan | Not activated | Activated | - |
| 28 | Cooling unit / Cool_unit | Not activated | Activated | - |
| 29 | CAN / CAN | Not activated | Activated | - |
| 31 | PROFINET CBE20 / PN CBE20 | Not activated | Activated | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0171, r0172, r0173

**Note:**
  
A "function module" is a functional expansion of a drive object that can be activated
when commissioning.  
DO: Drive Object

### p0112 Sampling times pre-setting p0115

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 5 | [ 0 ] 3 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Pre-assignment of the sampling times in p0115.  
The sampling times for the current controller/speed controller/flux controller/setpoint
channel / - / - / technology controller are pre-assigned as follows:  
p0112 = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5: 500 / 1500 / 1500 / 3000 / - / - / 1500 µs

**Recommend.:**
  
When changing the sampling times of the current and speed controllers (also refer
to p0115), then we recommend that after exiting commissioning (p0009 = 0) the controller
settings are re-calculated using p0340 = 4.

**Value:**
  
0:
Expert  
1:
xLow  
2:
Low  
3:
Standard  
4:
High  
5:
xHigh

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0092

**Note:**
  
For p0112 = 0 (expert) the individual sampling times in p0115 can be adjusted.

### p0115[0...6] Sampling times for internal control loops

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 500.00 [µs] | 16000.00 [µs] | [ 0 ] 500.00 [µs]  [ 1 ] 1500.00 [µs]  [ 2 ] 1500.00 [µs]  [ 3 ] 3000.00 [µs]  [ 4 ] 3000.00 [µs]  [ 5 ] 3000.00 [µs]  [ 6 ] 1500.00 [µs] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the sampling times for the control loops.  
The default setting is made using p0112 and can only be individually changed for p0112
= 0 (expert).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Current controller  
[
1]:
Speed controller  
[
2]:
Flux controller  
[
3]:
Setpoint channel  
[
4]:
Pos controller  
[
5]:
Positioning  
[
6]:
Technology controller

**Dependency:**
  
The sampling times can only be separately set if p0112 is 0 (expert). If a sampling
time is modified in the expert mode, then all of the sampling times with higher indices
are automatically changed in the same ratio as the sampling time itself was changed.
Slower time slices are only taken if the calculated sampling time is also permitted.
Upper limit is 8 ms.  
Higher-level controls must be calculated in integral ratios to lower-level controls
(e.g. p0115[1] = N * p0115[0]; where N is an integer number). The sampling time of
the speed controller (p0115[1]) can have as a maximum a value of 800% of the current
controller sampling time (p0115[0]).  
For servo drives, the maximum sampling time of the current controller is 250 µs and
for vector drives, 500 µs.  
The sampling times for setpoint channel (p0115[3]), position controller (p0115[4]),
positioning (p0115[5]) and technology controller (p0115[6]) must have at least 2x
the value of the current controller sampling time (p0115[0]).  
  
Refer to:
r0110, r0111, p0112

**Note:**
  
For function modules that can be activated (e.g. technology controller), the parameters
values are pre-assigned.

| --- | --- | --- |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

r00116[0] = recommended sampling time:  
Recommended value which would then make the complete system operational.  
r00116[1] = recommended sampling time:  
Recommended value, which after changing other clock cycles on the DRIVE-CLiQ line,
would result in an operational system.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Change only for the actual drive object  
[
1]:
Changing all objects on the DRIVE-CLiQ line

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0115

### p0125[0...n] Activate/deactivate power unit components

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 4) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Data sets | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate a power unit component.

**Recommend.:**
  
After inserting a component, before activating, first wait for Alarm A01317.

**Value:**
  
0:
Deactivate component  
1:
Activate component  
2:
Component deactivate and not present

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0126  
Refer to:
A01314, A01317

**Caution:**
  
For a parallel connection, the following applies:  
When deactivating individual power units using this parameter, it is not permissible
that the power units of the parallel connection involved are connected. Infeed units
should be disconnected from the line supply (for example, using a contactor). Motor
feeder cables should be disconnected. In addition, defective power units should be
disconnected from the DC link.

**Notice:**
  
Before deactivating a power unit, the user must ensure that if required this power
unit is removed from the trip line and this is then reconfigured.

**Note:**
  
The activation of a component can be rejected if the component was inserted for the
first time. In this case, it is only possible to activate the component when the pulses
for all of the drive objects are inhibited.  
For value = 0, 2:  
When a component is deactivated it no longer outputs any errors.  
For value = 0:  
The component was completely commissioned and is deactivated using this value. It
can be removed from the DRIVE-CLiQ without any error.  
For value = 1:  
The component must be available for error-free operation.  
For value = 2:  
A component in a project generated offline and set to this value must never be inserted
in the actual topology from the very start. This means that the component is marked
to be bypassed in the DRIVE-CLiQ line.  
For components that comprise several individual components (e.g. Double Motor Modules),
it is not permissible to set just one subset to this value.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Source motor data set  
[
1]:
Target motor data set  
[
2]:
Start copying procedure

**Note:**
  
Procedure:  
1. In Index 0, enter which motor data set should be copied.  
2. In Index 1, enter the motor data set data that is to be copied into.  
3. Start copying: set index 2 from 0 to 1.  
p0139[2] is automatically set to 0 when copying is completed.  
When copying, p0131 is not taken into account.

### p0141[0...n] Encoder interface (Sensor Module) component number

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 4) C2( 15) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** 4704, 8570 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Data sets | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**

**Description:**
  
This parameter is used to assign the encoder data set to an encoder evaluation (e.g.
SMC).  
This unique component number is assigned when parameterizing the topology.  
Only a component number can be entered that corresponds to an encoder evaluation.

**Note:**
  
If the encoder evaluation and encoder are integrated (motor with DRIVE-CLiQ), then
their component numbers are identical.  
For an SMC, different component numbers are assigned for the SMC (p0141) and the (actual)
encoder (p0142).  
SMC: Sensor Module Cabinet

encoder (p0142).

### p0145[0...n] Activate/deactivate encoder interface

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 4) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Data sets | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate an encoder interface (Sensor Module).

**Recommend.:**
  
After inserting a component, before activating, first wait for Alarm A01317.

**Value:**
  
0:
Deactivate component  
1:
Activate component  
2:
Component deactivate and not present

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0146

A01314, A01317

**Note:**
  
The deactivation of an encoder interface corresponds to the"parking encoder" function
and has the same effect.  
The activation of a component can be rejected if the component was inserted for the
first time.  
In this case, it is only possible to activate the component when the pulses for all
of the drive objects are inhibited.  
With the encoder interface for encoder 1 (motor encoder), the relevant drive object
for writing the parameter must be in the "Ready for operation" state.  
With the encoder interface for encoders 2 and 3, the parameter can also be written
during operation.  
For value = 0, 2:  
When a component is deactivated it no longer outputs any errors.  
For value = 0:  
The component was completely commissioned and is deactivated using this value. It
can be removed from the DRIVE-CLiQ without any error.  
For value = 1:  
The component must be available for error-free operation.  
For value = 2:  
A component in a project generated offline and set to this value must never be inserted
in the actual topology from the very start.  
For components that comprise several individual components (e.g. Double Motor Modules),
it is not permissible to set just one subset to this value.

### r0146[0...n] Encoder interface active/inactive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

2.6

1:

### p0187[0...n] Encoder 1 encoder data set number

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 4) C2( 15) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 4700, 8570 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Data sets | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 99 | [ 0 ] 99 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Assign a drive data set (= index) the corresponding encoder data set (EDS) for encoder
1.  
The value corresponds to the number of the assigned encoder data set.  
Example:  
Encoder 1 in drive data set 2 should be assigned to encoder data set 0.  
--> p0187[2] = 0

**Note:**
  
A value of 99 means that no encoder has been assigned to this drive data set (not
configured).

For the commissioning tool, this name cannot be entered using the expert list, but
is specified in the configuration wizards. The object name can be subsequently modified
in the Project Navigator using standard Windows resources.

**Note:**
  
The parameter is not influenced by setting the factory setting.

**Note:**
  
For parallel circuit configurations, the parameter index is assigned to a power unit.

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 00204 - 00402

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 00204 - 00402

### r0204 Power unit hardware properties

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the power unit topology.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | LSC 1 bridge A | Not present | Available | - |
| 01 | LSC 1 bridge B | Not present | Available | - |
| 02 | LSC 2 bridge A | Not present | Available | - |
| 03 | LSC 2 bridge B | Not present | Available | - |
| 04 | DC link | 2DC configuration | 1DC configuration | - |
| 05 | 1 DC config supports motor op with just one winding system (HM) | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | MSC 1 | Not present | Available | - |
| 10 | MSC 2 | Not present | Available | - |

### r0205 Power unit application

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10 | 90 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the application of the power unit

**Value:**
  
10:
Starting converter without redundancy  
11:
Starting converter with power unit redundancy  
12:
Starting converter with CU redundancy  
13:
Starting converter with power unit and CU redundancy  
14:
Starting converter with CU hot standby redundancy  
15:
Starting converter with power unit and CU hot standby redundancy  
20:
Continuous converter without redundancy  
21:
Continuous converter with power unit redundancy  
22:
Continuous converter with CU redundancy  
23:
Continuous converter with power unit and CU redundancy  
24:
Continuous converter with CU hot standby redundancy  
25:
Continuous converter w/ power unit and CU hot standby redundancy  
30:
Light converter without redundancy  
31:
Light converter with power unit redundancy  
32:
Light converter with CU redundancy  
33:
Light converter with power unit and CU redundancy  
34:
Light converter with CU hot standby redundancy  
35:
Light converter with power unit and CU hot standby redundancy  
90:
Rectifier

### r0210 Drive unit line supply voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Vrms] | - [Vrms] | [ ] - [Vrms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the drive unit supply voltage from the hardware description data.

**Notice:**
  
If, in the switched-off state (pulse inhibit), the supply voltage is higher than the
entered value, the Vdc controller may be automatically deactivated in some cases to
prevent the motor from accelerating the next time the system is switched on. In this
case, an appropriate alarm A07401 is output.

### p0247[0...n] MSC transformer, vector group for voltage measurement on the PS

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 111 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Enables the primary-side measurement (PS) at the MSC transformer, and sets the vector
group (VG) of the transformer.

**Value:**
  
0:
No PS measurement  
100:
PS voltage measurement at transformer with VG0  
101:
PS voltage measurement at transformer with VG1  
105:
PS voltage measurement at transformer with VG5  
106:
PS voltage measurement at transformer with VG7  
111:
PS voltage measurement at transformer with VG11

**Note:**
  
The vector group (VG) defines the offset angle in 30° increments between the MSC transformer
primary and secondary voltage. The primary voltage leads the secondary voltage by
VG*30°.  
Here, for drives with 2 DC links, the vector group should be set in subsystem 1.

### p0250 Power unit operating hours

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [h] | 4294967295 [h] | [ 0 ] 0 [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the operating hours of the power unit.  
The motor operating time counter continues to run when the pulses are enabled. When
the pulse enable is withdrawn, the counter is held and the value is saved in a non-volatile
fashion.

**Note:**
  
The operating hours counter can only be reset to 0.

### p0253 Power unit starting operations counter

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 4294967295 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the number of starting operations of the power unit.  
The counter for the number of starting operations is incremented each time the pulses
are enabled and the value is saved in a non-volatile fashion.

**Note:**
  
The counter for the number of starting operations can only be reset to 0.

### p0260 Cooling unit starting time 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9794, 9795 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 60.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 5.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets starting time 1 to monitor the cooling unit after switch-on command.  
After powering up, the following feedback signals must be present within starting
time 1:  
- "RKA switched on" (r0267.0)  
- "RKA differential pressure > alarm threshold" (r0275, p6298)  
When a fault occurs, an appropriate message is output.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F49159, A49175

**Note:**
  
RKA: cooling system  
If a differential pressure monitoring function has not been configured (hardware description
data), this starting time expires independently of the feedback signals.

### p0261 Cooling unit, starting time 2

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 1200.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 180.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets starting time 2 to monitor the cooling unit after switch-on command.  
After powering up, the following feedback signals must be present within starting
time 2:  
- "RKA ready" (r0267.1)  
- "no alarm RKA" for p0276.1 = 0  
- "no temperature alarm" for p0276.4 = 0  
- "no differential pressure alarm" for p0276.3 = 0

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F49159

### p0262 Cooling unit fault delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 5.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for the following messages in operation:  
- F49158 "Cooling unit: Differential pressure has fallen below the fault threshold"  
- F49159 "RKA switched on"  
- F49159 "RKA ready"  
- A49178 "wire break pressure actual value sensing"

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F49158, F49159

**Note:**
  
The fault is only output if the cause is present for a time longer than is set in
this parameter.

### r0265.0...3 BO: Cooling unit control word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the control word for the cooling unit.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Switch on cooling unit | Deactivating | Switch on | - |
| 01 | Message converter off | ON | OFF | - |
| 02 | Acknowledge faults | No acknowledgment | Acknowledgment | - |
| 03 | Leakage sensing responded | No leaked liquid | Leaked liquid | - |

### p0266[0...3] BI: Cooling unit feedback signals signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for the feedback signals from the cooling unit.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Cooling unit switched on  
[
1]:
Cooling unit ready for switching on  
[
2]:
Cooling unit alarm  
[
3]:
Cooling unit, no fault present

**Dependency:**
  
The status of the signals is displayed in r0267.  
  
Refer to:
r0267  
Refer to:
F49159

| 01 | RKA ready | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | RKA alarm occurred | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | RKA no fault present | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0266

### p0271[0...3] Cooling unit actual values smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [s] | 1.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.5 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time for the analog actual values for liquid cooling.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Temperature module stack  
[
1]:
Temperature DC link reactor  
[
2]:
Liquid pressure intake  
[
3]:
Liquid pressure outlet

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037, r0273, r0274

**Note:**
  
The output of fault messages is delayed depending on the set smoothing time in order
to decouple the sensor errors from the limit value violations.

### p0276 Cooling unit configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9795 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0001 0010 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration word for the control and monitoring of the cooling unit.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | No shutdown/switching on inhib. of cooling unit when leak occurs | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | No switching on inhibited converter for cooling unit alarm | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | No switching on inhibited converter for alarm pressure > p_max | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | No switching on inhibited conv. for differential pressure alarm | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | No switching on inhibited converter for temperature alarm | No | Yes | - |

### p0282[0...5] Line supply undervoltage threshold upper

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6746, 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 5.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 80.0 [%]  [ 1 ] 80.0 [%]  [ 2 ] 100.0 [%]  [ 3 ] 20.0 [%]  [ 4 ] 60.0 [%]  [ 5 ] 100.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the upper thresholds for identifying a line undervoltage condition.  
Thresholds for the absolute value of the line voltage (= amplitude), the positive
phase-sequence system of the line voltage and the negative phase-sequence system of
the line voltage can be set.  
When one of the thresholds in index 0 or 1 is fallen below - or exceeded in index
2 - then the pulses are inhibited and the line PLL remains in closed-loop control.  
Re-enabling is realized if the absolute value and the positive phase-sequence system
> threshold value and negative phase-sequence system < threshold value.  
When function "eLVRT" is activated (p3486.0 = 1), the pulses are not inhibited and
the converter remains operational for the time that has been set (p3488).  
The eLVRT is canceled if one of the thresholds in index 3 or 4 was fallen below or
was exceeded in index 5. The pulses are inhibited and the line PLL remain in closed-loop
control. Re-enabling is realized as described above.  
The setting is made as a percentage of the drive unit supply voltage (p0210).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Absolute line voltage value  
[
1]:
Positive phase-sequence system  
[
2]:
Negative phase-sequence system  
[
3]:
Absolute line voltage value eLVRT  
[
4]:
Positive phase-sequence system eLVRT  
[
5]:
Negative phase-sequence system eLVRT

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0288, r6312, r6313, r6433  
Refer to:
F06100, A06105, F06108, N06109

**Note:**
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)  
The following setting rules apply:  
Value in index 0 > value in index 3  
Value in index 0 > value in index 0 (p283)  
Value in index 1 > value in index 4  
Value in index 1 > value in index 1 (p283)  
Value in index 2 < value in index 5

### p0283[0...1] Line supply undervoltage threshold lower

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6746, 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 5 [%] | 100 [%] | [ 0 ] 20 [%]  [ 1 ] 20 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower thresholds for the line supply undervoltage.  
Thresholds for the absolute value of the line voltage (= amplitude) and the positive
phase-sequence system of the line voltage can be set.  
When one of these thresholds is fallen below, the pulses are inhibited and the free-running
of the line PLL is forced – and is not controlled from the line supply.  
The setting is made as a percentage of the drive unit supply voltage (p0210).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Absolute line voltage value  
[
1]:
Positive phase-sequence system

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6312, r6313, r6433  
Refer to:
F06100, A06105

**Note:**
  
The upper threshold must be set higher than the lower threshold.  
The following setting rules apply:  
Value in index 0 < value in index 0 (p282)  
Value in index 1 < value in index 1 (p282)

### p0284 Line supply frequency exceeded threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 100.0 [%] | 300.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 150.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold for an excessively high line supply frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
Set as a percentage of the rated line frequency.  
  
Refer to:
p0211, r6310, r6311  
Refer to:
F06500

**Note:**
  
When the threshold is exceeded, the initialization procedure to determine the line
supply frequency is re-started. If this initialization procedure is unsuccessful after
several repeated attempts, then an appropriate fault is output.

### p0285 Line supply frequency undershot, threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 80.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold for an excessively low line supply frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
Set as a percentage of the rated line frequency.  
  
Refer to:
p0211, r6310, r6311  
Refer to:
F06500

**Note:**
  
When the threshold is undershot, the initialization procedure to determine the line
supply frequency is re-started. If this initialization procedure is unsuccessful after
several repeated attempts, then an appropriate fault is output.

### p0286 Line supply voltage voltage step threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6746, 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [%] | 100 [%] | [ 0 ] 0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold for a fast and positive increase in the absolute value of
the line voltage.  
When the threshold is exceeded, the pulses are inhibited and the PLL remains in closed-loop
control.  
The following applies when the "eLVRT" function is active (p3486.0 = 1):  
The threshold determines the response when the line voltage returns (r3487.10 = 1).  
When the threshold is exceeded, the pulses are briefly inhibited (r3487.11 = 1) and
overcurrent detection I_max (r3487.9 = 1) is inactive.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6319  
Refer to:
A06302, F06303

**Note:**
  
The setting is made as a percentage of the drive unit supply voltage (p0210).  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)  
Setting value 0.0 activates the evaluation.

### p0300[0...n] Motor type selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6310 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 5 | [ 0 ] 5 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Selects the motor type.

**Value:**
  
0:
No motor  
5:
Synchronous motor separately excited

**Notice:**
  
If a catalog motor is selected (p0300 >= 100) and an associated motor code number
(p0301), then the parameters that are associated with this list cannot be changed
(write protection). The write protection is canceled if the motor type p0300 is set
to a non-Siemens motor that matches p0301 (e.g. p0300 = 2 for p0301 = 2xxxx). Write
protection is automatically canceled when the results of motor data identification
are copied to the motor parameters.

**Note:**
  
If a motor type has not been selected (p0300 = 0), then the drive commissioning routine
cannot be exited.

must be taken into account.

### p0305[0...n] Rated motor current

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the rated motor current (rating plate).  
For several winding systems, the current of a branch should be entered.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0349

**Notice:**
  
When selecting a catalog motor (p0301), this parameter is automatically pre-assigned
and is write protected. Information in p0300 should be carefully observed when removing
write protection.  
If p0305 is changed during quick commissioning (p0010 = 1), then the maximum current
p0640 is pre-assigned accordingly. This is not the case when commissioning the motor
(p0010 = 3).

**Note:**
  
When the parameter value is entered the connection type of the motor (star-delta)
must be taken into account.

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the number of motor pole pairs. The value is used for internal calculations.  
r0313 = 1: 2-pole motor  
r0313 = 2: 4-pole motor, etc.

**Dependency:**
  
For p0314 > 0, the entered value is displayed in r0313.  
For p0314 = 0, the pole pair number (r0313) is automatically calculated from the rated
power (p0307), rated frequency (p0310) and rated speed (p0311).  
  
Refer to:
p0307, p0310, p0311, p0314

**Note:**
  
For the automatic calculation, the pole pair number is set to the value of 2 if the
rated speed or the rated frequency is zero.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 255 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the motor pole pair number.  
p0314 = 1: 2-pole motor  
p0314 = 2: 4-pole motor, etc.

**Dependency:**
  
For p0314 = 0, the pole pair number is automatically calculated from the rated frequency
(p0310) and the rated speed (p0311) and displayed in r0313.

Displays the rated motor voltage.

**Note:**
  
For induction motors (p0300 = 1xx) the parameter is set to p0304.  
For synchronous motors, parameter r0339 = p0304 is displayed. If p0304 = 0, then r0339
is calculated from p0305 and p0316.

### p0340[0...n] Automatic calculation motor/control parameters

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 5 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to automatically calculate motor parameters and closed-loop control parameters
from rating plate data.

**Value:**
  
0:
No calculation  
1:
Complete calculation  
2:
Calculation of equivalent circuit diagram parameters  
3:
Calculation of closed-loop control parameters  
4:
Calculation of controller parameters  
5:
Calculation of technological limits and threshold values

**Notice:**
  
After the value has been modified, no further parameter modifications can be made
and the status is shown in r3996. Modifications can be made again when r3996 = 0.  
The following parameters are influenced using p0340:  
The parameters designated with (*) are not overwritten for catalog motors (p0300 >
100).  
p0340 = 1:  
--> All of the parameters influenced for p0340 = 2, 3, 4, 5  
--> p0341 (*)  
--> p0342, p0344, p0600, p0640, p1082, p1349, p1441, p1442, p1576, p1577, p1609, p1619,
p1620, p1621, p1654, p1726, p1825, p1828 ... p1832, p1909, p1959, p2000, p2001, p2002,
p2003, p3927, p3928  
p0340 = 2:  
--> p0350 (*), p0354 ... p0361 (*), p0652 ... p0660  
--> p0625 (matching p0350)  
p0340 = 3:  
--> All of the parameters influenced for p0340 = 4, 5  
--> p0346, p0347, p0492, p0622, p1262, p1320 ... p1327, p1582, p1584, p1616, p1744,
p1755, p1756, p2178  
p0340 = 4:  
--> p1290, p1292, p1293, p1299, p1338, p1339, p1340, p1341, p1345, p1346, p1460, p1461,
p1462, p1463, p1464, p1465, p1470, p1472, p1590, p1592, p1600, p1628, p1629, p1630,
p1643, p1703, p1715, p1717, p1740, p1757, p1760, p1761, p1764, p1767, p1781, p1783,
p1785, p1786, p1795, p7036, p7037, p7038  
p0340 = 5:  
--> p0260 ... p0264, p1037, p1038, p1520, p1521, p1530, p1531, p1802, p1803, p2140,
p2142, p2148, p2150, p2161, p2162, p2163, p2164, p2175, p2177, p2194, p3207, p3208,
p3815, p3820 ... p3829

**Note:**
  
p0340 = 1 contains the calculations of p0340 = 2, 3, 4, 5.  
p0340 = 2 calculates the motor parameters (p0350 ... p0360).  
p0340 = 3 contains the calculations of p0340 = 4, 5.  
p0340 = 4 only calculates the controller parameters.  
p0340 = 5 only calculates the controller limits.  
When quick commissioning is exited using p3900 > 0, p0340 is automatically set to
1.  
At the end of the calculations, p0340 is automatically set to 0.  
If the STARTER commissioning software writes a 3 into p0340 when "downloading to target
device", then this corresponds to a "complete calculation of the motor/control parameters
without equivalent circuit diagram data". The same calculations are carried out as
for p0340 = 1, however, without the equivalent circuit diagram parameters of the motor
(p0340 = 2), the motor moment of inertia (p0341) and the motor mass (p0344).  
For third-party linear synchronous motors (p0300 = 4) equivalent circuit diagram data
are not calculated (p0340 = 2).

### p0341[0...n] Motor moment of inertia

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_ALL | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6020, 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**25_1 | **Unit selection:**p0100 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000000 [kgm²] | 1000000.000000 [kgm²] | [ 0 ] 0.000000 [kgm²] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the motor moment of inertia (without load).

**Dependency:**
  
IEC drives (p0100 = 0): unit kg m^2  
NEMA drives (p0100 = 1): unit lb ft^2  
The parameter value is included, together with p0342, in the rated starting time of
the motor.  
  
Refer to:
p0342, r0345

### p0342[0...n] Ratio between the total and motor moment of inertia

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6020, 6030, 6031 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1.000 | 10000.000 | [ 0 ] 1.000 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the ratio between the total moment of inertia (load + motor) and the intrinsic
motor moment of inertia (no load).

**Dependency:**
  
This means that together with p0341, the rated starting time of the motor is calculated.  
  
Refer to:
p0341, r0345, p1498

**Note:**
  
The product of p0341 * p0342 is used when the speed controller (p0340 = 4) is calculated
automatically.

### r0345[0...n] Nominal motor starting time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

2.6

Displays the rated motor starting time.  
This time corresponds to the time from standstill up to reaching the motor rated speed
and the acceleration with motor rated torque (r0333).

**Dependency:**

r0313, r0333, r0336, p0341, p0342

### p0346[0...n] Motor excitation build-up time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the excitation build-up time of the motor.  
The flux actual value must have reached the limit value in parameter p6281 within
this time period.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6281  
Refer to:
F49910, A49912

**Notice:**
  
During excitation build up, the no-load excitation current (p0389) and a supplementary
value (r1602) are output as excitation current setpoint.

**Note:**
  
The parameter is calculated using p0340 = 1, 3.  
The result is formed from the maximum of the excitation time constant (p0388, p0389,
p0390, p0304, p0305, p0360) and damping time constant (r0384).  
If this time is excessively reduced, this can result in an inadequate flux actual
value. This is the case if the current limit of the excitation equipment is reached
while excitation is being built up.

### p0347[0...n] Motor de-excitation time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 60.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting the demagnetization time.  
The motor is demagnetized when switching-off or if the operating enable is missing
(r6261.5).  
Switching-on the motor sequence control (r6262) is inhibited for this time.

**Note:**
  
The parameter is calculated using p0340 = 1, 3.  
The result is formed from the maximum of the excitation time constant (p0388, p0389,
p0390, p0304, p0305, p0360) and damping time constant (r0384).  
The orientation angle is incorrectly determined when re-exciting with inadequate demagnetization.

### p0349 System of units motor equivalent circuit diagram data

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the actual system of units for motor equivalent circuit diagram data.

**Value:**
  
1:
System of units, physical  
2:
System of units, referred

**Dependency:**
  
The parameter can only be changed in an offline project using the commissioning tool.  
  
Refer to:
p0304, p0305, p0310

**Note:**
  
The reference parameter for resistances of the rated motor impedance Z = p0304 / (1.732
* p0305) is in the % units system.  
Inductances are converted into a resistance using the factor 2 * Pi * p0310.  
If a reference parameter (p0304, p305, p0310) is zero, then it is not possible to
make a changeover to "referred" values (per unit values).

**Description:**
  
Resistance of the power cable between the Motor Module and motor.

### p0353[0...n] Motor series inductance

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**15_1 | **Unit selection:**p0349 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [mH] | 1000000.000 [mH] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [mH] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the series inductance.

**Note:**
  
For the automatic calculation with p0340 = 1 or 3, the calculation of p0348 is influenced
by p0353 if p0348 was 0.  
For the automatic calculation with p0340 = 1, 3 or 4, the calculation of p1715 is
influenced by p0353.  
The series inductance is reset when quick commissioning is exited with p3900 > 0.

### p0354[0...n] Motor rotor resistance cold / damping resistance d axis

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_EQU | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6727 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**16_1 | **Unit selection:**p0349 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**PMSM, REL, RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00000 [ohm] | 300.00000 [ohm] | [ 0 ] 0.00000 [ohm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the damping resistance in the rotor direction (d axis).

**Note:**
  
The current in the damping circuit decays with the damping time constant in the rotor
direction (d axis, p0384).  
In the case of a low damping time constant (T < 0.2 s), the value of the damping resistance
from the equivalent circuit diagram data should be entered reduced by the factor approx.
root (T).

### p0355[0...n] Motor damping resistance q axis

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_EQU | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6727 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**16_1 | **Unit selection:**p0349 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**ASM, PMSM, REL, RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00000 [ohm] | 300.00000 [ohm] | [ 0 ] 0.00000 [ohm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the damping resistance of the separately excited synchronous motor quadrature
to the rotor direction (q axis).

**Note:**
  
The current in the damping circuit decays with the damping time constant quadrature
to the rotor direction (q axis, p0385).  
In the case of a low damping time constant T (< 0.2 s), the value of the damping resistance
from the equivalent circuit diagram data should be entered reduced by the factor approx.
root (T).

### p0358[0...n] Motor rotor leakage inductance / damping inductance d axis

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_EQU | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6727, 6732 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**15_1 | **Unit selection:**p0349 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**PMSM, REL, RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the damping inductance in the rotor direction (d axis).

**Note:**
  
Take the excitation inductance into account here.  
The value from the parallel circuit of the damping inductance in the rotor direction
(d axis) and the value of the excitation inductance should be entered.
