### p2720[0...n] Load gear configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for position tracking of a load gear.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Load gear activate position tracking | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Axis type | Rotary axis | Linear axis | - |
| 02 | Load gear reset position | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For the following events, the non-volatile, saved position values are automatically
reset:  
- when an encoder replacement has been identified.  
- when changing the configuration of the Encoder Data Set (EDS).  
- when adjusting the absolute encoder again

### p2721[0...n] Load gear rotary absolute encoder revolutions virtual

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 4194303 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the number of rotations that can be resolved for a rotary absolute encoder with
activated position tracking of the load gear.

**Dependency:**
  
This parameter is only of significance for an absolute encoder (p0404.1 = 1) with
activated position tracking of the load gear (p2720.0 = 1).

**Note:**
  
The resolution that is set must be able to be represented using r2723.  
For rotary axes/modulo axes, the following applies:  
This parameter is pre-set with p0421 when activating position tracking and can be
changed.  
For linear axes, the following applies:  
This parameter is pre-assigned with p0421 when activating position tracking, expanded
by 6 bits for multiturn information (maximum number of overflows) and cannot be changed.

### p2722[0...n] Load gear position tracking tolerance window

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 | 4294967300.00 | [ 0 ] 0.00 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets a tolerance window for position tracking.  
After the system is switched on, the difference between the saved position and the
actual position is determined, and depending on this, the following is initiated:  
Difference within the tolerance window --> The position is reproduced as a result
of the encoder actual value.  
Difference outside the tolerance window --> An appropriate message is output.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F07449

**Caution:**
  
Rotation, for example through a complete encoder range is not detected.

**Note:**
  
The value is entered in integer (complete) encoder pulses.  
For p2720.0 = 1, the value is automatically pre-assigned quarter of the encoder range.  
Example:  
Quarter of the encoder range = (p0408 * p0421) / 4  
It is possible that the tolerance window may not be able to be precisely set due to
the data type (floating point number with 23 bit mantissa).

### r2723[0...n] CO: Load gear absolute value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 4010, 4704 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the absolute value after the load gear.

**Notice:**
  
The encoder position actual value must be requested using the encoder control word
Gn_STW.13.

**Note:**
  
The increments are displayed in the format the same as r0483.

### r2724[0...n] CO: Load gear position difference

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the position difference before the load gear between switching off and switching
on.

**Note:**
  
The increments are displayed in the same format as for r0483/r2723.  
If the measuring gear of the motor encoder is not activated, the position difference
should be read in encoder increments.  
If the measuring gear of the motor encoder is activated, the position difference is
converted using the measuring gear factor.

### p2810[0...7] BI: AND logic operation inputs

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for the inputs of the AND logic operations.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
AND logic operation 0 input A  
[
1]:
AND logic operation 0 input B  
[
2]:
AND logic operation 1 input A  
[
3]:
AND logic operation 1 input B  
[
4]:
AND logic operation 2 input A  
[
5]:
AND logic operation 2 input B  
[
6]:
AND logic operation 3 input A  
[
7]:
AND logic operation 3 input B

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r2811

**Note:**
  
[0]: AND logic operation 0, input A --> the result is displayed in r2811.0.  
[1]: AND logic operation 0, input B --> the result is displayed in r2811.0.  
...  
[6]: AND logic operation 3, input A --> the result is displayed in r2811.3.  
[7]: AND logic operation 3, input B --> the result is displayed in r2811.3.

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 02900 - 03839

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 02900 - 03839

### p2900[0...n] CO: Fixed value 1 [%]

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 1021 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -10000.00 [%] | 10000.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting and connector output for a fixed percentage value.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p2901, r2902, p2930

**Notice:**
  
A BICO interconnection to a parameter that belongs to a drive data set always acts
on the effective data set.

**Note:**
  
The value can be used to interconnect a scaling function (e.g. scaling the main setpoint).

### p2901[0...n] CO: Fixed value 2 [%]

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 1021 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -10000.00 [%] | 10000.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting and connector output for a fixed percentage value.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p2900, p2930

**Notice:**
  
A BICO interconnection to a parameter that belongs to a drive data set always acts
on the effective data set.

**Note:**
  
The value can be used to interconnect a scaling function (e.g. scaling of the supplementary
setpoint)

### p3111[0...n] BI: External fault 3 enable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the enable signal of external fault 3.  
External fault 3 is initiated by the following AND logic operation:  
- BI: p2108 negated  
- BI: p3111  
- BI: p3112 negated

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p2108, p3110, p3112  
Refer to:
F07862

### r3113.0...15 CO/BO: NAMUR message bit bar

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status of the NAMUR message bit bar.  
The faults and alarms are assigned to the appropriate signaling/message classes and
influence a specific message bit.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Fault converter information electronics/software error | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Network fault | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | DC link overvoltage | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Fault drive converter power electronics | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Drive converter overtemperature | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Ground fault | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Motor overload | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Bus error | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | External safety-relevant shutdown | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Mot encoder fault | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Error communication internal | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Fault infeed | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Other faults | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
Hardware or software malfunction was identified. Carry out a POWER ON of the component
involved. If it occurs again, contact Technical Support.  
For bit 01:  
A line supply fault has occurred (phase failure, voltage level, ...). Check the line
supply / fuses. Check the supply voltage. Check the wiring.  
For bit 02:  
The DC link voltage has assumed an inadmissibly high value. Check the dimensioning
of the system (line supply, reactor, voltages). Check the infeed settings.  
For bit 03:  
An inadmissible operating state of the power electronics was identified (overcurrent,
overtemperature, IGBT failure, ...). Check that the permissible load cycles are maintained.
Check the ambient temperatures (fan).  
For bit 04:  
The temperature in the component has exceeded the highest permissible limit. Check
the ambient temperature / control cabinet cooling.  
For bit 05:  
A ground fault / inter-phase short-circuit was detected in the power cables or in
the motor windings. Check the power cables (connection). Check the motor.  
For bit 06:  
The motor was operated outside the permissible limits (temperature, current, torque,
...). Check the load cycles and limits that have been set. Check the ambient temperature
/ motor cooling.  
For bit 07:  
The communication to the higher-level control system (internal coupling, PROFIBUS,
PROFINET, ...) is either faulted or interrupted. Check the state of the higher-level
control system. Check the communication connection/wiring. Check the bus configuration
/ clock cycles.  
For bit 08:  
A safety operation monitoring function (Safety) has detected an error.  
For bit 09:  
When evaluating the encoder signals (track signals, zero marks, absolute values, ...)
an illegal signal state was detected. Check the encoder / state of the encoder signals.
Observe the maximum frequencies.  
For bit 10:  
The internal communication between the SINAMICS components is faulted or interrupted.
Check the DRIVE-CLiQ wiring. Ensure an EMC-compliant design. Observe the maximum permissible
quantity structure / clock cycles.  
For bit 11:  
The infeed is faulted or has failed. Check the infeed and the surroundings (line supply,
filter, reactors, fuses, ...). Check the closed-loop infeed control.  
For bit 15:  
Group fault. Determine the precise cause of the fault using the commissioning tool.

### r3115[0...63] Fault drive object initiating

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8050, 8060 |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the drive object number of the initiating drive object for this fault as
integer number.  
Value = 63:  
The fault was initiated by the drive object itself.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0945, r0947, r0948, r0949, r2109, r2130, r2133, r2136, r3120, r3122

**Notice:**
  
The values of this parameter are only saved in a volatile fashion and are lost when
switching off or for a warm restart.

**Note:**
  
The buffer parameters are cyclically updated in the background (refer to status signal
in r2139).  
The structure of the fault buffer and the assignment of the indices is shown in r0945.

### p3118[0...1] Issue messages

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 65535 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting to issue messages.  
The message is issued after writing a valid message number to index 0.  
All of the messages issued using this mechanism are reset after writing the value
0 to index 0.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Message number  
[
1]:
Message value

**Notice:**
  
This parameter may only be used by qualified service personnel.

**Note:**
  
Example:  
Alarm A07850 should be output with alarm value 5.  
p3118[1] = 5  
p3118[0] = 7850  
Additional messages can be issued.

### r3122[0...63] Diagnostic attribute fault

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8060 |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the diagnostic attribute of the fault which has occurred.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Hardware replacement recommended | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Message has gone | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 0 | Low | High | - |
| 17 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 1 | Low | High | - |
| 18 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 2 | Low | High | - |
| 19 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 3 | Low | High | - |
| 20 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 4 | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0945, r0947, r0948, r0949, r2109, r2130, r2133, r2136, r3120

**Note:**
  
The buffer parameters are cyclically updated in the background (refer to status signal
in r2139).  
The structure of the fault buffer and the assignment of the indices is shown in r0945.  
For bits 20 ... 16:  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 0: not assigned  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 0, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 1: hardware fault/software
error  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 0, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 2: line fault  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 0, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 3: supply voltage
fault  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 1, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 4: DC link fault  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 1, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 5: power electronics
faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 1, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 6: overtemperature
electronic components  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 1, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 7: ground fault/phase
fault detected  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 0, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 8: motor overload  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 0, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 9: communication
error to the higher-level control  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 0, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 10: safe monitoring
channel has identified an error  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 0, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 11: incorrect
position actual value/speed actual value or not available  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 12: internal
(DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 13: infeed unit
faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 14: braking controller/Braking
Module faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 15: line filter
faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 1, 0, 0, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 16: external
measured value/signal state outside the permissible range  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 1, 0, 0, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 17: application/technology
function faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 1, 0, 0, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 18: error in
the parameterization/configuration/commissioning sequence  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 1, 0, 0, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 19: general drive
fault  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 20: auxiliary
unit faulted

### r3123[0...63] Diagnostic attribute alarm

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8065 |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the diagnostic attribute of the alarm which has occurred.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Hardware replacement recommended | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Alarm class bit 0 | Low | High | - |
| 12 | Alarm class bit 1 | Low | High | - |
| 13 | Maintenance required | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Maintenance urgently required | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Message has gone | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 0 | Low | High | - |
| 17 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 1 | Low | High | - |
| 18 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 2 | Low | High | - |
| 19 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 3 | Low | High | - |
| 20 | PROFIdrive fault class bit 4 | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r2110, r2122, r2123, r2124, r2125, r2134, r2145, r2146, r3121

**Note:**
  
The buffer parameters are cyclically updated in the background (refer to status signal
in r2139).  
The structure of the alarm buffer and the assignment of the indices is shown in r2122.  
For bit 12, 11:  
These status bits are used for the classification of internal alarm classes and are
intended for diagnostic purposes only on certain automation systems with integrated
SINAMICS functionality.  
For bits 20 ... 16:  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 0: not assigned  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 0, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 1: hardware fault/software
error  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 0, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 2: line fault  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 0, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 3: supply voltage
fault  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 1, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 4: DC link fault  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 1, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 5: power electronics
faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 1, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 6: overtemperature
electronic components  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 0, 1, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 7: ground fault/phase
fault detected  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 0, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 8: motor overload  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 0, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 9: communication
error to the higher-level control  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 0, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 10: safe monitoring
channel has identified an error  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 0, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 11: incorrect
position actual value/speed actual value or not available  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 12: internal
(DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 13: infeed unit
faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 14: braking controller/Braking
Module faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 15: line filter
faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 1, 0, 0, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 16: external
measured value/signal state outside the permissible range  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 1, 0, 0, 0, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 17: application/technology
function faulted  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 1, 0, 0, 1, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 18: error in
the parameterization/configuration/commissioning sequence  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 1, 0, 0, 1, 1 --> PROFIdrive message class 19: general drive
fault  
Bits 20, 19, 18, 17, 16 = 0, 1, 1, 0, 0 --> PROFIdrive message class 20: auxiliary
unit faulted

4.4

Displays the component number of the oldest fault that is still active.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r2131, r3131

### p3135 Suppress active fault

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8060 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the suppression of r2139.3 "Fault present" for certain fault responses.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 08 | Suppression of fault response ENCODER | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Suppression of fault response NONE | OFF | ON | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0491, r2139

**Note:**
  
Depending on the suppression of a fault reaction in this parameter, r2139.1 "Acknowledgment
required" is set when at least one fault occurs.  
For bit 08:  
The suppression is only effective if p0491 = 1.

### p3201[0...n] Excitation current outside the tolerance threshold value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**ASM, PMSM, REL, RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 10.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold value for the "excitation current outside tolerance" message for
the excitation current monitoring.  
If the absolute value of the difference between the excitation current setpoint and
actual value (r1641 - r1626) exceeds the threshold value and the hysteresis is longer
than the selected delay time, then fault F07913 is output.  
This fault is withdrawn when the threshold voltage is undershot.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1626, r1641, p3202, p3203  
Refer to:
F07913

**Note:**
  
The monitoring function is only carried out for separately excited synchronous motors
(p0300 = 5).

### p3204[0...n] Flux outside the tolerance threshold value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**ASM, PMSM, REL, RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 10.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold value for the "flux outside the tolerance" message for the flux
monitoring.  
If the absolute value of the difference between the flux setpoint and actual value
(r0083 - r0084) falls below the threshold value with hysteresis longer than the selected
delay time, then fault F07914 is output.  
This fault is withdrawn when the threshold voltage is undershot.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0083, r0084, p3205, p3206  
Refer to:
F07914

**Note:**
  
The monitoring function is only carried out for separately excited synchronous motors
(p0300 = 5).  
The flux monitoring is only active after magnetizing (r0056.4 = 1).

### p3207[0...n] Zero current signal threshold value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_LIM_REF | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**6_4 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**ASM, PMSM, REL, RESM | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.01 [A] | 10000.00 [A] | [ 0 ] 1.00 [A] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold value for the zero current signal for the zero current monitoring.  
If the absolute current falls below the threshold value then r2199.6 is set to 1 after
the delay time has expired. The bit is reset if the threshold value and the hysteresis
are exceeded again.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r2199, p3208, p3209

**Note:**
  
The monitoring function is only carried out for separately excited synchronous motors
(p0300 = 5).  
The monitoring is only carried out for speeds less than the speed threshold value
in p2161 (r2199.0 = 1).

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

Display and connector output for the state of the sequence control.

**Value:**
  
0:
Not active  
5:
Normal operation  
10:
Switching on inhibited  
20:
Ready for switching on  
21:
Check the power-on conditions  
29:
Prepare for operation  
30:
Auxiliaries on  
31:
Output disconnector close  
32:
Motor circuit breaker close  
34:
Input transformer premagnetization on  
35:
Inrush switch close  
36:
Line Side Switch close  
38:
Wait for feedback signals  
39:
Ready  
40:
Excitation circuit breaker/contactor close  
41:
Ready with build-up excitation  
42:
Close inhibit MSS  
43:
Ready for operation without MSS  
44:
Determination offset angle line-side converter  
45:
Operation  
46:
Internal demagnetization  
48:
Stop  
49:
Quick stop  
50:
Circuit breaker open  
100:
Test mode  
101:
Test mode auxiliaries  
102:
Test mode output disconnector  
103:
Test mode excitation circuit breaker/contactor  
104:
Test mode output transformer demagnetization  
110:
Test mode circuit breaker input side  
120:
Test mode circuit breaker output side  
130:
Test mode inrush circuit breaker  
140:
Test mode input transformer premagnetization

### r3451[0...n] CO: Line PLL status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the status word of the line PLL.  
For bit 17: display if the threshold values in p283 index 0 or in index 1 were fallen
below.  
For bit 18: display if the threshold values in p282 index 0 or in index 1 were fallen
below or in index 3 exceeded.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Line supply OK | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | PLL not in synchronism | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Correction after angular step | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Initialization after free wheel | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Line angle step | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Line angle smooth step | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Line supply ccw | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | PLL not synchronous within the monitoring time | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | PLL does not stabilize | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Error occurred when synchronizing | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | PU thyristor monitoring active | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Phase integrator angle stationary | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | PSA values are incorrect | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Voltage OK | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | Undervoltage threshold lower | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Undervoltage threshold upper | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | Voltage step | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | Undervoltage outside the monitoring time | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | VSM wait for valid values | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | VSM values are incorrect | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | Wait for LSS to close | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Lower undervoltage threshold without delay | No | Yes | - |
| 25 | Upper undervoltage threshold without delay | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | Alarm suppression | No | Yes | - |
| 27 | Voltage step outside monitoring time | No | Yes | - |
| 28 | Measured values from PSA/VSM 0 | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | Measured values from PSA/VSM 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | Voltage step upward without delay | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | Voltage step downward without delay | No | Yes | - |

### p3458 Line supply PLL line angle smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1.0 [ms] | 1000.0 [ms] | [ 0 ] 50.0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time of the line angle of the line supply PLL.

**Note:**
  
The smoothing time is preset for operation on the line supply with local power generation.
With this setting, low-frequency excitation is significantly dampened.  
  
At 10Hz, the control loop of the line PLL has gain (V):  
  
p3458 = 50ms | 40ms | 30ms | 20ms | 10ms  
V10Hz = 0.05 | 0.08 | 0.14 | 0.30 | 1.2  
  
The control loop has a maximum gain of V= 1.67 at the following frequencies:  
  
p3458= 50ms | 40ms | 30ms | 20ms | 10ms  
fVmax =1.4 Hz| 1.6Hz | 2.1Hz | 3.2Hz | 7Hz  
  
However, a high setting value also reduces the dynamic performance when tracking the
line frequency.  
If necessary, this setting value should be reduced for island grids, where the frequency
can quickly change (e.g. caused by loads being shed).

### p3463[0...1] Line supply PLL line angle change phase failure detection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [°] | 60.0 [°] | [ 0 ] 10.0 [°]  [ 1 ] 4.0 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible line angle change (system deviation) to identify a phase failure.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Threshold inhibit pulses  
[
1]:
Threshold enable pulses

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3464  
Refer to:
A06205

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
If the line supply angle changes by more than this value, then a phase failure is
assumed. The pulses are then inhibited.  
For index [1]:  
In order to re-enable the pulses after an illegal change of the line angle, the line
angle change for the time (p3464) must be less than this value.

the line angle change for this time must be less than the set threshold value (p3463[1]).

### p3480[0...1] Modulation depth reserve firing angle limit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6715 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [°] | 60.0 [°] | [ 0 ] 10.0 [°]  [ 1 ] 0.0 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the firing angle from which the modulation depth reserve controller intervenes.  
Motor mode:  
If the line-side firing angle reaches this limit, the firing angle of the motor-side
converter (MSC) is reduced. This means that the modulation depth reserve is maintained.  
Regenerative mode:  
If the line-side firing angle undershoots this clearance for inverter limiting (p6335),
the firing angle of the motor-side converter (MSC) is increased.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Motor mode  
[
1]:
Regenerative mode

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6325, p6332, r6335  
Refer to:
A06800

**Note:**
  
For motoring operation:  
The effective modulation depth reserve is obtained from the difference between the
limit of the firing angle of the modulation depth reserve (p3480) and the rectifier
limiting of the LSC (p6332).

### p3484[0...n] eLVRT DC link current limit line asymmetry

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6710 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the factor to reduce the current limit during an asymmetrical line voltage dip
for the "eLVRT" function.  
The limit becomes active when a line undervoltage condition is identified (p0282).  
The factor is referred to the value of the current limit in p0640.  
For asymmetrical line voltage dips (e.g. 2-phase line dips), negative phase-sequence
systems occur, which generate current harmonics with twice the line frequency.  
With increasing negative phase-sequence system, the current limit is reduced depending
on the set factor (100 % - p3484).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0640, p3490, r6315

**Note:**
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)  
Example:  
In the event of a 2-phase short-circuit, the current limit is reduced as follows:  
(100 % - p3484) * p0640

### p3486 eLVRT configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6745 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0110 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the "eLVRT" function.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Activate eLVRT | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Deactivate eLVRT I_max | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Activate eLVRT line-side DC link voltage reduction | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0282, p0286, p3488, p6338

**Note:**
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)  
For bit 0 = 1:  
When activated, parameter p3458 must be set >= 50 ms.  
The interruption-free voltage dip is defined using the following parameter:  
Thresholds: p0282, p0283, p6338[0] Duration: p3488, p3499, p3494  
For asymmetrical line voltage dips, while eLVRT is active, the DC link current should
be limited by p3484.  
For bit 1 = 1:  
Overcurrent detection is deactivated when the voltage returns (p0286).  
For bit 2 = 1:  
When the voltage returns, the line-side DC link voltage is reduced (p0286).

### r3487.0...21 CO/BO: eLVRT status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6742, 6744, 6745, 6746 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the "eLVRT" function.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | eLVRT activated | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | eLVRT I_max deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | eLVRT line-side DC link voltage reduction activated | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | eLVRT ready | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | eLVRT active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | eLVRT completed | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | eLVRT MSC modulation depth intervention active | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | eLVRT set ramp function generator | OFF | ON | - |
| 08 | eLVRT set current controller | OFF | ON | - |
| 09 | eLVRT I_max inactive | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | eLVRT line voltage return | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | eLVRT LSC DC link voltage is reduced | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | eLVRT abort | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | eLVRT MSC modulation depth intervention hysteresis active | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | eLVRT upper line undervoltage threshold fallen below active | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | eLVRT in the operating range | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)

### p3488 eLVRT duration permissible

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6746 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 10000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 200 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible duration for a voltage dip for the "eLVRT" function.  
The duration starts with the identification of a line undervoltage condition (p0282).
After the permissible duration has elapsed, fault/alarm F(A)06108 is output.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0282, p3499, p6338  
Refer to:
F06108

**Note:**
  
When BICO input p3499 is activated, the permissible duration is the minimum value
from the value in parameter p3488 and the value read out at BICO input p3499.  
When the BICO input is deactivated, only the duration set in parameter p3488 is effective.  
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)

### p3489[0...2] eLVRT scaling and thresholds

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6742, 6746 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [%] | 200 [%] | [ 0 ] 100 [%]  [ 1 ] 100 [%]  [ 2 ] 70 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the scalings and thresholds for the "eLVRT" function.  
For index [0, 1]:  
Using these scaling factors, the current controllers are adapted to the motor-side
modulation depth intervention.  
For index [2]:  
The "eLVRT" function becomes active when this motor frequency is exceeded (r3487.4
= 1). The value is referred to the rated motor frequency.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Current controller scaling, system 1  
[
1]:
Current controller scaling, system 2  
[
2]:
eLVRT active motor frequency threshold

**Note:**
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)

### p3490[0...n] eLVRT DC link current limit line voltage dip

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6710 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the factor to reduce the current limit during a line voltage dip for the "eLVRT"
function.  
The limit becomes active when a line undervoltage condition is identified (r6313 <
95 %).  
The factor is referred to the value of the current limit in p0640.  
The current goes through a settling phase when line voltage dips occur. When the line
voltage decreases, the current limit is temporarily reduced depending on the set factor
((100 % - p3490 * r6313) * p0640).  
The current is reduced down to 80% as a maximum

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0640, p3484, r6315

**Note:**
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)

### p3492 Line PLL line supply undervoltage shutdown delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 50 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for shutdown due to a line supply undervoltage condition (A06105).  
After the line supply undervoltage is detected, the power unit is tripped (shut down)
after this delay time has expired.  
If, during this delay time, the line supply undervoltage is no longer detected, then
the power unit is not shut down.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0282, p0288  
Refer to:
A06105

**Note:**
  
The degree of ruggedness of the infeed with respect to fluctuations in the line supply
voltage can be increased by parameterizing this delay value.  
However, the following should be noted:  
- the infeed power decreases proportionally (linearly) with the line supply voltage.  
- when other components are connected, for low line supply voltage, operating faults
or damage can occur. In this case, the specifications of the connected electrical
components should always be carefully observed.

### p3493[0...1] Line supply PLL line supply change switch-in delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 5000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 40 [ms]  [ 1 ] 40 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for switching on after a line supply undervoltage condition (A06105)
or after a voltage step (A06302).  
After the cause of the fault has been removed, the power unit is only switched on
after this delay has expired.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switching on after line supply undervoltage delay time  
[
1]:
Switching on after a voltage step delay time

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A06105, A06302

### p3494[0...1] Line supply PLL line supply change OFF2 delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 30000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 2000 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for OFF2 for a line supply undervoltage condition (F06100) or
after a voltage step (F06303).  
p3494[0]:  
The time is started when a line supply undervoltage condition is detected (A06105).
Fault F06100 is output after the time expires (OFF2).  
p3494[1]:  
The time is started when a line supply step is detected (A06302). Fault F06303 is
output after the time expires (OFF2).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
OFF2 after a line supply undervoltage delay time  
[
1]:
OFF2 after a voltage step delay time

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0282, p0283  
Refer to:
F06100

**Note:**
  
For p3494[0]:  
After the line voltage returns, synchronization of the PLL has been completed after
the timer expires, otherwise fault F06500 is output (OFF2).  
During synchronization, for system deviations (p3460) up to 60°, the PLL remains in
the closed-loop control mode, otherwise line analysis is started.

### p3495 Line PLL line supply failure simulation duration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 5000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the time for which a line failure is simulated.

**Note:**
  
Properties of the line failure:  
1 ms - 999 ms: pulses are inhibited, PLL remains in closed-loop control.  
1000 ms - 5000 ms: pulses are inhibited, PLL is frozen.

### p3499 CI: eLVRT permissible duration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6746 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the permissible duration of eLVRT.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3488  
Refer to:
F06108

**Note:**
  
The signal that is read out and interconnected with BICO input p3499 is interpreted
as a value in milliseconds, which means for example that a value of 1000 corresponds
to 1000ms.  
  
The input is deactivated if '0' is interconnected with BICO input p3499.  
In this case, only the permissible duration in parameter p3488 is effective.  
When the input is activated, the permissible duration is the minimum value from parameter
p3488 and the value read out at the connector input.  
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)

### r3661[0...n] CO: VSM input line supply voltage u1 - u2

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**p0150 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**5_3 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [V] | - [V] | [ ] - [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the input voltage between phases L1 and L2 for the Voltage Sensing Module
(VSM).

**Note:**
  
X521.1 or X522.1: Connection of L1  
X521.2 or X522.2: Connection of L2  
X521.3 or X522.3: Connection of L3

### p3663 VSM measuring voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9880 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1.00 [Vrms] | 690.00 [Vrms] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [Vrms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the supply voltage on the Voltage Sensing Module (VSM).

**Note:**
  
X522.1: Connection of L1  
X522.2: Connection of L2  
X522.3: Connection of L3  
The synchronizing voltage for a medium-voltage drive is stepped-down using an instrument
transformer. Generally, this does not mean that it has to have a secondary voltage
of 100 V (phase-phase, rms).

### p3782 BI: Parallel switching unit, raise frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9816 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the command "raise frequency" from the parallel switching
unit.  
When BI: p3781 = 1 signal (operation) the commands "raise frequency" or "lower frequency"
are evaluated as follows:  
BI: p3782 = 0, BI: p3783 = 0 --> frequency remains unchanged  
BI: p3782 = 1, BI: p3783 = 0 --> frequency is raised  
BI: p3782 = 0, BI: p3783 = 1 --> frequency is lowered  
BI: p3782 = 1, BI: p3783 = 1 --> frequency remains unchanged

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3783

### p3786 BI: Parallel switching unit synchronism reached

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9815 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the signal "synchronism reached" from the parallel switching
unit.  
BI: p3786 = 1 signal  
The parallel switching unit signals "synchronism reached". The drive converter is
switched off after the delay time set in p3793.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3793

### p3787 BI: Parallel switching unit inhibit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9815 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the signal "inhibit" from the parallel switching unit.  
BI: p3787 = 1 signal:  
The external synchronizing unit is inhibited.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07946, F07948

**Note:**
  
Inhibit in the basic state (BI: p3781 = 0 signal):  
Alarm A07946 is output.  
Inhibit outside the basic state (BI: p3781 = 1 signal):  
Fault F07948 is output with fault value = 4 so that synchronizing is interrupted and
the parallel switching unit is shed.

### p3788[0...n] Parallel switching unit starting frequency bandwidth

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 9816 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [Hz] | 20.00 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 0.50 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the starting frequency bandwidth.  
If the actual frequency lies within the starting frequency bandwidth around the main
setpoint present (electrical rotating field) the parallel switching unit is started
or released.

### p3789[0...n] Parallel switching unit frequency adjustment time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 9816 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [s] | 480.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 80.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the adjustment time for the commands "raise frequency" and "lower frequency"
from the parallel switching unit.  
This time determines, as a function of the rated motor frequency (p0310) the gradient
(rate of rise) of the frequency ramp-function generator.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0310

| --- | --- | --- |

| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9815 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the signal "enable" of the parallel switching unit.  
BI: p3802 = 1 signal:  
The parallel switching unit is enabled.

### r3803.0...8 CO/BO: Parallel switching unit status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status word of the parallel switching unit.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Parallel switching unit is selected | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Parallel switching unit issues synchronizing command | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Parallel switching unit, operation | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Parallel switching unit fault | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Parallel switching unit, raise frequency | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Parallel switching unit, lower frequency | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Parallel switching unit, raise voltage | No | Yes | 9816 |
| 07 | Parallel switching unit, lower voltage | No | Yes | 9816 |
| 08 | Parallel switching unit inhibited | No | Yes | - |

### r3819.0...3 CO/BO: Parallel switching unit control word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the control word for the parallel switching unit.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Parallel switching unit enable | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Parallel switching unit start | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Parallel switching unit shed | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Parallel switching unit fault | No | Yes | - |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the friction characteristic.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Friction characteristic OK | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Record friction characteristic activated | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Record friction characteristic completed | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Record friction characteristic canceled | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Friction characteristic positive direction | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
Bit 0 = 1:  
All friction characteristic values (p3820 ... p3839) of all existing drive data sets
are plausible.

### p3842 Friction characteristic activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate and deactivate the friction characteristic.

**Value:**
  
0:
Friction characteristic deactivated  
1:
Friction characteristic activated

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1569, r3841, p3845

**Notice:**
  
In order that the friction characteristic can be activated, all friction characteristic
values (p3820 ... p3839) of all existing drive data sets must be plausible.  
For speed values the following applies:  
0.0 < p3820 < p3821 < ... < p3829 <= p0322 or p1082, if p0322 = 0  
For torque values, the following applies:  
0 <= p3830, p3831 ... p3839 <= r0333

### p3845 Record friction characteristic activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting for the friction characteristic record.  
After the next switch-on command, the friction characteristic is automatically recorded.

**Value:**
  
0:
Record friction characteristic deactivated  
1:
Record friction characteristic activated all directions  
2:
Record friction characteristic activated positive direction  
3:
Record friction characteristic activated negative direction

**Dependency:**
  
When selecting the friction characteristic measurement, the drive data set changeover
is suppressed.  
For linear drives (refer to r0108.12) it is not permissible to carry out the friction
characteristic measurement for mechanical systems that limit travel.

**Danger:**
  
For drives with a mechanical system that limit the distance moved, it must be ensured
that during recording, the friction characteristic is not reached. If this is not
the case, then it is not permissible that the measurement is carried out.

**Notice:**
  
To permanently accept the determined settings they must be saved in a non-volatile
fashion (p0971, p0977).

**Note:**
  
When the friction characteristic record is active, it is not possible to save the
parameters (p0971, p0977).  
When the friction characteristic record is active (p3845 > 0), it is not possible
to change p3820 ... p3829, p3830 ... p3839 and p3842.  
When recording the friction characteristic, in addition to the friction, the motor
losses are also determined (e.g. iron losses, eddy current losses and re-magnetizing
losses). A differentiation is not made between these individual loss components. We
recommend that a motor temperature sensor is used because torque deviations can also
be emulated/mapped on the characteristic due to the thermal influence.

### p3846[0...n] Record friction characteristic ramp-up/ramp-down time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 999999.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 10.000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the ramp-up/ramp-down time of the ramp-up/ramp-down function generator to automatically
Documenting the friction characteristic.  
The drive is accelerated from standstill (setpoint = 0) up to the maximum speed/velocity
(p1082) in this time.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3845

### p3847[0...n] Record friction characteristic time to warm up

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Functions | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 3600.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the warm-up time.  
To start the automatic recording, the highest selected speed (p3829) is approached
and this time is held. After this, the measurement is started with the highest speed.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3829, p3845

### p3848[0...n] CI: Friction characteristic speed actual value signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 7010 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the speed actual value of the friction characteristic.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r1443

**Notice:**
  
The interconnected signal of the speed actual value must be approximately the same
as the real motor speed so that when the motor model is replaced, no significant settling
operations occur.

### p3900 Completion of quick commissioning

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Exits quick commissioning (p0010 = 1) with automatic calculation of all parameters
of all existing drive data sets that depend on the entries made during quick commissioning.  
p3900 = 1 initially includes a parameter reset (factory setting, the same as p0970
= 1) for all parameters of the drive object; however, without overwriting the entries
made during the quick commissioning.  
The interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram selection (p0922) and the interconnections
via p0700, p1000 and p1500 are re-established and all of the dependent motor, open-loop
and control-loop control parameters are calculated (corresponding to p0340 = 1).  
p3900 = 2 includes the restoration of the interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram
selection (p0922) and the interconnections via p0700, p1000 and p1500 and the calculations
corresponding to p0340 = 1.  
p3900 = 3 only includes the calculations associated with the motor, open-loop and
closed-loop control parameters corresponding to p0340 = 1.

**Value:**
  
0:
No quick parameterization  
1:
Quick parameterization after parameter reset  
2:
Quick parameterization (only) for BICO and motor parameters  
3:
Quick parameterization for motor parameters (only)

**Notice:**
  
After the value has been modified, no further parameter modifications can be made
and the status is shown in r3996. Modifications can be made again when r3996 = 0.

**Note:**
  
When the calculations have been completed, p3900 and p0010 are automatically reset
to a value of zero.  
When calculating motor, open-loop and closed-loop control parameters (such as for
p0340 = 1) parameters associated with a selected Siemens catalog motor are not overwritten.

**Value:**

1:

When changing the master control in operation, the drive can manifest undesirable
behavior - e.g. it can accelerate up to another setpoint.

### p3990[0...n] Sequentially resets drive parameters

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 300 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Parameter to internally calculate subsequent (follow-on) parameters after a factory
setting was carried out.  
The parameter is then reset back to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Start a parameter reset  
3:
Start download of volatile parameters from RAM  
5:
Starts a safety parameter reset  
6:
Start reset non-safety/safety parameters  
10:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 10  
11:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 11  
12:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 12  
30:
Start loading delivery condition saved with p0971 = 30  
100:
Start a BICO interconnection reset  
300:
Only Siemens internal

**Dependency:**
  
This parameter is called when first commissioning the system and also for factory
settings if this has been initiated via p0970 or p3900.  
  
Refer to:
p0970, p3900

### r3996[0...1] Parameter write inhibit status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Displays whether writing to parameters is inhibited.  
r3996[0] = 0:  
Parameter write not inhibited.  
0 < r3996[0] < 100:  
Parameter write inhibited. The value shows how the calculations are progressing.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Progress calculations  
[
1]:
Cause

**Note:**
  
For index [1]:  
Only for internal Siemens troubleshooting.

### r4021[0...n] CO: PSA digital inputs terminal actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Displays the actual value of the digital inputs/outputs of the Power Stack Adapter
(PSA).  
This means that the actual input signal can be checked at terminal DI x prior to switching
from the simulation mode (p4095.x = 1) to the terminal mode (p4095.x = 0).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DI 0 (X322.8) fan feedback signal or leakage sensing | Low | High | - |
| 01 | DI 1 (X322.5) grounding switch open | Low | High | - |
| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) disconnector closed | Low | High | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) door contact circuit | Low | High | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) grounding switch closed | Low | High | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) disconnector open | Low | High | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) group alarm | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) group fault | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) | Low | High | - |

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4028[0...n] PSA set digital input/output

|  |  |  |

| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
