5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the bidirectional digital inputs/outputs on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) | Input | Output | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) | Input | Output | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) | Input | Output | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Input | Output | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Input | Output | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Input | Output | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Input | Output | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) | Input | Output | - |

**Note:**
  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output

### r4047[0...n] PSA digital outputs status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the digital outputs of Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 08 | DO 0 (X331.15) open line-side circuit breaker | Low | High | - |
| 09 | DO 1 (X331.14) open motor cct breaker or Na/M firing command1 | Low | High | - |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) close enable signal for circuit breaker | Low | High | - |
| 11 | DO 3 (X331.10) control door release | Low | High | - |
| 12 | DO 4 (X331.7) switching enable for disconnector | Low | High | - |
| 13 | DO 5 (X331.6) close disconnector or Na/M firing command1 | Low | High | - |
| 14 | DO 6 (X331.3) open disconnector or Nb firing command 1 | Low | High | - |
| 15 | DO 7 (X331.2) control fan | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4028, p4048

**Note:**
  
Inversion using p4048 has been taken into account.  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
The significance of the outputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The outputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4048[0...n] PSA invert digital outputs

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the signals at the digital outputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The inversion is only effective for the freely interconnectable digital outputs.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |

**Note:**
  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
The inversion is only effective for the freely interconnectable digital outputs.  
When the signal is configured as input, then the associated bit in p4048 has no function.

DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4630[0...n] Absolute encoder linear measuring step factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 | 4294967295 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the resolution of the absolute position for a linear absolute encoder as factor
from p0407.

**Note:**
  
The serial protocol of an absolute encoder provides the position with a certain resolution
(e.g. 100 nm).  
The resolution is calculated from p0407/p4630.

| 0 | 65535 | [ 0 ] 0 |

4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the trace functionality for OEM encoder manufacturers.

### p4642 Encoder fault test function

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 255 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Test function to initiate an encoder fault  
For p4642 = 1:  
When setting, an encoder fault is output once at the currently used motor encoder.
Depending on the configuration in p0491, the drive switches over to encoderless operation
This means that encoderless motor stopping can be tested.  
For p4642 = 2:  
When setting, an encoder fault is output once at the currently used motor encoder.
For braking, the drive still uses the commutation position of the encoder. This means
that a faster motor stopping with encoder can be tested (only functions with SMx modules,
otherwise the same response as for p4642 = 1).  
The following generally applies:  
After acknowledgment, the encoder fault that was issued is cleared and p4642 is set
= 0.

**Notice:**
  
It is not permissible that a data set is switched over between an encoder fault being
issued and its deletion.

### r4648.0 CO/BO: Encoder status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the encoder status word.  
For bit 00:  
The bit is a group message for all encoders of the axis, and is set for the following
alarms:  
A3x407: "Function limit reached"  
A3x415: "Signal level track A or B out of tolerance (alarm)"  
A3x419: "Track A or B out of tolerance"  
A3x442: "Battery voltage alarm threshold reached"  
x = encoder 1, 2, 3

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Encoder maintenance required | No | Yes | - |

**Notice:**
  
Maintenance work may have to be carried out on the encoder.

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
For test purposes, the bit can be set using p4642 = 3.  
The bit is reset using p4642 = 0.

### p4649[0...n] Encoder function reserve amplitude limit incremental signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 500 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Amplitude threshold of the incremental signals for the function reserve.  
If the set amplitude threshold on the incremental signals is fallen below, then alarm
A3x407 "Encoder x; function limit reached " is output.

**Note:**
  
An amplitude threshold of 230 mV can be used as default value.

### p4652[0...2] XIST1_ERW reset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 4750 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to reset the actual value in XIST_ERW (CO: r4653).

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Reset with zero mark  
2:
Reset with BICO  
3:
Reset with selected zero mark

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Encoder 1  
[
1]:
Encoder 2  
[
2]:
Encoder 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4653, r4654, p4655

**Note:**
  
The absolute value is only valid after passing the zero mark.  
For value = 1:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset when passing every zero mark.  
For value = 2:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset with a 0/1 edge via binector input p4655.  
If value = 3:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset after a 0/1 edge via binector input p4655 when passing
the next zero mark.

### p4660[0...2] Sensor Module filter bandwidth

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [kHz] | 20000.00 [kHz] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [kHz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the filter bandwidth for Sensor Module SMx10 (resolver) and SMx20 (sin/cos).  
The value set on the Sensor Module is displayed in r4661.  
Currently, the Sensor Module hardware only supports the following values:  
- 0: The Sensor Module's default setting is used.  
- 50 kHz  
- 170 kHz  
- 500 kHz  
- Unlimited: Only the bandwidth of the operational amplifier is effective.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Encoder 1  
[
1]:
Encoder 2  
[
2]:
Encoder 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4661

**Note:**
  
A value of zero is displayed if an encoder is not present.

### p4662[0...n] Encoder characteristic type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the characteristic type.  
For non-linear sensors, the interrelationship between the signal voltage and the position
can be defined using a third degree polynomial.

**Value:**
  
0:
Characteristic inactive  
1:
Characteristic polynomial third degree

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4663, p4664, p4665, p4666

**Note:**
  
For value = 1:  
A third degree polynomial is defined as follows:  
F(x) = K3 * x^3 + K2 * x^2 + K1 * x + K0  
Coefficients K0 ... K3 should be defined and entered into p4663 ... p4666.  
The sensor range is emulated to x = -0.5 ... +0.5.

### p4670[0...n] Analog sensor configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for evaluation on the analog sensor.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 06 | Set velocity to 0 | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Position value range | -0.5 / +0.5 pulse | 0.0 / 1.0 pulse | - |
| 09 | Fault/alarm messages | Fault | Alarm | - |
| 10 | Channel B act | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Channel A act | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Commutation angle constant | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Suppress faults | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Two-channel evaluation | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | Extrapolation | OFF | ON | - |

**Notice:**
  
For bit 06:  
Setting the bit sets the velocity actual value (r0061) permanently to 0.  
For bit 13:  
Setting the bit sets the commutation angle permanently to the commutation angle offset
(p0431).

**Note:**
  
For bit 09:  
A setting of bit = 0 will trigger a fault for the relevant channel if the actual value
is invalid.  
A setting of bit = 1 will trigger an alarm for the relevant channel if the actual
value is invalid.  
Zu Bit 10, 11:  
If both channels are activated, the actual value is generated from the average value
of both channels. If a channel fails (actual value invalid), it is not included when
the average value is generated.  
For bit 14:  
The bit is only evaluated for encoder 1. Otherwise no effect.  
For bit 15:  
When the bit is set, both input signals are separately evaluated and transferred.  
Prerequisite:  
Function "Extended cyclic DRIVE-CLiQ telegram" is activated (p0454.4 = 1).

### p4671[0...n] Analog sensor input

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the input circuit for the analog sensor.

**Value:**
  
0:
Differential  
1:
Single-ended A, B  
2:
Single-ended A*, B*  
3:
Single-ended A, B sensitive

**Note:**
  
p4671 = 0:  
The two signals on a track are evaluated differentially.  
p4671 = 1:  
Only the non-inverted signal on a track is evaluated.  
p4671 = 2:  
Only the inverted signal on a track is evaluated.  
p4671 = 3:  
Only the non-inverted signal on a track (high resolution) is evaluated.

### p4673[0...n] Analog sensor channel A voltage per encoder period

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -10.0000 [V] | 10.0000 [V] | [ 0 ] 6.0000 [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the output voltage range to be mapped for the connected analog sensor.  
The voltage range is determined by the following parameters:  
- p4672 (voltage at actual value 0)  
- p4673 (voltage per encoder period)

**Note:**
  
The minimum actual value which can be mapped is equal to p4672 - p4673/2.  
The maximum actual value which can be mapped is equal to p4672 + p4673/2.

At this voltage channel B supplies an actual value of zero.

### p4675[0...n] Analog sensor channel B voltage per encoder period

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -10.0000 [V] | 10.0000 [V] | [ 0 ] 6.0000 [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the output voltage range to be mapped for the connected analog sensor.  
The voltage range is determined by the following parameters:  
- p4674 (voltage at actual value 0)  
- p4675 (voltage per encoder period)

**Note:**
  
The minimum actual value which can be mapped is equal to p4674 - p4675/2.  
The maximum actual value which can be mapped is equal to p4674 + p4675/2.

### p4676[0...n] Analog sensor range limit threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold for limit monitoring of the absolute actual value on the analog
sensor.  
If this threshold is overshot by the actual value of a channel, a corresponding fault/alarm
(p4670.9) is output.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4673, p4675

### p4677[0...n] Analog sensor LVDT configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for LVDT mode on the analog sensor.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | LVDT ON | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Track B excitation | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Fixed value amplitude | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Fixed value amplitude and phase | No | Yes | - |

### p4680[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance permissible

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1000 | [ 0 ] 4 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible tolerance in encoder pulses for the zero mark distance in the
context of zero mark monitoring.  
Causes fault F3x100 to appear less frequently.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F31100

### p4681[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window limit 1 positive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1000 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the positive tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 1 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
The pulse number is corrected if the deviation is less than this limit. If it is higher
than this limit, fault F3x131 is triggered.  
If fault F3x131 is re-parameterized to an alarm (A) or no message (N), then the encoder
pulses that are not corrected are transferred to the accumulator (p4688). The accumulator
can be deactivated using p0437.7.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131

**Note:**
  
This monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).  
The positive limit describes additional pulses due to EMC.

### p4682[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window limit 1 negative

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -1001 | 0 | [ 0 ] -1001 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the negative tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 1 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
The pulse number is corrected if the deviation is less than this limit. If it is higher
than this limit, fault F3x131 is triggered.  
If fault F3x131 is re-parameterized to an alarm (A) or no message (N), then the encoder
pulses that are not corrected are transferred to the accumulator (p4688). The accumulator
can be deactivated using p0437.7.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4681, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131

**Note:**
  
This monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).  
For a set value = -1001, the negated value of p4681 becomes active.  
The negative limit describes the pulses lost due to a covered glass panel in the incremental
encoder.

### p4683[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window alarm threshold positive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100000 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the positive tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 2 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
Accumulator (p4688) is compared with this parameter, and where relevant, alarm A3x422
is output for 5 seconds.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4681, p4682, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131, A31422

**Note:**
  
Zero mark monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).

### p4684[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window alarm threshold negative

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the negative tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 2 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
Accumulator (p4688) is compared with this parameter, and where relevant, alarm A3x422
is output for 5 seconds.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4683, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131, A31422

**Note:**
  
Zero mark monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).  
For a set value = -100001, the negated value of p4683 is effective.

### p4685[0...n] Speed actual value average value generation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 20 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the number of current controller sampling times for average value generation
of the speed actual value.

**Note:**
  
Value = 0, 1: No average value generation.  
Higher values also mean higher dead times for the speed actual value.

### p4686[0...n] Zero mark minimum length

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 10 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the minimum length for the zero mark in 1/4 encoder pulses.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0425, p0437

**Note:**
  
The minimum length of the zero mark must be less than the zero mark distance (p4686
< p0425).  
The parameter is activated using p0437.1 = 1 (zero mark edge detection).

|  |  |  |

[
1]:
Encoder 2  
[
2]:

**Note:**
  
The display can only be reset to zero.

### p5820[0...n] DIH control word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0010 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the control word for DIH.  
Using the "DIH" function, torsional oscillations in the drive train are dampened,
which are excited as a result of interharmonic oscillations in the electrical torque.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Activate natural frequency 1 | No | Yes | 6775, 6777 |
| 01 | Activate natural frequency 2 | No | Yes | 6775, 6777 |
| 04 | Activate order number 1 | No | Yes | 6775 |
| 05 | Activate order number 2 | No | Yes | 6775 |
| 08 | Enable supplementary torque | No | Yes | 6777 |
| 09 | IHD enable cancel | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49993

**Note:**
  
DIH: damping interharmonics (damping interharmonic oscillations)  
For 12-pulse line converters, it is only permissible to select order number 1.  
For bit 00, 01 or 04, 05:  
Damping is enabled if at least one natural frequency and one order number are activated.

### p5821[0...n] DIH natural frequency 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6775, 6777 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 3.0 [Hz] | 36.0 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 12.0 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets natural frequency 1 of the mechanical drive train.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5822

### p5822[0...n] DIH natural frequency range 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6775 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 50.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 20.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets natural frequency range 1 of the mechanical drive train.  
Upper frequency limit = p5821 * (1 + p5822/100 %)  
Lower frequency limit = p5821 * (1 - p5822/100 %)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5821

**Note:**
  
To avoid overlap between the interharmonic order numbers, this value should be a maximum
of 25%.

| --- | --- | --- |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 3.0 [Hz] | 36.0 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 12.0 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

4.7

**Dependency:**

p5824

4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets natural frequency range 2 of the mechanical drive train.  
Upper frequency limit = p5823 * (1 + p5824/100 %)  
Lower frequency limit = p5823 * (1 - p5824/100 %)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5823

**Note:**
  
To avoid overlap between the interharmonic order numbers, this value should be a maximum
of 25%.

### p5825[0...n] DIH bandpass filter bandwidth

|  |  |  |

### r5826[0...1] CO: DIH torque actual value filtered

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6776 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2003 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Nm] | - [Nm] | [ ] - [Nm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the electrical torque actual value, which is generated
by the actual interharmonic oscillation.  
r5826[0]: torque actual value before coordinate transformation  
r5826[1]: torque actual value after coordinate transformation

**Dependency:**
  
For r5826[1]:  
The absolute value is only displayed if damping is enabled via control word (p5820),
and the frequency of the interharmonic oscillation lies in the natural frequency range
1 or 2.

### p5832[0...n] DIH modulation angle correction value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6776 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -90.0 [°] | 90.0 [°] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the correction value for the modulation angle.

**Note:**
  
Correction angle in the range +/-45° is added to the internally calculated modulation
angle.  
Correction angle outside this range deactivates the internal calculation. In this
case, only this value is used as modulation angle

### p5836[0...n] DIH supplementary torque response sensitivity

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 6777 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 500.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the response sensitivity to identify a torsional oscillation in the drive train.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5837, r5838

**Note:**
  
A supplementary torque (r5838) is not calculated below the response sensitivity.  
When setting the maximum value, a supplementary torque is always calculated.

### r5838 CO: DIH supplementary torque

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6777 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2003 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Nm] | - [Nm] | [ ] - [Nm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the supplementary torque.

**Dependency:**
  
An additional torque is not calculated when IHD is active (r5839.8 = 1) or during
DC link pulsing (r6260.19 = 0).  
  
Refer to:
p1513, p5836, p5837, r6260

**Note:**
  
The supplementary torque can be interconnected into the speed control loop via connector
input p1513.

### r5839.0...18 CO/BO: DIH status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the DIH.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Natural frequency 1 active | No | Yes | 6775 |
| 01 | Natural frequency 2 active | No | Yes | 6775 |
| 04 | Order number 1 active | No | Yes | 6775, 6776 |
| 05 | Order number 2 active | No | Yes | 6775, 6776 |
| 08 | DIH active | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Filter settling time running | Yes | No | - |
| 10 | Limiting active | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | DIH enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Modulation angle calculation active | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | DIH interrupted | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Enable supplementary torque | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | Supplementary torque active | No | Yes | 6777 |
| 18 | DIH or supplementary torque active | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
DIH: damping interharmonics (damping interharmonic oscillations)  
For bit 08, 17, 18:  
These bits can be used to switch over the drive data set (e.g. to support the damping
of torsional oscillations of the drive train with a dynamic speed controller setting).  
For bit 18:  
This bit is the result of the OR logic operation involving bit 8 and bit 17.

### p6000[0...n] BI: De-magnetizing equipment, ON command

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 9820, 9821, 9823, 9824 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the ON command of the de-magnetizing equipment of the output
transformer.  
If internal de-magnetizing is selected (p6008 = 2), the following applies:  
For a precise description of how internal de-magnetizing responds to the ON command,
please see the time diagram in the function diagram for internal de-magnetizing.

**Note:**
  
The ON command for the de-magnetizing equipment is effective with a 0/1 edge at binector
input p6000.

### r6005.0...20 CO/BO: De-magnetizing equipment sequence control status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9820, 9821, 9822, 9823 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the sequence control for the demagnetizing
equipment of the output transformer.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | De-magnetizing equipment activated | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | De-magnetizing equipment switched on | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | De-magnetizing equipment, disconnector closed | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | De-magnetizing completed | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | De-magnetizing equipment fault | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | De-magnetizing power-on condition fulfilled | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | De-magnetizing test mode requested | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Output transformer must be demagnetized | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | De-magnetizing running | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | De-magnetizing, On command | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | De-magnetizing, On feedback signal | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Feedback signal de-magnetizing completed | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | Feedback signal, de-magnetizing disconnector open | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | Feedback signal, de-magnetizing fault | No | Yes | - |

**Notice:**
  
The signals shown come from the sequence control and are not identical to the corresponding
feedback signals from the de-magnetizing equipment.

**Note:**
  
In the internal demagnetization mode (p6008 == 2), only bits 0,12,16 and 18 are displayed.

### p6008[0...n] De-magnetizing equipment enable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 9820, 9821, 9823, 9824 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

Setting to enable/inhibit de-magnetization of the output transformer.

### r6009 CO: De-magnetizing equipment sequence control status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9820, 9821, 9822, 9824 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 70 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the state of the sequence control of the demagnetizing
equipment.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inhibited  
10:
Inactive  
20:
OFF  
30:
De-magnetizing required  
40:
Switching-on operation  
50:
Active  
60:
Switching-off operation  
70:
Fault

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6005

**Note:**
  
In the "Demagnetization with internal process" mode (p6008 == 2), only the "Inhibited",
"Inactive" and "Active" states are displayed.

### p6010 BI: External command switch in excitation signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6496 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the external command "switch in excitation".  
BI: p6010 = 0 signal  
If an internal switch-in signal is not available, then the excitation is switched
out.  
BI: p6010 = 1 signal  
The excitation is switched in and signals "excitation ready" (r1649.1).

### p6012[0...3] Internal de-magnetizing modulation depths

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

[
2]:
Final value  
[
3]:
Modulation depth fixed value

**Note:**
  
The value entered will be rejected in the following cases:  
- if starting value > maximum value is entered in index 0.  
- if maximum value < start value or final value is entered in index 1.  
- if final value > maximum value or < start value is entered in index 2.  
- if de-magnetizing is operational when the value is entered.  
- for modulations which would exceed the maximum voltage at the transformer.  
- if the converter or de-magnetizing is operational.  
If index 3 is set to a value other than zero, this set modulation depth is implemented
for the duration set in p6013[3]. The ramp generator is then not active.

### p6013[0...3] Internal de-magnetizing modulation depth ramp

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9824 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 5 [s] | 240 [s] | [ 0 ] 5 [s]  [ 1 ] 15 [s]  [ 2 ] 5 [s]  [ 3 ] 15 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the ramp times for internal de-magnetizing.  
The following applies for index 0, 2:  
The time corresponds to a modulation depth change of 1.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Ramp-up time  
[
1]:
Maximum modulation depth duration  
[
2]:
Ramp-down time  
[
3]:
Fixed value control duration

### p6019 PU internal output disconnector mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9825 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the control mode for the internal output disconnectors.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Off only for a fault | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Opening under voltage permitted | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Manual operation permitted | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Manual operation on/off monitoring active | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
This bit is only active when the "Manual operation permitted" function has been activated
(bit 2 = 1).

### r6022.0...14 CO/BO: PU internal output disconnector control word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9825 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the control word for the internal output disconnectors.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Disconnector 1 enable at the PSA output | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Disconnector 1 close command at the PSA output | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Disconnector 1 open command at the PSA output | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Disconnector 1 close command at the PSA | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Disconnector 1 open command at the PSA | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Disconnector 1 present | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Disconnector 1 manual mode possible | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Disconnector 2 enable at the PSA output | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Disconnector 2 close command at the PSA output | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Disconnector 2 open command at the PSA output | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Disconnector 2 close command at the PSA | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Disconnector 2 open command at the PSA | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Disconnector 2 present | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Disconnector 2 manual mode possible | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6021

**Note:**
  
For bit 00 ... 02:  
These bits indicate the status of the control of the internal output disconnector
1 at the digital outputs (DO 4 ... DO 6) of the PSA for the line-side converter in
the 1st subsystem.  
For bits 08 to 10:  
These bits indicate the status of the control of the internal output disconnector
2 at the digital outputs (DO 4 ... DO 6) of the PSA for the line-side converter in
the 2nd subsystem.

### r6025.0...14 CO/BO: PU disconnector and grounding breaker display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9825 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the statuses of doors, disconnectors and grounding breakers in the subsystems.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Enable door release 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Door closed 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Disconnector 1 actuation enable | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Disconnector 1 open | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Disconnector 1 closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Grounding breaker 1 open | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Grounding breaker 1 closed | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Enable door release 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Door closed 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Disconnector 2 actuation enable | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Disconnector 2 open | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Disconnector 2 closed | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Grounding breaker 2 open | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Grounding breaker 2 closed | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 00, 08:  
Signal at PSA digital output 3  
For bit 01, 09:  
Signal at PSA digital input 3  
For bit 02, 10:  
Signal at PSA digital output 4  
For bit 03, 11:  
Signal at PSA digital input 5  
For bit 04, 12:  
Signal at PSA digital input 2  
If digital input 2 is not selected in the hardware description, the inverted state
of digital input 5 is displayed here.  
For bit 05, 13:  
Signal at PSA digital input 1  
If digital input 1 is not selected in the hardware description, the inverted state
of digital input 4 is displayed here.  
For bit 06, 14:  
Signal at PSA digital input 4  
For bit 03 ... 06, 11 ... 14:  
Grounding breaker and disconnector are optional components - selection in the hardware
description. These bits are "0" when not installed.  
For bits 08 to 14:  
These bits are only displayed for converters with 2 subsystems (DC links).  
If the auxiliary contact circuits (e.g. door contact circuit) are not separated according
to subsystems (description in the hardware description), bit 9 displays the state
of bit 1.

### p6026[0...7] BI: PU door release enable signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 6220.15   [ 1 ] 1   [ 2 ] 1   [ 3 ] 1   [ 4 ] 6220.15   [ 5 ] 1   [ 6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for the electromagnetic door release enable.  
The signals that are read in are ANDed with the internal states such as 'U=0', 'pulse
inhibit' and 'circuit breaker open' and output at Power Stack Adapter output DO3.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PSA_enable signal 1  
[
1]:
PSA_enable signal 2  
[
2]:
PSA_enable signal 3  
[
3]:
PSA_enable signal 4  
[
4]:
PSA_enable signal 5  
[
5]:
PSA_enable signal 6  
[
6]:
PSA_enable signal 7  
[
7]:
PSA_enable signal 8

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6027

**Note:**
  
The electromagnetic door release is an option in the power unit (PU).  
For an installed option, the following applies:  
Drives without PU redundancy:  
All release enable signals must be present in order to open the doors.  
The level of the enable signal is set in parameter p6027.  
Drives with PU redundancy:  
The release enable signals 1 ... 4 must be present in order to open the doors in the
PU subsystem 1.  
The release enable signals 5 ... 8 must be present in order to open the doors in the
PU subsystem 2.  
The level of the enable signal is set in parameter p6027.

### p6027 PU door release enable signals signal level

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1110 1110 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal level to evaluate for the signal sources of the door release enable.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Signal level door release enable signal 0 | Low | High | - |
| 01 | Signal level door release enable signal 1 | Low | High | - |
| 02 | Signal level door release enable signal 2 | Low | High | - |
| 03 | Signal level door release enable signal 3 | Low | High | - |
| 04 | Signal level door release enable signal 4 | Low | High | - |
| 05 | Signal level door release enable signal 5 | Low | High | - |
| 06 | Signal level door release enable signal 6 | Low | High | - |
| 07 | Signal level door release enable signal 7 | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6026

**Note:**
  
The bit positions correspond to the index of parameter p6026.

### p6039 PU DC link reactor minimum operating time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8038 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [s] | 86400 [s] | [ 0 ] 300 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the minimum operating time that is taken into account when calculating the delay
time in r6044.  
When the delay time in r6044 expires, the switching on inhibited is withdrawn and
the DC link reactor can be charged for at least the time set in this parameter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0207, r6041

**Note:**
  
The setting of the minimum operating time is effective if the rated current of the
DC link reactor in r6043 is less than the rated DC link current of the converter in
r0207.  
This is generally the case when used as starting converter.

### p6040 PU DC link reactor I2t response

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8038 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the response when the I2t monitoring for the DC link reactor responds.  
If the thermal utilization of the DC link reactor (r6045) exceeds the upper limit
value (r6041[0]) then the set response is executed and an appropriate signal is output.

**Value:**
  
0:
OFF2  
1:
Current reduction

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F30210, A30211

### r6044[0...n] CO: PU DC link reactor I2t remaining time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [s] | - [s] | [ ] - [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the remaining time or delay time.  
In operation:  
Displays the remaining time up until the response parameterized in p6040 is carried
out.  
In the switched off state:  
Displays the delay time that must expire until the drive can be switched on again.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6039

**Note:**
  
In operation:  
When making the calculation, it is assumed that the actual current setpoint does not
change - i.e. the speed and torque remain constant.  
In the switched off state:  
The delay time goes to "zero" if subsequent operation with the rated converter current
is possible in p6039. This corresponds to a typical starting sequence of a starting
converter.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the I2t monitoring of the DC link reactor.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DC link reactor 1 rated current < DC link rated current | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | DC link reactor 1 I2t > maximum value 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | DC link reactor 1 I2t > rated value | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | OFF2: DC link reactor 1 I2t > max | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | DC link reactor 1 current limiting active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | DC link reactor 1 switching on inhibited in cooling phase active | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | DC link reactor 2 rated current < DC link rated current | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | DC link reactor 2 I2t > maximum value | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | DC link reactor 2 I2t > rated value | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | OFF2: DC link reactor 2 I2t > max | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | DC link reactor 2 current limiting active | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | DC link reactor 2 switching on inhibited in cooling phase active | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For converters with only one subsystem (DC link), bits 8 ... 13 are not displayed.  
For converters with 2 DC links the state of bits 4 and 12 is identical.  
For converters with 2 DC links and without power unit redundancy, the state of bits
3 and 11 is identical.

### p6053[0...n] ELP integrator start threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 8033 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [V] | 100000.00 [V] | [ 0 ] 750.00 [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the start threshold for the integrator for the ground fault monitoring.  
For r6052 >= p6053, the following applies:  
The integrator output increases (p6054, r6056).  
For r6052 < p6053, the following applies:  
The integrator output decreases (p6055, r6056).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0210, r6052, p6054, p6055, r6056

**Note:**
  
After POWER ON, the parameter is pre-set from the hardware description data (25 %
of the rated power unit line supply voltage, r0208).

### p6054[0...n] ELP integrator increasing time constant

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 8033 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 10.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.20 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the time constant of the integrator for increasing integrator for ground fault
monitoring.  
The integrator output increases under the following condition:  
r6052 (displacement voltage, sum after filter) >= p6053 (integrator starting threshold).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6053, p6055, r6056

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the time constant of the integrator for decreasing integrator for ground fault
monitoring.  
The integrator output decreases under the following condition:

### p6060[0...1] BI: External command switch in power unit cooling signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), VECTORGL, VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the external command "switch on power unit cooling".  
BI: p6060 = 0 signal  
If there is no internal switch-on signal, then the power unit cooling is switched
off.  
Exceptions:  
- when the run-on time (p0264) is appropriately set.  
- when fault F49156 occurs.  
BI: p6060 = 1 signal  
The drive converter cooling is switched on.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Redundancy subsystem 1  
[
1]:
Redundancy subsystem 2

**Note:**
  
For redundancy, the cooling units of both subsystems are switched on using their own
index

### r6061[0...n] Power unit cooling digital inputs configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit, Fan) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the configuration of the digital inputs on the Power Stack Adapters (PSA)
for power unit cooling.  
There is a feedback signal for each PSA.  
Coding of word for air cooling:  
1) Nibble 0: Feedback signal contact track (DI 0).  
2) Nibble 1: Not used.  
3) Nibble 2: Not used.  
4) Nibble 3: Not used.  
Coding of word for liquid cooling:  
1) Nibble 0: Feedback signal leakage (DI 0).  
2) Nibble 1: Not used.  
3) Nibble 2: Not used.  
4) Nibble 3: Not used.  
The values of the individual nibbles have the following significance:  
0: Signal not assigned.  
1: Signal is assigned subsystem 1.  
2: Signal is assigned subsystem 2.  
3: Signal is assigned to the total system.

### r6062.0...3 BO: Cooling unit status word subsystem 2

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the control word of the cooling unit of subsystem 2 for power unit redundancy.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Switch on cooling unit | Deactivating | Switch on | - |
| 01 | Message converter off | ON | OFF | - |
| 02 | Acknowledge faults | No acknowledgment | Acknowledgment | - |
| 03 | Leakage sensing responded | No leaked liquid | Leaked liquid | - |

### r6068[0...n] PU offset angle measured

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**NSR, p0017 | **Func. diagram:** 8034 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°] | - [°] | [ ] - [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the measured offset angle.  
For r6068 = 360 °, the following applies:  
No sensible value was determined for the offset value.  
p6265 = 3: Determination offset angle line-side converter  
- r6068[0], r6068[1]: Displays the measured offset angle of the incoming-side transformer.  
p6265 = 4: Determination offset angle motor-side converter  
- r6068[0]: Displays the measured offset angle at the converter output between System
1 and System 2

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6067, p6265  
Refer to:
A30204

**Note:**
  
Procedure to determine the offset angle for the line-side converter:  
- set p6265 = 3.  
- enable pulses (BI: p0840). The offset angle is then automatically determined. This
state is displayed with r3402 = 44.  
- evaluate the result in r6068[0...n] approx. 10 s.  
Procedure to determine the offset angle for the motor-side converter:  
- set p6265 = 4.  
- enable pulses (BI: p0840). The offset angle is then automatically determined.  
- evaluate the result in r6068[0] after approx. 10 s.  
- when accepting the measured offset angle in p6397 (MSC phase shift) please note
that in the case of a counter-clockwise rotating field (r0063), the measured offset
angle has to be inverted.

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Line-side converter system 1 enable | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | Line-side converter system 2 enable | Inactive | Active | - |
| 08 | Motor-side converter system 1 enable | Inactive | Active | - |
| 09 | Motor-side converter system 2 enable | Inactive | Active | - |
| 13 | Activate motor-side converter offset angle determination | Inactive | Active | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6070

### p6070[0...1] PU thyristor monitoring operating mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8034 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 4 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the operating mode for the thyristor location monitoring.  
p6070 = 0:  
Thyristor self firing alarm A30201 de-activated  
Thyristor location display de-activated (r6078 and following)  
PSA protective response OFF2 deactivated  
Thyristor location fault fault F30110 de-activated  
Thyristor location display de-activated (r6072 and following)  
Valve monitoring fault F30202 active via setting in p6086  
p6070 = 1:  
Thyristor self firing alarm A30201 activated  
Thyristor location display activated (r6078 and following)  
PSA protective response OFF2 deactivated  
Thyristor location fault fault F30110 de-activated  
Valve monitoring fault F30202 deactivated  
Thyristor location display activated (r6072 and following)  
p6070 = 2:  
Thyristor self firing alarm A30201 activated  
Thyristor location display activated (r6078 and following)  
PSA protective response OFF2 activated  
Thyristor location fault fault F30110 activated  
Thyristor location display activated (r6072 and following)  
Valve monitoring fault F30202 active via setting in p6086  
p6070 = 3:  
Thyristor self firing alarm A30201 de-activated  
Thyristor location display de-activated (r6078 and following)  
Otherwise the same as setting 1  
p6070 = 4:  
Thyristor self firing alarm A30201 de-activated  
Thyristor location display de-activated (r6078 and following)  
Otherwise the same as setting 2

**Value:**
  
0:
Thyristor location monitoring off  
1:
Thyristor location monitoring test mode with self firing alarm  
2:
Thyristor location monitoring with self firing alarm  
3:
Thyristor location monitoring test mode w/out self firing alarm  
4:
Thyristor location monitoring without self firing alarm

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Line side  
[
1]:
Motor side

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6069, r6071, p6781  
Refer to:
F30110, A30200, A30201

**Notice:**
  
If actual value sensing on the input or output side is de-selected in parameter p6781,
it will no longer be possible to detect a short circuit in the converter. In this
case, it is strongly recommended to activate thyristor monitoring.

**Note:**
  
In spite of the enable, the monitoring is inhibited at certain operating points.  
The actual status of the enable signals is displayed in r6071.  
When magnetic firing is selected, thyristor location monitoring cannot be activated.  
In the test mode of the thyristor location monitoring, defects are displayed (r6072
and following).  
Deactivating the self firing alarm does not prevent a thyristor from self firing itself.

### r6071 CO: PU thyristor monitoring status word

|  |  |  |

2.6

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | LSC system 1 enable thyristor monitoring normal mode | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | LSC system 2 enable thyristor monitoring normal mode | Inactive | Active | - |
| 02 | MSC system 1 enable thyristor monitoring normal mode | Inactive | Active | - |
| 03 | MSC system 2 enable thyristor monitoring normal mode | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | LSC system 1 enable thyristor monitoring test mode | Inactive | Active | - |
| 05 | LSC system 2 enable thyristor monitoring test mode | Inactive | Active | - |
| 06 | MSC system 1 enable thyristor monitoring test mode | Inactive | Active | - |
| 07 | MSC system 2 enable thyristor monitoring test mode | Inactive | Active | - |
| 08 | LSC system 1 enable commutation monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 09 | LSC system 2 enable commutation monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 10 | MSC system 1 enable commutation monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 11 | MSC system 2 enable commutation monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 12 | LSC enable fiber-optic cable monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 13 | MSC enable fiber-optic cable monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 14 | LSC enable hold-off time measurement | Inactive | Active | - |
| 15 | MSC enable hold-off time measurement | Inactive | Active | - |
| 16 | LSC enable blocking voltage sensing | Inactive | Active | - |
| 17 | MSC enable block voltage sensing | Inactive | Active | - |
| 18 | LSC system 1 enable self-firing monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 19 | LSC system 2 enable self-firing monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 20 | MSC system 1 enable self-firing monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 21 | MSC system 2 enable self-firing monitoring | Inactive | Active | - |
| 22 | MSC offset angle determination in measuring range | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | LSC+MSC magnetic control | Inactive | Active | - |

|  |  |  |

[
0]:

[
1]:

### p6086[0...1] PU commutation duration monitoring enable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0001 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the enable for the commutation duration monitoring.  
Line-side converter (LSC):  
On the line side, the enable is only possible if thyristor monitoring is activated
in normal operation (p6070[0] = 2).  
Motor-side converter (MSC):  
On the motor side, the thyristor monitoring in normal operation must be activated
(p6070[1] = 2) in order to issue the enable signal for the commutation duration monitoring.
Further, the monitoring can only be switched-in in the load-commutated mode (p6260.19
= 1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Line side  
[
1]:
Motor side

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Commutation duration monitoring enable | Inactive | Active | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6070, r6260  
Refer to:
F30202

**Notice:**
  
If actual value sensing on the input or output side is de-selected in p6781, it will
no longer be possible to detect a short circuit in the converter. In this case, it
is strongly recommended to activate commutation monitoring.

**Note:**
  
When magnetic firing is selected, commutation monitoring cannot be activated.

### p6087[0...1] PU commutation duration monitoring supplementary angle

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [°] | 57.5 [°] | [ 0 ] 5.0 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the supplementary angle for the commutation duration monitoring.  
This supplementary angle is added to the actual overlap angle and is transferred to
the FPGA as converted time value of the commutation duration monitoring.  
Line-side converter (LSC):  
The result is displayed in r6088 as converted time value.  
Motor-side converter (MSC):  
The result is displayed in r6089 as converted time value.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Line side  
[
1]:
Motor side

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6086, r6088, r6089  
Refer to:
F30202

### r6088[0...n] PU LSC commutation monitoring value calculated

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [ms] | - [ms] | [ ] - [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
For the line-side converter (LSC) this displays the calculated commutation duration.  
The displayed value corresponds to a time converted from the actual line-side overlap
angle, the supplementary value (p6087[0]) and the actual line supply frequency.  
If the commutation duration is exceeded, a protective response is initiated on the
FPGA.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6086, p6087  
Refer to:
F30202

### r6089[0...n] PU MSC commutation duration monitoring value calculated

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [ms] | - [ms] | [ ] - [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
For the motor-side converter (MSC) this displays the calculated commutation duration.  
The displayed value corresponds to a time converted from the actual motor-side overlap
angle, the supplementary value (p6087[1]) and the actual motor frequency.  
If the commutation duration is exceeded, a protective response is initiated on the
FPGA.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6086, p6087  
Refer to:
F30202

### p6090[0...1] PU voltage monitoring window supplementary angle

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [°] | 30.0 [°] | [ 0 ] 5.0 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the supplementary angle for the voltage monitoring window.  
This supplementary angle is added, as converted time value, to the internally calculated
voltage monitoring value and transferred to the FPGA.  
Line-side converter (LSC):  
The result of the time value is displayed in r6091 as converted time value.  
Motor-side converter (MSC):  
The result of the time value is displayed in r6092 as converted time value.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Line side  
[
1]:
Motor side

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6086, r6088, r6089, r6091, r6092  
Refer to:
F30202
