### p6093[0...1] PU hold-off time measurement enable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8034 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to enable the hold-off time measurement.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Line side  
[
1]:
Motor side

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Hold-off time measurement enable | Inactive | Active | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6095, r6098

**Note:**
  
The hold-off time measurement is always performed on the line side for the LSC bridge
A valve arm 1and on the motor side always for the MSC valve arm 1.  
On the motor side, in order to enable the hold-off time measurement, the following
condition must prevail:  
- the motor frequency must be at least 2 Hz.  
When magnetic firing is selected, hold-off time measurement cannot be activated.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

2.6

Displays the minimum value of the hold-off time measurement on the line side.  
For the hold-off time measurement on the line side, only LSC, bridge A valve arm 1
is evaluated.

p6093

### r6100[0...n] CO: PU MSC hold-off time measurement Xk

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** 8034 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the commutation inductance Xk of the motor calculated from the result of
the hold-off time measurement.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6098

**Note:**
  
The following conditions must exist for the calculation:  
- minimum motor frequency of 2 Hz.  
- minimum DC link current of 20 % of the rated current.

### r6122 CO: PU thyristor monitoring group display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Group display of the fault messages for the thyristor monitoring.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Thyristor fault LSC system 1 bridge A | Okay | Defect | - |
| 01 | Thyristor self firing LSC system 1 bridge A | Okay | Defect | - |
| 02 | Commutation fault LSC system 1 bridge A | Okay | Defect | - |
| 03 | FOC damping LSC system 1 bridge A | Okay | Defect | - |
| 05 | Thyristor fault LSC system 1 bridge B | Okay | Defect | - |
| 06 | Thyristor self firing LSC system 1 bridge B | Okay | Defect | - |
| 07 | Commutation fault LSC system 1 bridge B | Okay | Defect | - |
| 08 | FOC damping LSC system 1 bridge B | Okay | Defect | - |
| 10 | Thyristor fault, MSC system 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 11 | Thyristor self firing, MSC system 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 12 | Commutation fault, MSC system 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 13 | FOC damping MSC system 1 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 16 | Thyristor fault LSC system 2 bridge A | Okay | Defect | - |
| 17 | Thyristor self firing LSC system 2 bridge A | Okay | Defect | - |
| 18 | Commutation fault LSC system 2 bridge A | Okay | Defect | - |
| 19 | FOC damping LSC system 2 bridge A | Okay | Defect | - |
| 21 | Thyristor fault LSC system 2 bridge B | Okay | Defect | - |
| 22 | Thyristor self firing LSC system 2 bridge B | Okay | Defect | - |
| 23 | Commutation fault LSC system 2 bridge B | Okay | Defect | - |
| 24 | FOC damping LSC system 2 bridge B | Okay | Defect | - |
| 26 | Thyristor fault, MSC system 2 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 27 | Thyristor self firing, MSC system 2 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 28 | Commutation fault, MSC system 2 | Okay | Defect | - |
| 29 | FOC damping MSC system 2 | Okay | Defect | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6070, p6115

**Note:**
  
When the monitoring is enabled, in the normal mode and in the test mode, the fault
states are displayed.  
FOC damping faults are only displayed if FOC monitoring has been enabled (p6115[x]
= 1).

### p6123[0...4] PU thyristor gating (TG)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 | 100000.0 | [ 0 ] 2.0   [ 1 ] 0.5   [ 2 ] 100.0   [ 3 ] 50.0   [ 4 ] 0.0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Setting values for the opto-electronic thyristor gating (TG).  
For index [0]:  
Electric motor frequency in Hz up to which the TG modules in the MSC are precharged
during forced operation. The value '0' deactivates TG precharging. The input value
is limited to maximum 10.0.  
For index [1]:  
Voltage in the DC link in pu with which the TG modules in the MSC should be precharged.
The amplitude of the phase-phase voltage at the LSC is the reference quantity. The
input value is limited to maximum 1.0.  
For index [4]:  
Number of cycles for precharging the TG in the MSC when the internal DMU starts (demagnetization
unit). The input value is limited to 3.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Precharging f limit  
[
1]:
Precharging Vdc  
[
2]:
PS buffer time  
[
3]:
PS minimum value  
[
4]:
Number of precharging cycles for the internal DMU

### p6125[0...1] BI: Changeover line supply LSS selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9803 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to select the active line-side circuit breaker.  
p6125[0] = 0, p6125[1] = 0 --> selects circuit-breaker 0  
p6125[0] = 1, p6125[1] = 0 --> selects circuit-breaker 1  
p6125[0] = 0, p6125[1] = 1 --> selects circuit-breaker 2  
p6125[0] = 1, p6125[1] = 1 --> selects circuit-breaker 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49970

**Note:**
  
LSS: Line Side Switch

### r6136.0...5 CO/BO: Changeover switch line supply LSS feedback signal status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9803 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status word for the feedback signals of the selected line-side circuit
breaker.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Feedback signal switch ready | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Feedback signal switch closed | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Feedback signal switch open | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 1 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 2 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Feedback signal no external trip | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6125

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |

### p6145[0...1] BI: Changeover switch motor MSS selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9804 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to select the active motor-side circuit breaker.  
p6145[0] = 0, p6145[1] = 0 --> selects motor side switch 0  
p6145[0] = 1, p6145[1] = 0 --> selects motor side switch 1  
p6145[0] = 0, p6145[1] = 1 --> selects motor side switch 2  
p6145[0] = 1, p6145[1] = 1 --> selects motor side switch 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49970

**Note:**
  
MSS: Motor Side Switch

### p6165[0...1] BI: Changeover switch PSU selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9817 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to select the active parallel switching unit (PSU).  
p6165[0] = 0, p6165[1] = 0 --> selects parallel switching unit 0  
p6165[0] = 1, p6165[1] = 0 --> selects parallel switching unit 1  
p6165[0] = 0, p6165[1] = 1 --> selects parallel switching unit 2  
p6165[0] = 1, p6165[1] = 1 --> selects parallel switching unit 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49971

### r6176.0...8 CO/BO: Changeover switch PSU feedback signal status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9817 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status word for the feedback signals of the selected parallel switching
unit (PSU).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Parallel switching unit is selected | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Parallel switching unit issues synchronizing command | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Parallel switching unit, operation | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Parallel switching unit fault | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Parallel switching unit, raise frequency | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Parallel switching unit, lower frequency | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Parallel switching unit, raise voltage | No | Yes | 9816 |
| 07 | Parallel switching unit, lower voltage | No | Yes | 9816 |
| 08 | Parallel switching unit inhibited | No | Yes | - |

### p6183[0...3] BI: Converter excitation external input signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6497 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source of the input signals to select the external excitation (input
4 bit --> output 2 bit).  
[0] = 0, [1] = 0, [2] = 0, [3] = 0 --> r6148 = 00 bin  
[0] = 1, [1] = 0, [2] = 0, [3] = 0 --> r6184 = 00 bin  
[0] = 0, [1] = 1, [2] = 0, [3] = 0 --> r6184 = 10 bin  
[0] = 0, [1] = 0, [2] = 1, [3] = 0 --> r6184 = 01 bin  
[0] = 0, [1] = 0, [2] = 0, [3] = 1 --> r6184 = 11 bin

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Bit 0  
[
1]:
Bit 1  
[
2]:
Bit 2  
[
3]:
Bit 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6184  
Refer to:
A49972

| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Converter output bit 0 | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Converter output bit 1 | OFF | ON | - |

### p6185[0...1] BI: Changeover switch excitation selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6497 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to select the active excitation equipment.  
p6185[0] = 0, p6185[1] = 0 --> selects excitation equipment 0  
p6185[0] = 1, p6185[1] = 0 --> selects excitation equipment 1  
p6185[0] = 0, p6185[1] = 1 --> selects excitation equipment 2  
p6185[0] = 1, p6185[1] = 1 --> selects excitation equipment 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49972

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

2.6

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Excitation ready for switching on feedback signal | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Excitation ready feedback signal | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Excitation operational feedback signal | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Excitation group signal fault | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Excitation group signal alarm | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Excitation at the voltage limit | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Excitation voltage present at the power unit | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Excitation in line operation | No | Yes | - |

### p6198 Changeover switch excitation mode for alarms

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6497 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to generate alarms A49973 "excitation not ready to be switched on" and
A49974 "excitation in line operation".  
p6198 = 0:  
The alarms are generated dependent on the excitation control word (r1648) as well
as the excitation status word (r1649).  
p6198 = 1:  
The alarms are generated depending on the excitation control word (r1648), excitation
status word (r1649) and r6221.4 "check power-on conditions".

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49973, A49974

### p6209[0...n] BI: Prepare drive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** - |

Parameters r3402 and r6222.7 indicate the switched on state.

Preparation is only supported in the settings "normal operation", "current-controlled
operation", "circulating operation" and "semi-manual operation" in parameter p6265.

### p6210 On/off mode selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Selects the 1st or 2nd channel control for the on/off command.  
For 1-channel operator control, the following applies:  
The binector input for the on/off command is BI: p0840.  
For 2-channel operator control, the following applies:  
The binector input for the on command is BI: p0840.  
The binector input for the off command is BI: p6211

**Value:**
  
0:
On/off 1 channel control  
1:
On/off 2 channel control

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0840, p6211

### p6219[0...n] Transformer inrush delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 600.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 5.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for the transformer inrush.  
After the delay time has expired, the line supply monitoring/line supply control is
enabled.  
The delay time is started after the incoming switch has been closed.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r3402, p6231, p6239, p6248

**Note:**
  
A change to the parameter setting, whether as a result of a manual input or a DDS
changeover, has no effect while the closing sequence is in progress.  
For drives with inrush circuit breaker (p6231) the following condition applies:  
p6219 > p6248 (inrush LSS close duration inrush circuit).

### r6220.0...15 CO/BO: Sequence control control word 2

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2511, 2610, 2611, 9821 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the control word 2 for the sequence control.  
For bit 02:  
Without an enable signal, the motor-side circuit breakers either remain open or are
opened.  
The drive remains in its present state or changes from "Operation" into "Ready for
operation" (r3402).  
In the "Ready for operation" state, the motor-side circuit breaker can be closed using
this control bit.  
To change into the "Operation" state, the motor-side circuit breaker must be closed
and the operating enable present in r0898.3.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | ON command (2-channel input) | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | OC/OFF command (2-channel input) | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Enable motor-side circuit breaker | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | OC/OFF command from the parallel switching unit | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Lamp test on | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Prepare drive | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | External converter enable | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | ON command subsystem 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | ON command subsystem 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Build-up excitation enable | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Test operation on | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Stop | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Safety circuit closed | Open | Closed | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 02:  
This control bit has no effect if there is no motor-side circuit breaker.

### r6221.0...31 CO/BO: Sequence control control command

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2512, 2610, 6330, 6497, 9805, 9815, 9821 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the control commands of the sequence control.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Auxiliaries 1 on | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Internal output disconnector 1 close | OFF | ON | - |
| 02 | Power circuit excitation on | OFF | ON | - |
| 03 | Output transformer de-magnetizing on | OFF | ON | - |
| 04 | Check the power-on conditions | OFF | ON | 6497 |
| 05 | Inrush circuit breaker 1 close | OFF | ON | - |
| 06 | Inrush circuit breaker 2 close | OFF | ON | - |
| 07 | Motor sequence control on | OFF | ON | - |
| 08 | Line-side circuit breaker 1 close | OFF | ON | - |
| 09 | Motor side circuit breaker 1 close | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Motor subsystem 1 on | OFF | ON | - |
| 11 | Ground fault monitoring subsystem 1 enable | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Line-side circuit breaker 2 close | OFF | ON | - |
| 13 | Motor side circuit breaker 2 close | OFF | ON | - |
| 14 | Motor subsystem 2 on | OFF | ON | - |
| 15 | Ground fault monitoring subsystem 2 enable | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Auxiliaries 2 on | OFF | ON | - |
| 17 | Internal output disconnector 2 close | OFF | ON | - |
| 18 | PREMAG input transformer 1 on | OFF | ON | - |
| 19 | PREMAG input transformer 2 on | OFF | ON | - |
| 24 | Control signal lamp 'operation' | OFF | ON | - |
| 25 | Control signal lamp 'off' | OFF | ON | - |
| 26 | Control signal lamp 'alarm' | OFF | ON | - |
| 27 | Control signal lamp 'fault' | OFF | ON | - |
| 28 | Control signal lamp 'active' | OFF | ON | - |
| 31 | Contactor safety combination | OFF | ON | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6650

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
The de-magnetizing equipment is only controlled from the sequence control in the test
mode (p6650 = 4).

### r6222.0...30 CO/BO: Sequence control status word 2

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2513 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word 2 of the sequence control.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Converter cooling on | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Output disconnector close | OFF | ON | - |
| 02 | Power circuit excitation on | OFF | ON | - |
| 03 | Output transformer de-magnetizing on | OFF | ON | - |
| 06 | Inrush circuit breakers are closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 07 | Drive converter prepared | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Incoming circuit breaker close | OFF | ON | - |
| 09 | Output circuit breaker close | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Converter is operational | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Offset angle line-side converter determination | Inactive | Active | - |
| 12 | All subsystems in normal operation | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Stop active | Inactive | Active | - |
| 15 | Quick stop active | Inactive | Active | - |
| 16 | Circuit breaker open active | Inactive | Active | - |
| 17 | Open output-side circuit breaker (bypass) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 18 | Enable output-side circuit breaker changeover (bypass) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 19 | MDS without step-up transformer selected (bypass) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 20 | Operation without step-up transformer permitted (bypass) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 21 | Operation with step-up transformer permitted (bypass) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 22 | Ramp-function generator enabled (bypass) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 23 | MDS with step-up transformer selected (bypass) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 24 | New alarm detected | No | Yes | - |
| 25 | Fault with stop response OFF1 is present | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | Fault with stop response OFF2 is present | No | Yes | - |
| 27 | Fault with stop response OFF3 is present | No | Yes | - |
| 28 | CU passive: sequencer has been corrected | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | Close inhibit motor circuit breaker | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | Wait for motor CB close enable | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 17:  
Open command to open the circuit breaker and disconnector at the output.  
When opening, the signal is present for 5 s. In line operation (r1649.15) the signal
is a steady-state signal.  
For bit 18:  
Changeover operation in the bypass circuit is permitted.  
The signal is available in the open (switched-off) state as well as during a bypass
changeover including MDS changeover. Bypass changeover initiated using p0852 or p6208.  
For bit 20:  
Indicates whether in the present operating state, operation is possible without a
step-a transformer.  
For drives without encoder, this state can only be identified when the machine is
energized.  
For bit 21:  
Indicates whether in the present operating state, operation is possible with a step-a
transformer.  
The state is displayed, if the converter was able to determine the output frequency
(without encoder) or the speed (with encoder).  
For bit 20, 21 = 0, the decision as to how to start should be made externally!  
For bit 22:  
Enabling the RFG.  
This bit can be interconnected to p1141 to control the RFG. The RFG is then held during
the changeover. All circuit breakers and/or disconnectors must be externally controlled.
The setting "No CB on the output side" must be selected in parameter p6230[1].  
For bit 29:  
The switching on inhibited is withdrawn if bit 2 in control word 2 (r6220) is not
active.  
For bit 30:  
The system waits for a positive edge from bit 2 in control word 2 (r6220).  
Bits 17 ... 22 are outputs to control a step-up transformer bypass circuit at the
converter output.

| 02 | Motor-side circuit breaker 1 is closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 03 | Converter subsystem 1 is on | OFF | ON | - |
| 04 | Subsystem 1 fault present | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Subsystem 1 is off | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Inrush circuit breaker 1 is closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 07 | Normal operation subsystem 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Subsystem 2 is ready for switching on | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Line-side circuit breaker 2 is closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Motor-side circuit breaker 2 is closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 11 | Converter subsystem 2 is on | OFF | ON | - |
| 12 | Subsystem 2 fault present | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Subsystem 2 is off | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Inrush circuit breaker 2 is closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 15 | Normal operation subsystem 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Converter cooling 1 is on | OFF | ON | - |
| 17 | Output disconnector 1 is closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 18 | Switching on inhibited subsystem 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | PREMAG input transformer completed subsystem 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | Converter cooling 2 is on | OFF | ON | - |
| 21 | Output disconnector 2 is closed | OFF | ON | - |
| 22 | Switching on inhibited subsystem 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | PREMAG input transformer completed subsystem 2 | No | Yes | - |

### r6224[0...n] CO: Sequence control subsystem state

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 140 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the state of the sequence control of the subsystems.  
The sequence control indicates 'normal operation' if there is no fault in the subsystem
(r7002), no missing enable signal (r6225) and the subsystem command is present (r6220).

**Value:**
  
0:
Not active  
5:
Normal operation  
10:
Switching on inhibited  
20:
Ready for switching on  
21:
Check the power-on conditions  
29:
Prepare for operation  
30:
Auxiliaries on  
31:
Output disconnector close  
32:
Motor circuit breaker close  
34:
Input transformer premagnetization on  
35:
Inrush switch close  
36:
Line Side Switch close  
38:
Wait for feedback signals  
39:
Ready  
40:
Excitation circuit breaker/contactor close  
41:
Ready with build-up excitation  
42:
Close inhibit MSS  
43:
Ready for operation without MSS  
44:
Determination offset angle line-side converter  
45:
Operation  
46:
Internal demagnetization  
48:
Stop  
49:
Quick stop  
50:
Circuit breaker open  
100:
Test mode  
101:
Test mode auxiliaries  
102:
Test mode output disconnector  
103:
Test mode excitation circuit breaker/contactor  
104:
Test mode output transformer demagnetization  
110:
Test mode circuit breaker input side  
120:
Test mode circuit breaker output side  
130:
Test mode inrush circuit breaker  
140:
Test mode input transformer premagnetization

**Note:**
  
This section of the sequence control is only active for drives with power unit redundancy.
Drives without redundant power units always remain in the 'normal operation' state.

### r6225[0...n] CO: Missing enable signals for subsystem

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for missing enable signals that prevent operation with
the subsystem.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | ON command still active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | OFF2 enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | OFF3 enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Operation enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | 2-channel off enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | External switching on inhibited active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Commissioning active | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Drive converter cooling not ready for switching on | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Drive converter cooling not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Line PLL not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Ramp-function generator enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Ramp-function generator start missing | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Setpoint enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Transformer inrush incoming transformer active | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Output transformer de-magnetizing active | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Output transformer not de-magnetized | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Ext. enable signals missing | No | Yes | 2635 |
| 17 | Excitation equipment not ready or line operation | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Parallel switching unit fault present | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | Subsystem ON command still active | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | Reactor, I2T load too high | No | Yes | 8038 |
| 22 | Safety circuit open | Closed | Open | - |
| 23 | Cooling-down time after turn mode active | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Motor data set changeover active | No | Yes | - |
| 25 | Switching on inhibited motor sequence control active | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | Drive passive or subsystem not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 27 | Internal output disconnector not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 28 | Subsystem off enable missing | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | Fault in the power unit | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | Circuit breaker control blocked | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | Circuit breaker not ready | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0046, p6230

**Note:**
  
The bits 0 ... 7, 10 ... 15, 17, 18, 22 ... 27 indicate the missing enable signals
that act on the complete system. These bits are also displayed in r0046.  
Bits 8, 9, 16, 20, 21, 26, 28 ... 31 indicate the state in the subsystem. A "1" in
one of these bits prevents the powering-up and the operating enable in the subsystem.

### r6229.0...8 CO/BO: Inrush LSS status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status word of the inrush circuit breaker monitoring.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Inrush switching operations available system 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Inrush switching operations available system 2 | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6223, r6234, r6238, p6590  
Refer to:
A49907

**Note:**
  
For bit 00, 08:  
It is reset when the switching operations are finished and the subsystem is deactivated.
It is set again when the quiescent time has expired. As standard, there is an interconnection
with the power-on enable (p6590), which prevents renewed power-on during the quiescent
time (r6238).

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 06230 - 06350

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 06230 - 06350

### p6230[0...1] Circuit breaker configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 5 | [ 0 ] 1   [ 1 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the circuit breaker.

**Value:**
  
0:
No circuit breaker  
1:
1 circuit breaker primary side  
2:
Circuit breaker in each subsystem primary side  
3:
Circuit breaker in each subsystem secondary side  
4:
Bypass + 2 stepup circuit breaker  
5:
Bypass + stepup + motor circuit breaker

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Line side  
[
1]:
Motor side

**Note:**
  
The selection of the circuit breaker for the transformer bypass on the output side
is only supported in the motor-side index.  
For the setting p6230[1] = 4, the control of the switch in the bypass circuit and
in the step-up circuit is supported on the primary side and secondary side.  
The switch at the primary of the stepup transformer and the switch in the bypass circuit
must both be circuit breakers.  
The following assignment applies:  
MSS 1 -> circuit breaker in the bypass circuit  
MSS 2 -> circuit breaker (or disconnector) in the stepup circuit, secondary side  
MSS 3 -> circuit breaker in the stepup circuit, primary side  
For the setting p6230[1] = 5, the control of the switch in the bypass circuit, in
the stepup circuit and in the motor feeder cable is supported. The control of the
switches in the stepup circuit on the primary side and secondary side should be combined.  
A circuit breaker must be used for the switch in the motor feeder cable.  
The following assignment applies:  
MSS 1 -> circuit breaker (or disconnector) in the bypass circuit  
MSS 2 -> all circuit breakers (or disconnectors) in the stepup circuit  
MSS 3 -> circuit breaker in the motor feeder cable  
MSS1 and MSS2 are always controlled if MSS3 is open.  
If the monitoring times in parameters p6674 and p6675 are set > 2 for MSS1 and MSS2,
then during a switchover in the bypass circuit, MSS1 and MSS2 are simultaneously controlled.
If this is not the case, then MSS2 is only controlled when MSS1 has ended the switching
operation.

### p6231 Inrush LSS configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the inrush circuit breaker.

**Value:**
  
0:
No inrush circuit breaker  
1:
Inrush circuit breaker parallel line-side circuit breaker  
2:
Inrush circuit breaker in series with line-side circuit breaker

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6230

**Caution:**
  
In order to guarantee a power-on enable when all of the switching operations have
been utilized (r6234), there must be an interconnection between r6229 bit 0/8 (switching
operations available) and the power-on enable p6590.

**Note:**
  
The inrush circuit breaker can be selected in the following cases:  
- p6230 = 1 (1 circuit breaker, primary side)  
- p6230 = 2 (circuit breaker in each subsystem, primary side)

### p6232[0...n] Sequence control stop response selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the stop responses for commands and faults.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | OFF1 command as for command OFF2 | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Stop response OFF1 as for OFF2 | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | OFF3 command as for command OFF2 | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Stop response OFF3 as for OFF2 | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Switching on inhibited for faults without stop response | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Cancel shutdown for operating inhibit | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Response for PSG synchronizing command | Off2 | MSS off | - |

### p6233 Number of inrush LSS close operations

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum possible number of close operations for the inrush circuit breaker.  
If the number of close operations is exceeded within the monitoring time (p6235) then
bit "Inrush switching operation available" in the inrush LSS status word (r6229) is
set.

**Dependency:**

r6229, p6231, p6235

### p6235 Inrush LSS monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time for the close operations of the inrush circuit breaker.  
The monitoring time must always be less than the quiescent time (p6237).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6231, p6233, p6237

**Warning:**
  
If the monitoring time is set to be longer than the quiescent time (p6237), the quiescent
time (p6237) is increased, with the result that p6237 >= p6235 is applicable.

### p6237 Inrush LSS quiescent time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

| 1.0 [s] | 3600.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 600.0 [s] |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the quiescent time for the inrush circuit breaker.  
The quiescent time must always be more than the monitoring time (p6235).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6231, p6235

**Warning:**
  
If the quiescent time is set to be less than the monitoring time (p6235), the monitoring
time (p6235) is decreased, with the result that p6237 >= p6235 is applicable.

**Note:**
  
The quiescent time is started after the monitoring time (p6235) has expired.

### p6239[0...n] Inrush LSS overlap time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 5.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the overlap time for the inrush circuit breaker.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6219, p6231

**Note:**
  
For p6231 = 1 (inrush circuit breaker connected in parallel with line circuit breaker),
the following applies:  
- in this configuration, the overlap time setting is relevant for the inrush circuit
breaker. The overlap time is the time during which the main and inrush circuit breakers
may be simultaneously closed.  
- the following must be observed: p6239 (inrush LSS overlap time) < p6248 (inrush
LSS close duration inrush circuit).  
For p6231 = 2 (inrush circuit breaker connected in series with line circuit breaker)
the following applies:  
- the overlap time is not relevant.  
The overlap time is the time during which the main and inrush circuit breakers may
be simultaneously closed.  
A change to the parameter setting, whether as a result of a manual input or a DDS
changeover, has no effect while the closing sequence is in progress.

### p6244[0...n] Voltage model delay time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [ms] | 10000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 500 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for the converter enable.  
In the "circulating operation" mode, the converter enable is delayed by this time
until the voltage model has stabilized.  
In the "normal operation" mode, after the converter has been enabled (r6260.5) the
"permissible flux difference in converter operation" (p6283) is not monitored for
this time.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6260, r6262

| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 10.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time for external converter enable.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r3402, p6227, p6228, p6265

**Note:**
  
The converter enable delay is not effective in the manual operating modes (p6265 =
100, 110).

### p6248[0...n] Inrush LSS close duration inrush circuit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 20.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 3.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
The close duration of the LSS must be greater than the inrush overlap time (p6239).  
For p6231 = 1 (inrush circuit breaker connected in parallel with line circuit breaker),
the following applies:  
The time for which the inrush breaker is closed is set in the parameter.  
For p6231 = 2 (inrush circuit breaker connected in series with line circuit breaker)
the following applies:  
The time for which the line-side circuit breaker alone is closed is set in the parameter.
After the time has expired, the inrush breaker that bypasses the inrush resistor is
closed.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6219, p6231

**Note:**
  
A change to the parameter setting, whether as a result of a manual input or a DDS
changeover, has no effect while the closing sequence is in progress.  
The following applies:  
p6239 (inrush LSS overlap time) < p6248 (inrush LSS close duration inrush circuit)

### p6249[0...n] Circuit breaker closing time delay

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 600.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
After the line circuit breaker in subsystem 1 has closed, the line circuit breaker
of subsystem 2 (including existing inrush circuit breakers) is not closed until the
time set in this parameter has expired.  
The converters are enabled when the time set in parameter p6219 has expired.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6219, p6230

**Note:**
  
For p6249 = 0, delayed closure of the line circuit breaker is inactive.  
A setting of > 0 can be entered only for drives with at least 2 subsystems and if
each of these has a separate line circuit breaker.  
A change to the parameter setting, whether as a result of a manual input or a DDS
changeover, has no effect while the closing sequence is in progress.

### r6250.0...8 CO/BO: Output transformer bypass control commands

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the control commands to control the transformer bypass
circuit at the converter output.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Enable RFG | Inactive | Active | - |
| 05 | MSS 1 close | Inactive | Active | - |
| 06 | MSS 2 close | Inactive | Active | - |
| 07 | MSS 3 close | Inactive | Active | - |
| 08 | Open all circuit breakers on the output side | Inactive | Active | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6230

**Note:**
  
The assignment of the circuit breaker on the output side (MSS) depends on the setting
of p6230[1].  
p6230[1] = 4:  
Bit 5: MSS 1 -> circuit breaker in the bypass circuit  
Bits 6: MSS 2 -> circuit breaker (or disconnector) in the step-up circuit, secondary
side  
Bit 7: MSS 3 -> circuit breaker in the step-up circuit, primary side  
p6230[1] = 5:  
Bit 5: MSS 1 -> circuit breaker (or disconnector) in the bypass circuit  
Bit 6: MSS 2 -> all circuit breakers (or disconnectors) in the stepup circuit  
Bit 7: MSS 3 -> circuit breaker in the motor feeder cable

|  |  |  |

Display and BICO output for the status of the control of the transformer bypass circuit
at the converter output.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Operation without step-up transformer permitted | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | Operation with step-up transformer permitted | Inactive | Active | - |
| 02 | MDS without step-up transformer selected | Inactive | Active | - |
| 03 | MDS with step-up transformer selected | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Enable output-side circuit breaker changeover | Inactive | Active | - |
| 05 | MSS 1 is closed | Inactive | Active | - |
| 06 | MSS 2 is closed | Inactive | Active | - |
| 07 | MSS 3 is closed | Inactive | Active | - |
| 08 | Close/open CB active | Inactive | Active | - |
| 09 | Ready for enable | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6230

**Note:**
  
The assignment of the circuit breaker on the output side (MSS) depends on the setting
of p6230[1].  
p6230[1] = 4:  
Bit 5: MSS 1 -> circuit breaker in the bypass circuit  
Bits 6: MSS 2 -> circuit breaker (or disconnector) in the step-up circuit, secondary
side  
Bit 7: MSS 3 -> circuit breaker in the step-up circuit, primary side  
p6230[1] = 5:  
Bit 5: MSS 1 -> circuit breaker (or disconnector) in the bypass circuit  
Bit 6: MSS 2 -> all circuit breakers (or disconnectors) in the stepup circuit  
Bit 7: MSS 3 -> circuit breaker in the motor feeder cable

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting to test individual firing command channels of a converter.

**Value:**
  
0:
Firing command test deactivated  
1:
Test firing command light transmitter X11  
2:
Test firing command light transmitter X12  
3:
Test firing command light transmitter X13  
4:
Test firing command light transmitter X14  
5:
Test firing command light transmitter X15  
6:
Test firing command light transmitter X16  
7:
Test firing command light transmitter X21  
8:
Test firing command light transmitter X22  
9:
Test firing command light transmitter X23  
10:
Test firing command light transmitter X24  
11:
Test firing command light transmitter X25  
12:
Test firing command light transmitter X26  
13:
Test firing command light transmitter X31  
14:
Test firing command light transmitter X32  
15:
Test firing command light transmitter X33  
16:
Test firing command light transmitter X34  
17:
Test firing command light transmitter X35  
18:
Test firing command light transmitter X36  
19:
Test firing command light transmitter X41  
20:
Test firing command light transmitter X42  
21:
Test firing command light transmitter X43  
22:
Test firing command light transmitter X44  
23:
Test firing command light transmitter X45  
24:
Test firing command light transmitter X46  
25:
Test firing command light transmitter X51  
26:
Test firing command light transmitter X52  
27:
Test firing command light transmitter X53  
28:
Test firing command light transmitter X54  
29:
Test firing command light transmitter X55  
30:
Test firing command light transmitter X56  
31:
Test firing command light transmitter X61  
32:
Test firing command light transmitter X62  
33:
Test firing command light transmitter X63  
34:
Test firing command light transmitter X64  
35:
Test firing command light transmitter X65  
36:
Test firing command light transmitter X66

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6259, p6265, p6266

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
this test mode.

**Note:**
  
Manual operation must be set for this test (p6265 = 100, 110).  
For p6265 = 110 "Manual operation":  
In this operating mode, the test can be performed without voltage at the terminals
of the LSC and MSC.  
To do this, p6350 must be set =1 "Open-loop controlled operation without synchronizing
voltage".  
The frequency is set using p6351 (LSC) and p6285 (MSC).  
For p6265 = 100 "Partially manual operation":  
In this operating mode, the test can be performed with voltage at the terminals of
the LSC and MSC.  
To do this, p6350 must be set = 2 "Open-loop controlled operation with synchronizing
voltage".  
In general:  
Parameter p6266 is used to generally enable the pulses.  
It is recommended that only the pulses of the converter, in which the test should
be performed, are enabled.  
The firing command test can be deactivated by de-selecting the manual operating mode
in parameter p6265.  
Only one firing command test can be simultaneously executed in the line-side converter
or motor-side converter.

### r6260.0...31 CO/BO: Motor sequence control control commands

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the control commands of the motor sequence control.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | EMF offset control | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Fast EMF offset control | OFF | ON | - |
| 02 | Excitation equipment enable | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Voltage model AC quantity structure enable | OFF | ON | - |
| 04 | Voltage model DC quantity structure enable | OFF | ON | - |
| 05 | Converter enable | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Speed actual value enable from encoder | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | PLL enable encoder operation | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Pulse enable LSC 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Pulse enable MSC 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Pulse enable LSC 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Pulse enable MSC 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Speed controller enable | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | Flux controller enable | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Hold flux controller | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | Motor control MSC | No | Yes | 6718, 6726, 8035 |
| 20 | Motor torque direction | Negative | Positive | 6718 |
| 21 | Request motor operation | Gen operation | mot oper | - |
| 22 | Set ramp-function generator to speed actual value | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | Offset monitoring DC link voltage actual values | Inactive | Active | - |
| 24 | Offset monitoring current actual values | Inactive | Active | - |
| 25 | Offset monitoring voltage actual values | Inactive | Active | - |
| 26 | Offset monitoring voltage actual values star point | Inactive | Active | - |
| 27 | Calculate encoder offset | Inactive | Active | - |
| 28 | Use encoder offset from the reference measurement | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | PLL U_model enable | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | PLL U_model high switch-on level | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | MDS changeover (with motor) | Enable | Inhibit | - |

**Note:**
  
The enable signal of offset monitoring (including offset determination) in p6902 and
following is logically AND'ed with bits 24 ... 26.  
The enable signal in bit 24 ... 26 takes place with a time delay of approx. 30 s after
the drive has been switched off (pulses are inhibited, circuit breaker and disconnector
are open, motor is de-excited, motor speed is below the standstill detection in p1226).  
Offset monitoring (including offset determination) is deactivated in the operating
modes (p6265) "Manual operation", "Semi-manual operation" and "Circulating operation"
(bits 24 ... 26 = 0).  
When offset monitoring is enabled (including offset determination) of the motor voltage
actual values bit 25 =1, the EMF offset control is deactivated (bits 0, 1 = 0).

| 09 | Pulses MSC 1 enabled | No | Yes | - |

| 17 | Flux controller is enabled | No | Yes | - |

| 20 | Test operation active | Inactive | Active | - |
| 21 | Test operation rotor position sensing completed | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | Manual control active | Inactive | Active | - |
| 23 | Offset angle determination, motor winding systems | Inactive | Active | - |
| 24 | Switching on inhibited active | Inactive | Active | - |

### p6264[0...n] Motor sequence control configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0011 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the motor sequence control.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Starting with AC quantity model up to n_min | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Reversing with AC quantity model | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Hold speed controller for excitation current deviation | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Hold speed controller for line undervoltage | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Hold speed controller for line undervoltage with time limit | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
p6270  
  
Refer to:
p6270

**Note:**
  
For bit 3, 4:  
For at least one active alarm A06105, A06205 or A06302 of the line monitoring, the
pulses of the LSC are inhibited.  
Bit 3 = 1, bit 4 = x:  
The integral component of the speed controller is kept for the duration of the active
alarms.  
Bit 3 = 0, bit 4 = 1:  
As above. If alarms are active for longer than 1 s, then the speed controller is inhibited
and its integral component deleted.  
Bit 3 = 0, bit 4 = 0:  
Speed controller is inhibited for the duration of the active alarms and its integral
component deleted.

### p6265 Motor sequence control test operations

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9824 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 110 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode for test operation for the motor sequence control.  
The selected mode determines the sequence when powering up.  
In each of these modes, the ON command in control word 1 bit 0 (r0898.0) is used to
switch on and the operating enable in control word 1 bit 3 (r0898.3).  
For p6265 = 0 - "Normal operation" mode:  
The auxiliaries and the excitation equipment are switched on and existing circuit
breakers (p6230) are closed.  
After the motor excitation has been built up and rotor position sensing, the converters
are enabled.  
For p6265 = 1 - "Rot pos sensing" mode:  
The excitation equipment is switched on.  
Corresponding to the number in p6240, the motor excitation is built up and the rotor
located. The converter pulses are not enabled.  
For p6265 = 2 - "Current controlled operation" mode:  
The auxiliaries are switched on and existing circuit breakers (p6230) are closed.  
The motor is not excited. The converter pulses are enabled and DC link clocking forced
(p6260).  
For p6265 = 3 - "Line-side converter offset angle determination" mode:  
The auxiliaries are switched on and existing incoming circuit breakers (p6230) are
closed.  
The motor is not excited. The offset angle of the converter is measured in the state
of the sequence control "determination offset angle of the line-side converter" in
r3402. The measured offset angle is displayed in r6068.  
For p6265 = 4 - "Motor-side converter offset angle determination" mode:  
The auxiliaries and the excitation equipment are switched on and existing circuit
breakers (p6230) are closed.  
After the motor excitation has been built up and rotor position sensing, only the
converters in subsystem 1 are enabled.  
With a sufficiently high motor voltage and frequency, offset angle determination is
enabled between the winding systems. During the measurement, the pulses of the converter
in subsystem 1 are inhibited. If the motor voltage and frequency are not high enough,
the measurement is interrupted and the pulses in the subsystem are re-enabled in order
to re-accelerate the motor. With a sufficiently high motor voltage and frequency,
the operation is re-started (prerequisite: drive system with 2 windings). The absolute
minimum speed for determining the offset angle on the motor side is displayed in parameter
r6065 and should be used in the fixed setpoint p1001 for the measuring operation.
The measured offset angle is displayed in r6068[0].  
For p6265 = 5 - "Line-side converter offset angle determination with premagnetization":  
The auxiliaries are switched on and if available, also the input transformer premagnetization.
Any existing circuit breakers (p6230) are not closed.  
For p6265 = 7 - "Internal demagnetization" mode:  
The auxiliaries are switched on and existing circuit breakers (p6230) are closed.  
The motor is not excited. The converter pulses are enabled (p6260) and the internal
demagnetization operation (p6009) is started for the converter.  
For p6265 = 10 - "Circulating operation" mode:  
The auxiliaries are switched on and existing circuit breakers (p6230) are closed.
After the motor PLL has synchronized, the converter pulses are enabled. The drive
converter is connected to the line supply via the terminals on the output side.  
Note:  
With the setting "closed-loop current controlled operation" p6350 = 3 and p6353 =
20.0 % are pre-assigned.  
For p6265 = 20, 21 - "Operation with subsystem 1, 2" mode:  
The auxiliaries and the excitation equipment are switched on and existing circuit
breakers (p6230) are closed.  
After the motor excitation has been built up and rotor position sensing, only the
converters in the selected subsystem are enabled.  
With "Subsystem 1" selected, when the motor voltage and frequency are sufficiently
high, then the offset angle determination is enabled between the winding systems.
During the measurement, the pulses of the converter in subsystem 1 remain enabled
(precondition: drive system with 2 windings).  
For p6265 = 100 "Partially manual operation" mode:  
The auxiliaries and the excitation equipment are switched on and existing circuit
breakers (p6230) are closed.  
p6266 is used to control the converter enable signals.  
For p6265 = 110 "Partially manual operation" mode:  
The auxiliaries and excitation equipment are not switched on and the existing circuit
breaker (p6230) is not closed.  
p6266 is used to control the converter enable signals.

**Value:**
  
0:
Normal operation  
1:
Rotor position sensing  
2:
Closed-loop current controlled operation  
3:
Line-side converter offset angle determination  
4:
Motor-side converter offset angle determination  
5:
Line-side converter offset angle determ. with premagnetization  
7:
De-magnetizing internal  
10:
Circulating operation  
20:
Operation with subsystem 1  
21:
Operation with subsystem 2  
100:
Semi-manual operation  
110:
Manual operation

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6230, p6240, p6266, p6350, p6353  
Refer to:
A07318

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the test operation.

**Note:**
  
The parameter can only be changed in the state "switching on inhibited" or "ready
for switching on" of the sequence control (r3402).  
p6350 is pre-assigned 0 with the setting "normal operation", "rotor position sensing"
or "line offset angle determination".  
p6350 is pre-assigned 3 with the setting "current-controlled operation" or "circulating
operation".

### p6266 Motor sequence control manual operation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 30000 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the pulse enable for "manual operation" mode of the motor sequence control.

**Value:**
  
0:
No pulse enable  
1:
Pulse enable LSC 1  
2:
Pulse enable MSC 1  
3:
Pulse enable LSC 1 and MSC 1  
10:
Pulse enable LSC 2  
20:
Pulse enable MSC 2  
30:
Pulse enable LSC 2 and MSC 2  
99:
Pulse enable excitation equipment  
10000:
Pulse enable all LSC  
20000:
Pulse enable all MSC  
30000:
Pulse enable all LSC and MSC

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6265

**Caution:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
manual operation.

### p6267[0...1] Manual operation with 1st and 2nd pulse

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets long/short pulses in "manual operation" mode of the motor sequence control.  
The short pulse duration (= operation with 1st and 2nd pulse) is specified in p6340
and/or p6359.  
The long pulse duration is permanently set to 120 ° electrical.

**Value:**
  
0:
Operation with long pulses  
1:
Operation with 1st and 2nd short pulses

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Line side  
[
1]:
Motor side

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6265, p6266, p6340, p6359

**Caution:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
manual operation.

**Note:**
  
The parameter can only be changed for the setting p6265 = manual or semi-manual operation
and when the pulses are inhibited. When manual modes are de-selected in p6265, the
mode with long pulses is entered in this parameter.  
  
In the motor-side converter, operation with 1st and 2nd short pulse setting, only
the 1st short pulse is generated.

### p6268 Meas pulse DA

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
In topologies with a common PSA for the LSC and MSC, gating unit sector 1 (= firing
command 1) of the LSC or the MSC, which is to be output at DO 1 (or DO 5), can be
selected in this parameter.

**Value:**
  
0:
Output pulse 1 from LSC  
1:
Output pulse 1 from MSC

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6265, p6266, p6340, p6359

**Note:**
  
In topologies with one PSA each for the LSC and MSC, this parameter cannot be written
to. In this case, gating unit sector 1 of the LSC or MSC is output at the particular
PSA.  
Gating unit sector 1 of the selected converter is only output at PSA DO 1 if no CB
has been set on the motor side (see p6230).  
Gating unit sector 1 of the selected converter is only output at PSA DO 5 if there
is no internal output disconnector (see r6022).  
The status word of the PSA DI/DO is indicated in parameter r6580.

### p6270[0...n] Motor closed-loop control minimum frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_ALL | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [Hz] | 15.0 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the minimum electrical frequency of the motor control.  
If the minimum frequency is undershot for a longer time than that set in p6245 an
appropriate fault is output.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6245, p6264, p6271, p6280  
Refer to:
F49911

**Note:**
  
The minimum frequency also has an influence on the activation of AC and DC quantity
models of the motor closed-loop control (p6280, p6264).

### p6274[0...n] Rotor position sensing with AC quantity model, max. frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_ALL | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [Hz] | 30.0 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 7.5 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the electrical motor frequency up to which the rotor position is sensed using
the AC quantity model in order to determine the offset angle of the encoder.  
Above this frequency, the offset is determined in the DC quantity model.  
The offset angle is the difference from the electrical pole wheel position and the
orientation angle of the model.

**Note:**
  
The setting is only effective during operation with an encoder (p1300 =21, 23).

### p6278[0...n] Reverse field excitation speed setp. rot. field inv. hysteresis

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -20000.00 [rpm] | 20000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 10.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the hysteresis of the speed setpoint for rotating field inversion of the stator
current in the reverse field exciter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6277

**Note:**
  
The amount of the value entered in the parameter is dynamically limited to the rated
speed of the motor.  
The value 0 is not permissible.  
The sign of the hysteresis defines the rotating field of the stator current for the
reverse field exciter depending on the mechanical direction of rotation.  
The hysteresis is symmetrical around the value in parameter p6277.

### r6279.0...15 CO/BO: Flux monitoring and speed monitoring state

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the flux monitoring and speed monitoring.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Minimum speed exceeded | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Load commutation operation speed exceeded | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Speed sensing active | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Speed threshold stop reached | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Motor model changeover speed encoderless operation exceeded | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Speed controller, changeover speed encoderless oper. exceeded | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Torque-generating current setpoint not equal to zero | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Torque-generating current setpoint is positive | Negative | Positive | - |
| 08 | AC quantity model flux threshold exceeded | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Flux threshold build-up excitation exceeded | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | DC quantity model flux threshold exceeded | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Converter operation flux threshold exceeded | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Flux actual value in the tolerance bandwidth | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Mech. direction of rotation from reverse field excitation | Turns anti-clockw. | Turns clockwise | - |
| 14 | Torque-generating abs current setpoint value less than threshold | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Torque-generating Iset * f is positive | Negative | Positive | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1404, p1755, p1756, p6270, p6271, p6272, p6273, p6276, p6280, p6281, p6282

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
The threshold value is set in p6270.  
For bit 01:  
The threshold value is set in p6272.  
For bit 03:  
The threshold value is set in p1226.  
For bit 04:  
The threshold value is set in p1752.  
For bit 05:  
The threshold value is set in p1404.  
For bit 07, 14:  
The threshold value is set in p6276.  
For bit 08:  
The threshold value is set in p6280.  
For bit 09:  
The threshold value is set in p6281.  
For bit 10:  
The threshold value is set in p6282.  
For bit 11:  
The threshold value is set in p6283.  
For bit 13:  
The threshold value is set in p6278.

### p6280[0...n] Rotor position sensing flux threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 50.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the flux threshold when building up motor excitation.  
If the absolute value of the flux determined is below this threshold, then the AC
quantity model of the motor closed-loop control is activated.  
Above this threshold, based on a motor frequency detected above the minimum frequency
in parameter p6270, the DC quantity model of the motor closed-loop control is activated.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6270, p6271

**Note:**
  
If the motor frequency is detected prior to excitation build-up, for example, using
a residual tachometer or speed encoder, and this is higher than the minimum frequency,
then the DC quantity model of the motor closed-loop control is activated.

### p6281[0...n] Build-up excitation flux threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 90.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the flux threshold that must be achieved as a minimum when building up excitation.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6264

**Note:**
  
Depending on the setting in p6264, the DC quantity model of the motor closed-loop
control will be activated when this flux threshold is reached.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
