**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the flux threshold after build-up of excitation above which the DC quantity voltage
model is switched in.

### p6283[0...n] Converter operation flux difference

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 20.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible flux setpoint/actual value difference in converter operation.  
If the difference is greater than that set, then the converter is inhibited and an
appropriate alarm is output.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6284  
Refer to:
A49912

### p6286 CO: Excitation current setpoint for test operation

|  |  |  |

### p6290[0...n] Turn mode activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6330 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate the turn mode.  
The turn mode can only be activated if the flux reduction is deactivated in regenerative
operation (p6300).

**Value:**
  
0:
Turn mode deactivated  
1:
Turn mode activated

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6300

### p6291[0...n] Turn mode maximum time with rated flux

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6330 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 100.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 25.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum time with rated flux in the turn mode.  
For thermal reasons, in the turn mode, the motor may only be operated with rated flux
for a specific time. After this, the flux must be reduced.

### p6292[0...n] Turn mode cooling-down time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6330 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 86400.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 60.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the cooling-down time in the turn mode.  
For thermal reasons, after withdrawing "motor sequence control on" (r6221.7 = 1/0)
the machine may only be again operated with rated flux after this time has expired.

### p6293[0...n] CO: Turn mode flux setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6330 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**PMSM, REL | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10.0 [%] | 200.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 30.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the flux setpoint for the turn mode.  
The value is referred to the rated motor flux.

### p6294[0...n] Turn mode flux setpoint ramp time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6330 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.10 [s] | 50.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the ramp time for the flux setpoint in the turn mode.  
When a start is made to reduce the flux, the setpoint is ramped from the actual value
to the value in p6293 within this time.  
When the flux reduction is reset, the flux setpoint is again ramped to the rated flux
within this time.

### r6295.0...3 CO/BO: Turn mode status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6330 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status word for the turn mode.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Turn mode active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Flux reduction active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Minimum speed for flux reduction reached | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Operation with rated flux inhibited | No | Yes | - |

### p6298 Cooling unit differential pressure min value alarm thresh

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit), VECTORSL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [bar] | 10.0 [bar] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [bar] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold for the minimum differential pressure.

**Note:**
  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
Alarm threshold > fault threshold (hardware description data)  
If the alarm threshold is set lower than the fault threshold, then the value is limited
to 0.2 bar above the fault threshold.  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the hardware description
data.

### p6299[0...1] Cooling unit intake temperature maximum value alarm threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 20.0 [°C] | 100.0 [°C] | [ 0 ] 40.0 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold for the maximum intake temperature for liquid cooling or
intake temperature for air cooling.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Temperature module stack  
[
1]:
Temperature DC link reactor

**Note:**
  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
Alarm threshold < fault threshold (hardware description data)  
If the alarm threshold is set higher than the fault threshold, then the value is limited
to 2 K below the fault threshold.  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the hardware description
data.

### p6301[0...n] CO: EMF limiting maximum EMF in regenerative mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6725 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

Sets the maximum EMF for the EMF limiting in the regenerative mode.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6302

**Note:**
  
The parameter is pre-assigned to the drive converter voltage when commissioning the
motor (from the hardware description).  
If there is a transformer (p0240, p0241), then the parameter value always refers to
the drive converter output voltage.

### p6302[0...n] EMF limiting flux tolerance bandwidth

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_ALL | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 15.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the tolerance bandwidth for the flux reduction in regenerative mode.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0084, r1598, r6279, p6301

**Note:**
  
If the absolute value of the difference between the flux setpoint (r1598) and flux
actual value (r0084) lies within this tolerance bandwidth, then r6279.12 is set to
1.

### r6310[0...n] CO: Line frequency PLL smoothed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** 6799 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p0514 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Hz] | - [Hz] | [ ] - [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the smoothed PLL line frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6311

**Notice:**
  
After a line supply failure or failure of the 24 V supply of the module, the display
is frozen at the last permissible value.  
If the parameter is used for a BICO interconnection, scaling to the rated line frequency
(p0211) is carried out.

**Note:**
  
Smoothing time constant = 300 ms  
The signal is not suitable as a process quantity and may only be used as a display
quantity.  
The line frequency is available smoothed (r6310) and unsmoothed (r6311).  
A positive sign of the frequency is obtained when the line supply phases U, V and
W are connected with the correct phase sequence.  
A negative sign of the frequency is obtained when the 3 line phases are interchanged
therefore designating a negative direction of the rotating field of the 3-phase line
supply voltage.

r6310

is frozen at the last permissible value.

(p0211) is carried out.

**Note:**
  
The line frequency is available smoothed (r6310) and unsmoothed (r6311).  
A positive sign of the frequency is obtained when the line supply phases U, V and
W are connected with the correct phase sequence.  
A negative sign of the frequency is obtained when the 3 line phases are interchanged
therefore designating a negative direction of the rotating field of the 3-phase line
supply voltage.

### r6312[0...n] CO: Line supply voltage PLL slightly smoothed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** 6740, 6799, 8026 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**5_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [Vrms] | - [Vrms] | [ ] - [Vrms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the slightly smoothed actual value of the PLL line
voltage.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6313, r6315, r6433

**Notice:**
  
After failure of the 24 V supply of the module, the display is frozen at the last
permissible value.

**Note:**
  
The line supply voltage is displayed in the following parameters:  
r6312 - smoothed using an average value filter (depending on the number of pulses)
or bandstop filter with twice the line frequency (slightly smoothed)  
r6313 - smoothed using an average value with twice the line frequency  
r6315 - measured

r6312 - smoothed using an average value filter (depending on the number of pulses)
or bandstop filter with twice the line frequency (slightly smoothed)  
r6313 - smoothed using an average value with twice the line frequency  
r6315 - measured

### p6318 Line voltage sensing selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to select the line voltage sensing.

**Value:**
  
0:
VSM in front of the line-side circuit breaker  
1:
VSM after the line-side circuit breaker  
2:
PSA at the terminals of the line converter  
3:
VSM at the terminals of the line-side converter

**Note:**
  
For p6318 = 1, 2, 3, the following applies:  
The PLL starts after detecting r3402 = 32 "circuit breaker closed".  
The setting p6318 = 3 is only permissible for a rated voltage less than 690 V (r0208
<= 690 V).

### p6329 Current controller DC link inductance scaling

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 200.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the scaling of the effective DC link reactor inductance calculated from the configuration
data.  
After the parameter is changed, the control parameters of the current controller are
automatically calculated. In so doing, the parameters, dependent on the inductance
of the DC link reactor and coupling factor, are recalculated.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1702, p1715, p1717, p6339, p6345

**Note:**
  
The D gain of the current controller precontrol is obtained during the machine control
from p1709 and during the DC link pulsing from p1702.

### p6333 LSC inverter limiting thyristor hold-off time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6740 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [ms] | 4.000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.500 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the thyristor hold-off time of the line-side converter (LSC).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6335

**Note:**
  
The extinction angle is determined as follows from the thyristor hold-off time:  
Extinction angle = 2 * Pi * line frequency * thyristor hold-off time

### p6334[0...n] LSC inverter limiting L_commutation uncoupled

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6740 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 12.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the uncoupled commutation inductance for the line-side converter (LSC).

**Recommend.:**
  
The uncoupled commutation inductance corresponds to the transformer short-circuit
reactance of the converter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6335, p6344

**Notice:**
  
Zuk scaling is carried out dependent upon the supply voltage Uver (p0210) and the
apparent power S of a converter subsystem.  
For other forms of scaling, the commutation inductances should be converted as follows.  
Zuk = (Uver * Uver) / S = p0210 / (root(2) * p0207)

**Note:**
  
The overlap angle is calculated from the total commutation inductance uk.

### r6335[0...n] CO: LSC inverter limiting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** 6740, 6750 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Modulation | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2005 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°] | - [°] | [ ] - [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the inverter limiting for the line-side converter (LSC).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6333, p6334, p6337

**Note:**
  
The inverter limiting corresponds to the calculated inverter stability limit.  
The inverter stability limit is obtained from the extinction angle and the overlap
angle.  
Inverter stability limit = 180 - extinction angle - overload angle

### p6336[0...1] LSC firing angle change limiting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6741 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10.00 [°] | 90.00 [°] | [ 0 ] 30.00 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the limit of the firing angle change between two commutations for the line-side
converter (LSC) in normal operation (index 0) and when the motor is operated with
just one winding system (index 1).  
Example:  
For 6-pulse systems, the time between two commutations is 3.3 ms.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Normal operation, 6 or 12-pulse  
[
1]:
Motor operated with just one winding system (HM) 6-pulse

### p6338[0...1] Current controller absolute line voltage value limiting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6744 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10.00 [%] | 140.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 40.00 [%]  [ 1 ] 120.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the limits for the absolute line supply voltage absolute value when calculating
the modulation depth.  
Index 0: Lower limit  
Index 1: Upper limit  
The limits are deactivated when function "eLVRT" (p3486.0 = 1) is active.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6324

**Note:**
  
eLVRT: Enhanced low voltage ride through (uninterruptible operation without pulse
inhibit during voltage dips in the line supply)  
The modulation depth is calculated from the product of the current controller output
and the referred absolute line supply voltage. The factor for the absolute line supply
voltage is pre-assigned to 1 by setting the lower and upper limit to 100.

### p6339[0...2] Intermittent current adaptation controller

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6741 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 110.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 10.00 [%]  [ 1 ] 90.00 [%]  [ 2 ] 10.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the intermittent current limit and scaling for the intermittent current adaptation.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Intermittent current limit  
[
1]:
Scaling  
[
2]:
Intermit curr limit, motor op with just one winding system (HM)

**Note:**
  
For p6339[0]:  
The value is calculated and automatically pre-set from the configuration data and
the scaling of the DC link inductance (p6328).  
The highest value that can be set is 50 %.  
Scaling is made to the rated DC link current.  
The starting point of the current-dependent current controller adaptation is also
set using the intermittent current limit where the adapted current controller integral
time (p1717 x p6343[0]) is effective.

| 1.50 [°] | 59.00 [°] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [°] |

2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the length of the firing commands for second pulse cancellation for the line-side
converter (LSC).

### p6342[0...3] Intermittent current current limits adaptation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_CON | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6741 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [%] | 50.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 2.000 [%]  [ 1 ] 4.000 [%]  [ 2 ] 0.500 [%]  [ 3 ] 0.500 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the current limits for the adaptation of the current controller in the intermittent
current range.  
p6342[0]:  
Sets the lower starting point of the current-dependent current controller adaptation.
From this point onwards, the adapted current controller integral time p1717 * p6343[0]
is effective.  
p6342[1]:  
Sets the hysteresis for the upper intervention point (intermittent current limit,
p6339) of the current-dependent current controller adaptation. Up to this point, the
adapted current controller integral time p1717 * p6343[1] is effective.  
p6342[2]:  
Sets the lowest current setpoint realized by the current controller.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Intervention point, lower  
[
1]:
Intervention point, upper hysteresis  
[
2]:
Current setpoint, lower limit  
[
3]:
Current setpoint, lower limit, semi motor operation

**Note:**
  
For p6342[0, 1]:  
Scaling is made to the intermittent current limit (p6339).  
For p6342[2]:  
Scaling is made to the rated DC link current (p0207).  
The value is calculated from the intermittent current limit (p6339) and automatically
pre-set.

### p6343[0...1] Intermittent current Tn adaptation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6741 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.010 [%] | 100.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 1.000 [%]  [ 1 ] 50.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the Tn adaptation at the intervention points of the current controller in the
intermittent current range.  
p6343[0]:  
Sets the factor for the integral time for the lower intervention point of the current-dependent
current controller adaptation (p6342[0]). From the lower invention point onwards,
the adapted current controller integral time p1717 * p6343[0] is effective.  
p6343[1]:  
Sets the factor for the integral time for the upper intervention point of the current-dependent
current controller adaptation (p6339]). From the upper invention point onwards, the
adapted current controller integral time p1717 * p6343[1] is effective.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Tn adaptation lower  
[
1]:
Tn adaptation upper

**Note:**
  
With p6343[0, 1] = 100 % the current controller adaptation is disabled in the intermittent
current range

### p6344[0...n] LSC inverter limiting L_commutation coupled

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6740 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the coupled commutation inductance for the line-side converter (LSC).

**Recommend.:**
  
The coupled commutation inductance corresponds to the short-circuit reactance of the
line.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6334, r6335

**Notice:**
  
Making an entry generates the calculation of the inverter stability limit for coupled
systems.  
Zuk scaling is carried out dependent upon the supply voltage Uver (p0210) and the
apparent power S of a converter subsystem. For other forms of scaling, the commutation
inductances should be converted as follows.  
Zuk = (Uver * Uver) / S = p0210 / (root(2) * p0207)

**Note:**
  
The overlap angle is calculated from the total commutation inductance uk.

### p6345 Current controller DC link inductance machine control scal

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 200.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the scaling of the effective DC link reactor inductance calculated from the configuration
data during machine control.  
After the parameter is changed, the control parameters of the current controller are
automatically calculated. In so doing, the parameters, dependent on the inductance
of the DC link reactor and coupling factor, are recalculated.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p1702, p1715, p1717, p6329, p6339

**Note:**
  
The D gain of the current controller precontrol is obtained during the machine control
from p1709 and during the DC link pulsing from p1702.  
For p6345 = 0, p1702 remains active over the complete operating range.

### p6346[0...n] LSC rotating field direction

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the rotating field direction to the three-phase voltage systems present at the
input-side converters (LSC).

**Recommend.:**
  
The "Determination of offset angle line-side converter" mode (p6265 = 3) can be activated
to check the rotating field direction and offset angle.

**Value:**
  
0:
Rotating field direction positive  
1:
Rotating field negative

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may set and use this
parameter.  
If the setting in this parameter is changed, then a new offset angle (p6067) between
the reference system and the voltage available at the converters must be determined.

**Note:**
  
It may be necessary to select the direction of rotation when interchanging the phases
between the transformer secondary to the converters or at the primary of the transformer.  
The reference system available at the Voltage Sensing Module (VSM) must always rotate
in the clockwise direction.

### p6347[0...n] Current controller precontrol P gain MSC scaling

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6741 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**REL | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 200.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the proportional gain of the current controller precontrol to compensate the
line-side converter commutation.  
Using the value, the DC voltage change of the motor-side converter, calculated from
the motor data, is scaled.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0356, p0357, p0358, p0359, p0360, p1705

### p6350 LSC manual operation mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 5 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to manual operation.  
For p6350 = 1:  
The line PLL including the line voltage monitoring is disabled.  
The gating units of the LSC and MSC run freely.  
- setting the frequency and firing angle via p6351 or p6352 (LSC).  
- setting the frequency and firing angle via p6285 or p6354 (MSC).  
For p6350 = 2:  
The line PLL is activated. The firing angle should be entered via p6352 or CI p17494.  
The motor PLL activates itself when voltage is connected to the terminals of the MSC.  
The firing angle should be entered via p6354.  
For p6350 = 3:  
The line PLL is activated. The current setpoint should be entered via p6353 or CI
17485.  
For p6350 = 4:  
The line PLL is activated. Open-loop controlled operation with modulation depth via
CI p17490.  
For p6350 = 5:  
The line PLL is activated. Closed-loop controlled operation (e.g. in LSC1) with current
setpoint via CI p17485 and open-loop controlled operation (e.g. in LSC2) with modulation
depth via CI p17490.

**Value:**
  
0:
Manual operation off  
1:
Open-loop controlled operation without synchronizing voltage  
2:
Open-loop controlled operation with synchronizing voltage  
3:
Closed-loop current controlled operation  
4:
Open-loop controlled operation via CI  
5:
Closed-loop/open-loop controlled operation via CI

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6265, p6351, p6352, p6353, p17485

**Note:**
  
p6353 is preassigned 0 % in all modes.  
For p6265 = 0 (normal operation), the following applies:  
Here, a selection must be made between the mode "manual operation off" and "current
controlled operation" (p6350 = 0, 3).  
Here, the operating mode "manual operation off" is automatically set (p6350 = 0).  
For p6265 = 2 (current controlled operation), 10 (circulating operation) the following
applies:  
Here, the "current controlled operation" mode is automatically set (p6350 = 3).  
For p6265 = 100, 110 (manual operation), the following applies:  
In this case, one of the following modes should be selected: "open-loop controlled
operation with synchronizing voltage", "open-loop controlled operation without synchronizing
voltage" or "current controlled operation" (p6350 = 1, 2, 3, 4 or 5).  
For p6265 = 1 (rotor position sensing), 3 (determining the offset angle line-side
converter) the following applies:  
Here, the operating mode "manual operation off" is automatically set (p6350 = 0).

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

into account the set limit (p6365).  
The inverter stability limit is obtained from the extinction angle and the overlap
angle.  
Inverter stability limit = 180 - extinction angle - overload angle

### p6366 MSC inverter limiting thyristor hold-off time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6718 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [ms] | 4.000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.500 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the thyristor hold-off time for the motor-side converter (MSC).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6361

**Note:**
  
The extinction angle is determined as follows from the thyristor hold-off time.  
Extinction angle = 2 * Pi * output frequency * thyristor hold-off time

### p6368[0...n] MSC correction firing angle at low speeds

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 6718 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2005 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [°] | 40.00 [°] | [ 0 ] 15.00 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting of the correction angle, effective at the changeover speed, to compensate
the voltage drive across the resistors for low EMF.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6272, p6369

**Note:**
  
The correction angle is reduced along a linear characteristic with increasing speed.

|  |  |  |

Sets the number of values via which the EMF is averaged.

### p6380[0...n] Voltage model PLL dynamic factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 [%] | 150 [%] | [ 0 ] 50 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the dynamic factor of the PLL to determine the speed while the pulses are inhibited.

**Note:**
  
A high dynamic factor increases the tendency to oscillate and can even cause instability.  
PLL: Phase-Locked Loop

### p6383[0...n] Voltage model PLL angular deviation permissible

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [°] | 180.0 [°] | [ 0 ] 20.0 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible angular deviation for operation with an encoder.  
The encoder actual value is corrected if the absolute value of the angular deviation
between the voltage model and the encoder lies outside the specified value for the
duration specified in p6384.  
If the absolute value of the angular deviation of the encoder signal in encoder operation
lies outside the specified value, then the closed-loop control and therefore the frequency
are held.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p6384  
Refer to:
A30231

**Note:**
  
For encoders with a zero mark and activated correction of the pulse number (p0437.2
= 1) the angular deviation monitoring can be deactivated using p6383 = 0.0.

### p6384[0...n] Voltage model PLL angular deviation permissible timer

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 | 1000 | [ 0 ] 3 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the number of sampling values for which the angular deviation may be greater
than the permissible threshold value (p6383).  
After this number has been exceeded, the encoder actual value is corrected or the
PLL control loop is re-synchronized.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6383  
Refer to:
A30231

r0210

### p6390[0...n] Voltage model AC integrator time constant

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 [s] | 100000 [s] | [ 0 ] 1000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the time constant of the integrator for the AC quantity voltage model to sense
the rotor position of the synchronous motor.  
In so doing, to avoid open integrators, 1st order delay elements are used with the
specified time constant.  
The integration is better approached with increasing time constant.  
The stability is higher at lower time constant.

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.5 [ms] | 1000.0 [ms] | [ 0 ] 5.0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the filter time constant for the inverter quantity voltage model to sense the
rotor position of the synchronous motor.

### p6393[0...n] Voltage model DC damping factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 | 5.0 | [ 0 ] 1.0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the damping factor for the DC quantity voltage model.  
In so doing, the change of flux with respect to time scaled with the damping factor
is applied to the EMF. The damping effect decreases with increasing value.

### p6394[0...n] Voltage model DC frequency deviation permissible

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1.0 [%] | 250.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 50.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible deviation of the frequency calculated from the DC quantity voltage
model to the filtered value.  
The specified limit value is referred to the rated motor frequency.  
If the absolute value of the deviation lies outside the specified value, then the
closed-loop control and therefore the frequency are held.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F30232

### p6395[0...n] Voltage model DC frequency deviation timer

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**CALC_MOD_REG | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.01 [s] | 10.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.10 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the timer during which deviations between the DC quantity voltage model and the
filtered frequency may be present for longer than the permissible threshold value
(p6394).  
After this time is exceeded, fault F30232 is output.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6394  
Refer to:
F30232

### p6396[0...n] MSC rotating field direction

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the rotating field direction to the three-phase voltage systems present at the
output-side converters (MSC).

**Recommend.:**
  
The "Determination of offset angle motor-side converter" mode (p6265 = 4) can be activated
to check the rotating field direction and offset angle.

**Value:**
  
0:
Rotating field direction positive  
1:
Rotating field negative

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may set and use this
parameter.  
After changing this parameter, the phase offset (p6397) between the motor converters
must be redetermined.

**Note:**
  
It may be necessary to select the direction of rotation in the following cases:  
- counterclockwise rotating motor shaft with a clockwise rotating reference system
(--> p6396 = 1 for all MSC).  
- interchanged phase in the cables of an output converter to the motor (--> p6396
= 1 in the MSC where the phase has been interchanged).  
The reference system is determined from the measured output voltages in MSC1.  
The rotating field direction of the encoder signals must match that of the voltage
system at MSC1.  
Otherwise, track A/B of the encoder must be interchanged at the Sensor Module.

### p6397[0...n] MSC phase shift second system

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 9 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the phase shift of the second system with respect to the first system for MSC
(motor-side converter)

**Value:**
  
0:
Shift by +30 °  
1:
Shift by -30 °  
2:
Shift by 0 °  
3:
Shift by +90 °  
4:
Shift by -90 °  
5:
Shift by +120 °  
6:
Shift by -120 °  
7:
Shift by +150 °  
8:
Shift by -150 °  
9:
Shift by 180 °

**Note:**
  
For p6397 = 0, 3, 5, 7 the following applies: The second systems lags for a clockwise
rotating field.  
For p6397 = 1, 4, 6, 8 the following applies: The second systems leads for a clockwise
rotating field.

### p6399 Motor maximum voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8024 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1.0 | 3.0 | [ 0 ] 1.5 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum motor voltage.  
The value is referred to the rated motor voltage (p0304).  
The threshold for the output voltage monitoring is formed as follows:  
Lower value from p0304 x p6399 (after commissioning) and the hardware description
data (r0208, "Permissible MSC overvoltage") on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0208, p0304  
Refer to:
F30002

### p6408[0...n] MSC current actual value sensing motor inductance correction

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 300.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Without motor-side actual value current sensing, the output-side phase currents (r0069)
and their derivation (= current change) are calculated from the DC link current or
the input-side phase currents.  
The motor inductance determines the current change while commutating in the motor-side
converter.  
The factor set here weights the motor inductance in this calculation.  
The motor inductance is determined by setting parameter p0353 and the following.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6356, p6781

**Note:**
  
Motor-side current actual value sensing has to be de-selected (p6781.2 = 0) in order
for the parameter to be effective.

### p6409[0...n] MSC curr actual value sensing short-time measurement correction

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 300.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Without motor-side actual value current sensing, the output-side phase currents (r0069)
and their derivation (= current change) are calculated from the DC link current or
the input-side phase currents.  
Outside commutation, in the motor-side converter, the current change is emulated from
the difference of 2 short-time measuring intervals.  
The difference can be weighted using this correction factor.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6781

**Note:**
  
Motor-side actual value sensing has to be de-selected (p6781.2 = 0) in order for the
parameter to be effective.  
When selecting the input-side current actual value sensing (p6781.0 = 1) it may be
necessary to set the correction factor to less than 100 %.

### p6500[0...87] BI: Transf_monit messages, signal source

|  |  |  |

only be executed in full if the delay time setting for OFF1 (p3490) is greater than
the ramp-down time for the drive.

### r6543 HW-DESC Version CF

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the version of the hardware description data on the CompactFlash Card.  
The version is displayed in the form MAMIVA (major|minor|version, example: 010305).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6545, r6547  
Refer to:
F01275, A01276

### r6545[0...37] HW-DESC GUID CF

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the GUID of the hardware description data on the CompactFlash Card.  
The individual characters of the GUID are displayed as ASCII code in the form {XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX}
in the indices.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F01275

**Note:**
  
An ASCII table (excerpt) can be found, for example, in the appendix to the List Manual.  
GUID: Globally Unique IDentifier

### p6550[0...n] I2t motor monitoring activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate the I2t motor monitoring function.  
0: I2t motor monitoring function deactivated.  
1: I2t motor monitoring function activated.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0034, p0604, p0605, p0606, p6551, p6552, p6553, p6554, p6555  
Refer to:
A07972, F07973

**Notice:**
  
The output of the alarm or fault for the I2t motor monitoring must be activated (p6551,
p6552).  
The values for p6553, p6554 and p6555 must be appropriately adapted before activating
the monitoring function of the system, corresponding to the actual situation.

### p6551[0...n] I2t alarm output activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate the alarm output for the I2t motor monitoring.  
When the selected alarm threshold is exceeded, the appropriate alarm is output.  
0: Alarm output deactivated.  
1: Alarm output activated.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0604, p6550, p6553, p6554, p6555  
Refer to:
A07972

**Notice:**
  
The values for p6553, p6554 and p6555 must be appropriately adapted before activating
the monitoring function of the system, corresponding to the actual situation.

### p6552[0...n] I2t fault output activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate the fault output for the I2t motor monitoring.  
When the selected fault threshold is exceeded or after the timer has expired for the
alarm threshold, the appropriate fault is output.  
0: Fault output deactivated  
1: Fault output activated

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0604, p0605, p0606, p6550, p6553, p6554, p6555  
Refer to:
F07973

**Notice:**
  
The values for p6553, p6554 and p6555 must be appropriately adapted before activating
the monitoring function of the system, corresponding to the actual situation.

### p6553[0...n] I2t motor monitoring thermal time constant

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 100 [s] | 10000 [s] | [ 0 ] 1000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the thermal motor time constant for the I2t motor monitoring function.  
Should be assigned 1/4 of the time that the motor requires at the rated voltage, rated
current and rated frequency as well as the ambient temperature to reach 98% of its
rated temperature.

### p6554[0...n] I2t motor monitoring cooling-down constant natural ventilation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 100 [s] | 10000 [s] | [ 0 ] 1000 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the thermal time constant for the I2t motor monitoring when a naturally ventilated
motor cools down.  
Must be assigned 1/4 of the time that the naturally ventilated motor at standstill
requires to cool down from its rated temperature to the ambient temperature.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0335

### p6555[0...n] I2t motor monitoring f = 0.0 natural ventilation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 8017 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 | 1.000 | [ 0 ] 0.400 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Enters the point of a motor utilization characteristic for the I2t motor monitoring
of a naturally ventilated motor for f = 0.0 * rated motor frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0335

### p6556[0...n] I2t motor monitoring f = 0.5 natural ventilation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

Enters the point of a motor utilization characteristic for the I2t motor monitoring
of a naturally ventilated motor for f = 0.5 * rated motor frequency.

### p6576[0...1] BI: Safety circuit signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the safety circuits.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
EMERGENCY OFF pushbutton feedback signal  
[
1]:
External safety circuit feedback signal

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A30091, F30098

**Note:**
  
For p6576[0] = 0 signal, alarm A30091 "Safety circuit interrupted" is output.  
For p6576[1] = 0 signal, fault F30098 "External safety circuit interrupted" is output.

### p6577[0...29] BI: Circuit monitoring functions signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8032 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for the current monitoring functions.  
BI: p6577[x] = 0 signal  
Protective breaker tripped.  
BI: p6577[x] = 1 signal  
Protective breaker not tripped.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Protective breaker trip main circuit  
[
1]:
Protective breaker trip, main circuit 1  
[
2]:
Protective breaker trip, main circuit 2  
[
3]:
Protective breaker trip internal 24 V circuit  
[
4]:
Protective breaker trip internal 24 V circuit 1  
[
5]:
Protective breaker trip internal 24 V circuit 2  
[
6]:
Protective breaker trip external 24 V circuit  
[
7]:
Protective breaker trip external 24 V circuit 1  
[
8]:
Protective breaker trip external 24 V circuit 2  
[
9]:
Protective breaker trip PU supply 24 V circuit  
[
10]:
Protective breaker trip PU supply 24 V circuit 1  
[
11]:
Protective breaker trip PU supply 24 V circuit 2  
[
12]:
Protective breaker trip PLC 24 V circuit  
[
13]:
Protective breaker trip synchronizing voltage  
[
14]:
Protective breaker trip fan circuit  
[
15]:
Protective breaker trip, synchronizing voltage 1  
[
16]:
Protective breaker trip, synchronizing voltage 2  
[
17...29]:
Not used

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49920, A49921, A49922, A49923, A49924, A49925, A49926, A49927

**Note:**
  
The following applies when interconnecting the signal sources:  
Either index 3 or indices 4 and 5 may be interconnected.  
Either index 6 or indices 7 and 8 may be interconnected.  
Either index 9 or indices 10 and 11 may be interconnected.  
Either index 13 or indices 15 and 16 may be interconnected.

### r6578.0...1 BO: Contactor safety combination status and control word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8044 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status and control words of the contactor safety combination.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Emergency Stop circuit opened | Yes | No | - |
| 01 | External safety circuit is opened | Yes | No | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6576

### r6587.0...19 CO/BO: Circuit monitoring functions status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8032 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the circuit monitoring functions.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Protective breaker trip main circuit | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Protective breaker trip, main circuit 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Protective breaker trip, main circuit 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Protective breaker trip internal 24 V circuit | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Protective breaker trip internal 24 V circuit 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Protective breaker trip internal 24 V circuit 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Protective breaker trip external 24 V circuit | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Protective breaker trip external 24 V circuit 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Protective breaker trip external 24 V circuit 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Protective breaker trip PU supply 24 V circuit | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Protective breaker trip PU supply 24 V circuit 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Protective breaker trip PU supply 24 V circuit 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Protective breaker trip PLC 24 V circuit | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Protective breaker trip synchronizing voltage | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Protective breaker fan circuit | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Message output enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Protective breaker, subsystem 1 tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 17 | Protective breaker subsystem 2 tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 18 | Protective breaker trip, synchronizing voltage 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | Protective breaker trip, synchronizing voltage 2 | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6577

### p6590[0...15] BI: Close enable signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 6509.24   [ 1 ] 6587.16   [ 2 ] 6229.0   [ 3...7 ] 1   [ 8 ] 6509.25   [ 9 ] 6587.17   [ 10 ] 6229.8   [ 11...15 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for the extended close enable signals.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Extended close enable 0  
[
1]:
Extended close enable 1  
[
2]:
Extended close enable 2  
[
3]:
Extended close enable 3  
[
4]:
Extended close enable 4  
[
5]:
Extended close enable 5  
[
6]:
Extended close enable 6  
[
7]:
Extended close enable 7  
[
8]:
Extended close enable 8  
[
9]:
Extended close enable 9  
[
10]:
Extended close enable 10  
[
11]:
Extended close enable 11  
[
12]:
Extended close enable 12  
[
13]:
Extended close enable 13  
[
14]:
Extended close enable 14  
[
15]:
Extended close enable 15

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6591, r6592

**Note:**
  
For drives with redundant power units, index 0 ... 7 is assigned to subsystem 1 and
8 ... 15 to subsystem 2.  
Index 0(8) is interconnected to the status word for transformer monitoring r6509.24(25)
by default.  
Index 1(9) is interconnected to the status word for current monitoring r6587.16(17)
by default.

### p6591 Close enable signal level

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1111 1111 1111 1111 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to evaluate the close enable signals.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Signal level close enable signal 0 | Low | High | - |
| 01 | Signal level close enable signal 1 | Low | High | - |
| 02 | Signal level close enable signal 2 | Low | High | - |
| 03 | Signal level close enable signal 3 | Low | High | - |
| 04 | Signal level close enable signal 4 | Low | High | - |
| 05 | Signal level close enable signal 5 | Low | High | - |
| 06 | Signal level close enable signal 6 | Low | High | - |
| 07 | Signal level close enable signal 7 | Low | High | - |
| 08 | Signal level close enable signal 8 | Low | High | - |
| 09 | Signal level close enable signal 9 | Low | High | - |
| 10 | Signal level close enable signal 10 | Low | High | - |
| 11 | Signal level close enable signal 11 | Low | High | - |
| 12 | Signal level close enable signal 12 | Low | High | - |
| 13 | Signal level close enable signal 13 | Low | High | - |
| 14 | Signal level close enable signal 14 | Low | High | - |
| 15 | Signal level close enable signal 15 | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6590, r6592

**Note:**
  
The value specified in a bit determines the signal level at which the close enable
signal must be present.

### r6592.0...15 CO/BO: Close enable signal state

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the state of the individual input signals of the close enable signals after
level adaptation.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Extended close enable signal 0 | Low | High | - |
| 01 | Extended close enable signal 1 | Low | High | - |
| 02 | Extended close enable signal 2 | Low | High | - |
| 03 | Extended close enable signal 3 | Low | High | - |
| 04 | Extended close enable signal 4 | Low | High | - |
| 05 | Extended close enable signal 5 | Low | High | - |
| 06 | Extended close enable signal 6 | Low | High | - |
| 07 | Extended close enable signal 7 | Low | High | - |
| 08 | Extended close enable signal 8 | Low | High | - |
| 09 | Extended close enable signal 9 | Low | High | - |
| 10 | Extended close enable signal 10 | Low | High | - |
| 11 | Extended close enable signal 11 | Low | High | - |
| 12 | Extended close enable signal 12 | Low | High | - |
| 13 | Extended close enable signal 13 | Low | High | - |
| 14 | Extended close enable signal 14 | Low | High | - |
| 15 | Extended close enable signal 15 | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6590, p6591

**Note:**
  
A logical '1' indicates the state of the enable signal.  
Index 0(8) displays the enable signal for transformer monitoring r6509.24(25) in the
default setting of p6590.  
Index 1(9) displays the enable signal for current monitoring r6587.16(17) in the default
setting of p6590.

### p6601[0...n] I = f(n) motor mode ordinate value 0

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |

Sets ordinate value 0 of the limiting characteristic in motor mode for the DC link
current over the absolute speed.

The ordinate value refers to the value of the current limit in p0640.  
The associated abscissa value 0 is "0".

| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 80.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets ordinate value 3 of the limiting characteristic in generator mode for the DC
link current over the absolute speed.

**Note:**
  
The ordinate value refers to the value of the current limit in p0640.  
The associated abscissa value 0 is "0".

### p6650 Sequence control test mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 99 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate the test mode.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Test operation auxiliaries  
2:
Test operation output disconnector  
3:
Test operation, excitation power circuit  
4:
Test operation output transformer demagnetization  
10:
Test operation incoming circuit breaker  
20:
Test operation circuit breaker output side  
30:
Test operation inrush circuit breaker  
31:
Test operation input transformer premagnetization  
99:
Test operation off

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0840, p6651  
Refer to:
A07318

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the test mode.

**Note:**
  
The test is started when the logical AND operation of the signals of binector inputs
p6651 and p0840 is fulfilled.

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 06651 - 06860

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORGL 06651 - 06860

### p6651 BI: Sequence control test mode ON/OFF

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9821, 9824 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to switch in/switch out the test.  
Test mode is set using p6650.  
The test is started in REMOTE mode if the logical AND operation of the signal values
of binector input p6651 and p0840 supply a "1 signal".

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0840, p6650

### p6655 Acknowledgment external pulse time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 5.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the pulse time for the external acknowledgment.

**Note:**
  
The total duration of the acknowledgment procedure is extended by this time.  
The pulse time has been selected too short if a fault from the external peripherals
must be acknowledged twice.

### r6660.0...5 CO/BO: Line-side circuit breaker system 1 control signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays (binector outputs) the control signals for switch 0.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
For SINAMICS GL150, this signal is not supported.

### r6661.0...5 CO/BO: Motor-side circuit breaker 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output of the control signals for switch 1.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6230

**Note:**
  
The assignment of the circuit breaker on the output side (MSS) depends on the setting
of p6230[1].  
p6230[1] = 4:  
MSS1 -> circuit breaker in the bypass circuit  
p6230[1] = 5:  
MSS1 -> circuit breaker (or disconnector) in the bypass circuit  
For bit 03:  
For SINAMICS GL150, this signal is not supported.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

of p6230[1]  
p6230[1] = 4:

p6230[1] = 5:  
MSS 2 -> all circuit breakers (or disconnectors) in the step-up circuit
