### p6670[0...6] BI: Switch simulation mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source of the simulation mode for the switch.  
When the simulation mode is activated, the feedback signals for the switch are set
the same as the software commands. All monitoring functions and the switching commands
are deactivated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
LSS system 1  
[
1]:
MSS system 1/bypass MSS 1  
[
2]:
LSS system 2  
[
3]:
MSS system 2/Bypass MSS 2  
[
4]:
Inrush LSS system 1  
[
5]:
Inrush LSS system 2  
[
6]:
Bypass MSS 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6660, r6661, r6662, r6663, r6664, r6665, r6666

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the simulation mode.

**Note:**
  
BI: p6670[x] = 1 signal: Simulation mode activated  
BI: p6670[x] = 0 signal: Simulation mode deactivated

### p6671[0...6] BI: Switch on/off

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to close/open the switch.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
LSS system 1  
[
1]:
MSS system 1/bypass MSS 1  
[
2]:
LSS system 2  
[
3]:
MSS system 2/Bypass MSS 2  
[
4]:
Inrush LSS system 1  
[
5]:
Inrush LSS system 2  
[
6]:
Bypass MSS 3

**Note:**
  
The switches are only switched by the sequence control, i.e. for SINAMICS GL150, binector
input p6671 is not active.

### p6675[0...6] Switch open monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.300 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time of the feedback signals when opening the switch.  
The feedback signal within the time in p6675 must indicate that the switch has reliably
opened (i.e. bounce can no longer be detected). The monitoring time in p6675 must
be set greater than the time in p6676 as it additionally takes into account the bounce
time of the feedback signal.  
The following applies: p6675 > p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
LSS system 1  
[
1]:
MSS system 1/bypass MSS 1  
[
2]:
LSS system 2  
[
3]:
MSS system 2/Bypass MSS 2  
[
4]:
Inrush LSS system 1  
[
5]:
Inrush LSS system 2  
[
6]:
Bypass MSS 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6674, p6676  
Refer to:
F07306, F07307, F07308, F07309, F07310, F07311, F07312

**Danger:**
  
If this monitoring function responds, then it must be assumed that the switch had
not opened. In this case, the drive system is not disconnected from the line supply.

### p6676[0...6] Switch max. opening time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.200 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum permissible opening time of the switch.  
The feedback signal must indicate for the first time that the switch has opened within
the time in p6676. The feedback signal must be stable within the time in p6675 (i.e.
bounce can no longer be detected).  
The following applies: p6675 > p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
LSS system 1  
[
1]:
MSS system 1/bypass MSS 1  
[
2]:
LSS system 2  
[
3]:
MSS system 2/Bypass MSS 2  
[
4]:
Inrush LSS system 1  
[
5]:
Inrush LSS system 2  
[
6]:
Bypass MSS 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6675  
Refer to:
F07306, F07307, F07308, F07309, F07310, F07311, F07312

**Danger:**
  
The switch used must open within this time in order to protect the drive system.  
Switch with a longer opening time than specified in this parameter cannot be used.

### r6690[0...6] CO: Switch status word display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the switch feedback signal.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
LSS system 1  
[
1]:
MSS system 1/bypass MSS 1  
[
2]:
LSS system 2  
[
3]:
MSS system 2/Bypass MSS 2  
[
4]:
Inrush LSS system 1  
[
5]:
Inrush LSS system 2  
[
6]:
Bypass MSS 3

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Feedback signal switch ready | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Feedback signal switch closed | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Feedback signal switch open | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 1 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 2 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Feedback signal no external trip | No | Yes | - |

| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the feedback signal of the switch.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
LSS system 1  
[
1]:
MSS system 1/bypass MSS 1  
[
2]:
LSS system 2  
[
3]:
MSS system 2/Bypass MSS 2  
[
4]:
Inrush LSS system 1  
[
5]:
Inrush LSS system 2  
[
6]:
Bypass MSS 3

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Feedback signal switch ready | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Feedback signal switch closed | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Feedback signal switch open | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 1 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 2 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Feedback signal no external trip | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6684

### r6692[0...6] Switch fault status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the fault state of the switch.  
This parameter indicates as to whether the cause of the fault is present.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
LSS system 1  
[
1]:
MSS system 1/bypass MSS 1  
[
2]:
LSS system 2  
[
3]:
MSS system 2/Bypass MSS 2  
[
4]:
Inrush LSS system 1  
[
5]:
Inrush LSS system 2  
[
6]:
Bypass MSS 3

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Not ready | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Feedback signal closed missing | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Feedback signal open missing | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Too slow | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Auxiliary voltage 1 missing | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Auxiliary voltage 2 missing | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Consistency error | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Crowbar thyristor and feedback signal missing | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | External trip | No | Yes | - |

### r6727 CO: Offset commutation angle to pole wheel angle

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 4715 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2005 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°] | - [°] | [ ] - [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Offset angle between the electrical commutation angle (from the encoder) and the electrical
pole wheel angle.

**Note:**
  
If there is still no offset – or during an active encoder fault, value 3600 is displayed.  
The offset angle itself is either determined during the pole wheel identification
(=rotor position sensing) (incremental encoder) – or from the absolute position of
the absolute encoder.  
The offset is also determined after an encoder fault has been resolved from the zero
track of the incremental encoder or from the absolute position.  
However, the precondition is that the setting is not equal to the factory setting
of parameter p6729.  
When using an encoder with absolute position, the offset angle is only displayed in
the test operation rotor position sensing (p6265).

### r6728 CO: Offset commutation reference to pole wheel angle

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 4715 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2005 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°] | - [°] | [ ] - [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Offset angle between the synchronized electrical commutation angle (from the encoder
with zero track or absolute position track) and the electrical pole wheel angle.

**Note:**
  
If there is still no offset, cannot be determined – or if, with an encoder fault active,
value 3600 is displayed.  
The offset angle itself is either determined during the pole wheel position identification
(= rotor position sensing) under the assumption that the encoder type (p0400) has
a zero track or an absolute track.  
The value measured in this way can then be transferred in parameter p6729.

### p6729[0...n] Setting the commutation reference to pole wheel angle

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**MDS, p0130 | **Func. diagram:** 4715 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2005 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -180.00 [°] | 180.00 [°] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Offset angle between the synchronized electrical commutation angle (from the incremental
encoder with zero track or absolute position track) and the electrical pole wheel
angle.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6728

**Notice:**
  
This value must be redetermined after the encoder has been mounted.  
The value applies to the electrical system. If a mechanical measured value is available,
before entry, it must be multiplied by the pole pair number.

**Note:**
  
The angle determined in r6728 is entered here and saved.  
However, only in operation without encoder, or when the pulses are inhibited, in the
converter.

### r6741[0...n] CO: PSA actual value channel 10 value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the value of actual value channel 10 (X80) for the
Power Stack Adapter (PSA). The value is displayed corresponding to the set display
mode (p6770).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6770

**Note:**
  
The actual value is scaled to the maximum mapping range of the shunt (-100.00 ...
100.00). Display value = 100.00 applies: The actual value sensing has failed. Display
value = 9999.99 applies: The actual value sensing is not available

| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the value of actual value channel 5 (X75) for the
Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The value is displayed corresponding to the set display mode (p6770).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6770

**Note:**
  
The actual value is scaled to the maximum mapping range of the shunt (-100.00 ...
100.00).  
Display value = 100.00 applies: The actual value sensing has failed.  
Display value = 9999.99 applies: The actual value sensing is not available.

### p6770 PSA actual value channels display mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the required display mode for actual value channels for the Power Stack Adapter
(PSA).

**Value:**
  
0:
Not calibrated and with offset  
1:
Poss. calibrated and offset-free  
2:
Offset  
3:
AVT mapping range

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765, r6766, r6767, r6768, r6769, p6902, p6903

**Note:**
  
For p6770 = 1 the following applies:  
p6902 and p6903 define whether the offset calculation should be taken into account.  
For p6770 = 3 the following applies:  
The values displayed are absolute values in volts or amperes (not in %).

|  |  |  |

2.6

Sets the display mode for r6771 (FPGA register actual value).

**Value:**
  
0:

1:
Short-time actual value difference channel 5  
2:
Short-time actual value channel 2  
3:
Short-time actual value channel 3  
4:
RX fault (receipt of TG messages)  
5:
Status actual value channels  
6:
Phase angle phase integrator N Low  
7:
Phase angle phase integrator N High  
8:
Phase angle phase integrator M Low  
9:
Phase angle phase integrator M High  
10:
Angle at the voltage zero crossover N1  
11:
Angle at the voltage zero crossover N2  
12:
Angle at the voltage zero crossover M  
13:
Tµ measurement N  
14:
Tµ measurement M  
15:
Status automatic state machine N  
16:
Status automatic state machine N1  
17:
Status automatic state machine N2  
18:
Status automatic state machine M  
19:
Status automatic state machine M  
20:
Status min/max monitoring N  
21:
Status min/max monitoring M  
22:
Thyristor fault 1  
23:
Thyristor fault 2  
24:
Thyristor fault 3  
25:
Self firing 1  
26:
Self firing 2  
27:
Self firing 3  
28:
Power supply monitoring  
29:
Alarm fiber-optic cable damping  
30:
Status trip line input  
31:
Status trip line output  
32:
Status protective responses N  
33:
Status protective responses M  
34:
Status protective response  
35:
Status digital inputs/outputs unidirectional  
36:
Status digital inputs/outputs bidirectional

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6771

### p6780[0...3] Current differential measurement filter setting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 7 | [ 0 ] 4 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the required filter coefficients for the current differential measurement.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Stage 1  
[
1]:
Stage 2  
[
2]:
Stage 3  
[
3]:
Stage 4

**Note:**
  
The current differential measurement is used to correct the measured voltage actual
values.  
The current measured values are filtered using a recursive filter (4th order low-pass
filter).  
The parameter acts differently on the individual stages of the filter.  
p6780[0...3] = 0: Filter stage 0 ... 3 deactivated  
p6780[0...3] = 1 ... 7: Filter stage 0 ... 3 activated, the filter effect increases
from a minimum to a maximum

### p6781 Actual value sensing configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Changes the actual value sensing configuration specified by the hardware description.  
The setting applies to the entire converter.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Actual value sensing input current | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Actual value sensing DC link current | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Actual value sensing output current | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Configuration from HW description | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A30118

**Notice:**
  
If actual value sensing is de-selected on the input or output side, it will no longer
be possible to detect a short circuit in the converter. In this case it is recommended
to activate commutation monitoring in p6086.

**Note:**
  
At least one instance of actual value sensing must be active.

### p6782[0...n] Actual value sensing synclost fault counter

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 bin | 0010 0011 1100 0011 0100 0101 1111 1111 bin | [ 0 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Counter for the unsuccessful synchronization operations of the AVT data flow of the
PSA actual value channels 2 (X72) ,3 (X73), 4(X74) and 5 (X75).  
  
The number of faults are displayed as 9-digit decimal number <czzzzzzzz>:  
c = channel number 2...5  
zzzzzzzz = fault counter 0...99999999.  
  
The counter of the selected channel is set to '0' by entering a channel number (2...5).
All of the fault counters are reset by entering 0.  
  
The fault counter is also reset after a fault event has been resolved (F30105).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F30105

**Note:**
  
Only those actual value channels are displayed that were configured in the HW_description
of the converter.  
  
The channels are displayed in a 2s cycle.

### p6790[0...1] Fan differential pressure alarm threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [Pa] | 2000 [Pa] | [ 0 ] 0 [Pa]  [ 1 ] 0 [Pa] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold for the measured differential pressures.  
Alarm A49111 is output if one of the measured pressure actual values falls below the
threshold.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
DP thyristor stack  
[
1]:
DP DC link reactor

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6791  
Refer to:
A49111

**Note:**
  
When running up for the first time, the thresholds are preassigned with data from
the hardware description.

### p6794[0...1] Fan monitoring time and time delay when switching-on

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 20.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 8.00 [s]  [ 1 ] 0.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
For index [0]:  
Sets the monitoring time for switching on the fans.  
If not all of the conditions are fulfilled within this monitoring time after switching
on, then an appropriate signal is output.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the switch-on delay time of the fan in power unit system 2.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F49110

### p6828[0...n] PSA offset actual value channel 10

### p6850[0...n] PSA analog output 0 AND logic operation value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the value to be ANDed with the signal source in connector input p6840 for analog
output 0 (X332.15/X332.16) of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Signal sources with data type Unsigned16/Unsigned32 are ANDed with this selected value.  
With the factory setting of p6860 and p6870 the result is displayed as follows at
the analog output:  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 1 --> analog output = 10 V  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 0 --> analog output = 5 V

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6840

| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the value to be ANDed with the signal source in connector input p6845 for analog
output 5 (X332.5/X332.6) of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Signal sources with data type Unsigned16/Unsigned32 are ANDed with this selected value.  
With the factory setting of p6865 and p6875 the result is displayed as follows at
the analog output:  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 1 --> analog output = 10 V  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 0 --> analog output = 5 V

Displays the actual signal value for analog output 5 (X332.6/X332.5) of the Power
Stack Adapter (PSA).

### r6888[0...3] CO: PSA analog outputs calibration signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Calibration signals for the analog outputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
For the factory settings of p6860 to p6867 and p6870 to p6877, by appropriately interconnecting
connector input CI: p6840 to p6847 with r6888[0...3], the required calibration signals
are effective at the analog output.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Constant value -1.0 --> 0 V  
[
1]:
Constant value 0.0 --> 5 V  
[
2]:
Constant value +1.0 --> 10 V  
[
3]:
Sawtooth with 1 Hz from -1.0 ... +1.0 --> 0 ... 10 V

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6840

**Note:**
  
When setting the scaling factor (p6842) and offset (p6843) to the factory setting,
the following apply:  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[0] --> analog output = 0 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[1] --> analog output = 5 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[2] --> analog output = 10 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[3] --> analog output = sawtooth 0 ...
10 V with 1 Hz

### p6901 DC link voltage actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for DC link voltage actual value sensing.  
In mode p6901 = 0, the offset is determined and in operation is taken into account
for the actual value calculation.  
In mode p6901 = 1, the offset is determined - but is not taken into account for the
actual value calculation.  
In mode p6901 = 2, the offset determined in mode p6901 = 0 is saved and is subsequently
taken into account in the actual value calculation, regardless of the operating state
(unchanged).  
  
  
When the mode is enabled (p6901 = 0) for an operating inhibit, the offset is determined.  
For the inhibited mode (p6901 = 1), the offset is determined – but is not taken into
account for the actual value calculation.

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6828

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.  
Before determining the offset, it must be ensured that the system is in a no voltage
condition to avoid measuring errors.  
To permanently save the offset values in mode p6901 = 2, the parameters must be saved
in a non-volatile manner (p0977 = 1 or "copy RAM to ROM").

### p6902 Current actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for the current actual value sensing.  
In mode p6902 = 0 the offset is determined in the no-current state (p6260.24 = 1)
and is taken into account in operation for the actual value calculation.  
In mode p6902 = 1 the offset is determined - but is not taken into account for the
actual value calculation.  
In mode p6902 = 2, the offset determined in mode p6902 = 0 is saved and is subsequently
taken into account in the actual value calculation, regardless of the operating state
(unchanged).

**Recommend.:**
  
It is recommended - in addition to the display in p6260.24 =1 - to carry out a measurement
to ensure the no-current state prior to saving the offset (p6902 = 0 -> 2) (e.g. motor
is at a standstill, the converters are in the no-voltage state, the circuit breakers
are open).

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6260, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765, p6832, p6833, p6834, p6835, p6950  
Refer to:
F30106

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.  
In mode p6902 = 0 the offset is only determined if the enable signal in p6260.24 =
1 is present.  
In the other modes of p6902, p6260.24 = 0.  
To permanently save the offset values in mode p6902 = 2, the parameters must be saved
in a non-volatile manner (p0977 = 1 or "copy RAM to ROM").

### p6903 Voltage actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for voltage actual value sensing.  
In mode p6903 = 0, the offset is determined in the no-voltage state (p6260.25 = 1)
and is taken into account in operation for the actual value calculation.  
In mode p6903 = 1 the offset is determined - but is not taken into account for the
actual value calculation.  
In mode p6903 = 2, the offset determined in mode p6903 = 0 is saved and is subsequently
taken into account in the actual value calculation, regardless of the operating state
(unchanged).

**Recommend.:**
  
Prior to saving the offset (p6903 = 0 -> 2), it is recommended - in addition to the
display in p6260.25 =1 - to carry out a measurement to ensure that a no-voltage state
really exists (e.g. motor is at a standstill, the converters are in the no-voltage
state, the circuit breakers are open).

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6260, r6760, r6761, p6830, p6831, p6838, p6839, p6950  
Refer to:
F30106

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.  
In mode p6903 = 0 the offset is only determined if the enable signal in r6260.25 =
1 is present.  
In the other modes of p6903, r6260.25 = 0.  
To permanently save the offset values in mode p6903 = 2, the parameters must be saved
in a non-volatile manner (p0977 = 1 or "copy RAM to ROM").

### p6904 Star voltage actual values offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode to sense the star voltage actual values.  
In mode p6904 = 0 the offset is determined in the no-voltage state (p6260.26 = 1)
and is taken into account in operation for the actual value calculation.  
In mode p6904 = 1, the offset is determined but not taken into account when calculating
the actual value.  
In mode p6904 = 2, the offset determined in mode p6904 = 0 is saved and is subsequently
taken into account in the actual value calculation, regardless of the operating state
(unchanged).

**Recommend.:**
  
Prior to saving the offset (p6904 = 0 -> 2), it is recommended - in addition to the
display in p6260.26 =1 - to carry out a measurement to ensure that a no-voltage state
really exists (e.g. motor is at a standstill, the converters are in the no-voltage
state, the circuit breakers are open).

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6260, r6766, r6767, r6768, p6836, p6837, p6838, p6950  
Refer to:
F30106

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.  
In mode p6904 = 0, the offset is only determined if the enable signal in p6260.26
= 1 is present.  
In the other modes of p6904, p6260.26 = 0.  
To permanently save the offset values in mode p6904 = 2, the parameters must be saved
in a non-volatile manner (p0977 = 1 or "copy RAM to ROM").

### p6944[0...n] PSA analog inputs type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9984 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0011 0011 0011 0011 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the type of analog inputs on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
In addition, the associated switches S5 and S6 must be appropriately set.  
AI 0: S5.0 = V --> voltage input, S5.0 = I --> current input  
AI 1: S5.1 = V --> voltage input, S5.1 = I --> current input  
AI 2: S6.0 = V --> voltage input, S6.0 = I --> current input  
AI 3: S6.1 = V --> voltage input, S6.1 = I --> current input  
The scaling (final value) can be set using parameters p17460, p17463, p17466, p17469.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | PSA AI 0 , type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 01 | PSA AI 0, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 02 | PSA AI 0, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | PSA AI 1, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 05 | PSA AI 1, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 06 | PSA AI 1, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | PSA AI 2, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 09 | PSA AI 2, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 10 | PSA AI 2, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | PSA AI 3, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 13 | PSA AI 3, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 14 | PSA AI 3, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6946, r6947, r6948, r6949, p17460, r17462, p17463, r17465, p17466, r17468, p17469,
r17471  
Refer to:
A30125

**Notice:**
  
For operation as a voltage input/current input, switch S5.0, S5.1, S6.0 or S6.1 must
be appropriately set.

**Note:**
  
The settings of the individual inputs can only be changed if these are not preset
by the hardware description (DESC0000).

|  |  |  |

AI 2+: X323.13  
AI 2-: X323.14

The input is not enabled via the hardware description data.  
For a display value = -199, the following applies:  
An implausible measured value is available at the input.  
The following generally applies:  
The value is also displayed in a per unit representation in parameter r17468.

### r6951[0...n] CO: PSA temperature input 0 value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9985 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**21_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2006 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°C] | - [°C] | [ ] - [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the smoothed value at temperature input 0 (TEMP 0)
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6955, p6959  
Refer to:
A30018, A30019

**Note:**
  
The input signal can be smoothed for all PSA using p6959[4].  
For 4-conductor temperature sensors, the following applies  
I_IN 0: X333.13  
TEMP 0-: X333.14  
TEMP 0+: X333.15  
I_OUT 0: X333.16  
For 2-conductor temperature sensors, the following external jumpers are required:  
X333.13 - X333.14  
X333.15 - X333.16  
For a display value = -200 °C, the following applies:  
The input is not enabled via parameter p6945 or via the hardware description data.  
For a display value = -199 °C, the following applies:  
An implausible measured value is available at the input.

### p6955[0...n] PSA temperature input 0 offset

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTORSL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -100.0 [°C] | 100.0 [°C] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the temperature offset for the PT100 measured value at PSA temperature input
0 (PSA TEMP 0).  
If there is a difference between the temperature displayed in r0037 and the actual
temperature, this offset can be entered in this parameter, thereby compensating for
the difference.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037

### p6959[0...7] PSA analog inputs smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, A_INFMV | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [ms] | 1000.0 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time constant of the 1st order low-pass filter for the analog inputs
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
This setting is the same for all PDS.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Analog input 0  
[
1]:
Analog input 1  
[
2]:
Analog input 2  
[
3]:
Analog input 3  
[
4]:
Temperature input 0  
[
5]:
Temperature input 1  
[
6]:
Temperature input 2  
[
7]:
Temperature input 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037, r6951, r6952, r6954, p6955

**Note:**
  
AI: Analog Input  
TEMP: temperature sensor input PT100

### r6992.0...15 CO/BO: Recorder status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the recorder.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Internal activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 01 | External activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 02 | Internal trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 03 | External trigger 1.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 04 | External trigger 1.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 05 | External trigger 1.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 06 | External trigger 1.4 | Not set | Set | - |
| 07 | External trigger 2.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 08 | External trigger 2.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 09 | External trigger 2.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 10 | Hardware trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 11 | Data buffering running | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Post trigger time running | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Data being stored | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Data buffer full | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Trigger group signal | Not set | Set | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6993, p6994, r6997, p6998, p6999  
Refer to:
A49998

### p6993[0...5] Recorder trigger 2 bit mask

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0001 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the bit mask for trigger signal 2 (p6994) of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6994

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.

### p6994[0...5] CI: Recorder trigger 2 signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for trigger 2 of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6993

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.

### p6995 Recorder hardware trace pre-trigger time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTORSL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the pretrigger of the hardware trace.

**Value:**
  
0:
Hardware trace pre-trigger at 50.0%  
1:
Hardware trace pre-trigger at 75.0%  
2:
Hardware trace pre-trigger at 87.5%

### p6996[0...255] Recorder signals

|  |  |  |

### p6998[0...4] BI: Recorder trigger 1 signal sources

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources to activate and trigger the recorder.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External activation  
[
1]:
External trigger 1.1  
[
2]:
External trigger 1.2  
[
3]:
External trigger 1.3  
[
4]:
External trigger 1.4

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
The external recorder activation extends the internal activation. If the recorder
has already been activated internally, then it can be externally triggered via p6998[1..4]
or p6994[0..2], without requiring that p6998[0] = 1.

### p6999[0...8] Recorder parameterization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2000 | [ 0 ] 1   [ 1 ] 2000   [ 2 ] 1900   [ 3 ] 0   [ 4 ] 0   [ 5 ] 100   [ 6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 1   [ 8 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to parameterize the recorder.  
The recorder supplies up to 256 internal variables (depending on the parameterization).
The maximum recording time is 2000 ms. The variables are acquired in an integer multiple
of the current controller sampling time – and a pretrigger can be set. The values
can then be acyclically written to the memory card.  
The development and system test departments have the software necessary to decode
the content.  
Normally, this recorder is always enabled. However, if problems occur for topologies
with a low remaining CPU time, then it can be deactivated using this parameter (this
saves approx. 5 ... 20 µs CPU time).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Enable  
[
1]:
Recording time  
[
2]:
Pretrigger time internal trigger  
[
3]:
Output message  
[
4]:
Recording factor  
[
5]:
Pretrigger time external trigger  
[
6]:
Hardware trace in synchronism  
[
7]:
Hardware trigger active  
[
8]:
Recording sampling time factor

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49998

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
Enables or disables the function.  
p6999[0] = 0  
Inhibits the function.  
p6999[0] = 1  
Enables the function. A POWER ON is required after the enable.  
p6999[0] = 20  
Sets the factory setting for p6999, p6996, p6993, p6994, p6998.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the recording time, [ms]. The value is discretized with the current controller
sampling time.  
For index [2]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the internal trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [3]:  
Enables or inhibits the output of message A49998 when a trigger event is initiated.  
For index [4]:  
p6999[4] = n, n = 0 ... 4  
Recording with the factor, which extends the record time p6999[1] and the pre-trigger
time p6999[2] 2^n times, and reduces the number of signals 2^n times.  
Example:  
p6999[1] = 2000, p6999[2] = 1000, p6999[4] = 4  
--> recording time: 2000 ms * 2^4 = 32 s, pre-trigger time:1000 ms * 2^4 = 16 s, number
of signals 128/16 = 8  
When setting p6999[4] to 0, the results are as follows:  
--> recording time: 2000 ms, pre-trigger time: 1000 ms, number of signals: 128  
The maximum number of signals depends on the drive object.  
For index [5]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the external trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [6]:  
Activating/deactivating wait time for a hardware trace to be completely uploaded.  
p6999[6] = 1  
After a trigger event, the system waits for the hardware trace upload to be completed.
A new trigger is not possible until this upload has been completed.  
p6999[6] = 0  
After a trigger event, the system does NOT wait for the hardware trace upload to be
completed. Recorder trace can be immediately re-triggered.  
For index [7]:  
Deactivates/activates the hardware trigger. When p6999[7] = 0, the hardware trigger
is deactivated, and a hardware fault means that the recorder is no longer triggered.  
For index [8]:  
Sets the factor for the recording sampling time.  
Example:  
p6999[8] = 2 --> sampling time to record/trace signals is 2 x current controller sampling
time  
After a power on, this parameter has the same value as before the change.

### r7001.0...14 CO/BO: PU_redundant operating display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Modulation | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Operating display of the DRIVE-CLiQ components (PSA and VSM) of the subsystems.  
The DRIVE-CLiQ components are deactivated if their communications to the Control Unit
were interrupted.  
After at least one component was deactivated and all components are again active,
a sub-boot is required.  
The sub-boot is automatically started with the next pulse inhibit for p7857 = 1. For
p7857 = 0, the sub-boot is inhibited.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Subsystem 1: Ready for operation | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Subsystem 1: CU sub-boot necessary | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Subsystem 1: DRIVE-CLiQ components deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Subsystem 1: PSA1 deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Subsystem 1: PSA2 deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Subsystem 1: VSM deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Subsystem 2: Ready for operation | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Subsystem 2: CU sub-boot necessary | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Subsystem 2: DRIVE-CLiQ components deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Subsystem 2: PSA1 deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Subsystem 2: PSA2 deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Subsystem 2: VSM deactivated | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7002, r7100, r7101, r7102, r7103, r7104, r7105, p7857  
Refer to:
F05080, F05081

### r7002.0...10 CO/BO: PU_redundancy fault display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Fault display of the subsystems when power unit redundancy is activated (r0205 = x1
or x3).  
For faults in a subsystem (A05080, A05081) operation is continued with the other subsystem.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Subsystem 1: Ready | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Subsystem 1: Fault present | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Subsystem 1: A fault that has not been acknowledged is present | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Subsystem 2: Ready | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Subsystem 2: Fault present | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Subsystem 2: A fault that has not been acknowledged is present | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0205, r7001, r7100, r7101, r7102, r7103, r7104, r7105  
Refer to:
F05080, F05081

**Notice:**
  
Faults that have occurred are only saved in the ring buffer (r7100[0...99] ... r7105[0...99])
and are not signaled as is generally the case.  
Faults in subsystem 1 or 2 are signaled using A05080 or A05081.

**Note:**
  
The fault state of a subsystem is only derived from the faults that are assigned to
the subsystem. Other faults are not taken into account here.

### r7005.0...31 CO/BO: CU redundancy status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the status word for redundant operation of the Control Unit (CU).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Operating state requested | Passive | Active | - |
| 01 | CU state not inverted | Passive | Active | - |
| 02 | CU state inverted | Active | Passive | - |
| 03 | Other CU state not inverted | Passive | Active | - |
| 04 | Other CU state inverted | Active | Passive | - |
| 05 | Other CU state undefined | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | State changeover condition fulfilled | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | State being changed over | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Both CUs in the active state | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Both CUs in the passive state | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | CU state feedback | Passive | Active | - |
| 11 | Group signal CU act requested | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | CU state ready | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Enable stop response in the sequencer | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Fault is present from a non-redundant component | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | CU redundancy enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | CU state active | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | CU state active clock cycle bit set | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | CU state ready | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | CU state ready clock cycle bit set | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | CU state no stop response sys 1 set | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | CU state no stop response sys 1 cycle bit set | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | CU state no stop response sys 2 set | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | CU state no stop response sys 2 cycle bit set | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | CU sign-of-life bit set | No | Yes | - |
| 25 | CU active counter bit 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | CU active counter bit 3 | No | Yes | - |
| 27 | CU active counter bit 4 | No | Yes | - |
| 28 | CU active counter bit 5 | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | CU active counter bit 6 | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | CU active counter bit 7 | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | CU active counter bit 8 | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7112, p7120

**Note:**
  
The following bits are of significance for CU redundancy:  
Bit 00 ... 11, 14, 15  
For CU hot standby redundancy (from firmware version 4.7 and higher) the following
bits are of significance:  
Bit 00 ... 03, 08, 09, 12, 13, 15, 16 ... 31  
For bit 15:  
For CU redundancy and CU hot standby redundancy, this bit is set.  
This bit is set from hardware description data.

### r7007.0...17 CO/BO: Power unit operating mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Displays the operating mode for drives with power redundancy.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Redundant power unit operating mode | Normal operation | HM operation | - |
| 01 | Motor op with just one winding system (HM) power unit system 1 | Inactive | Active | - |
| 02 | Motor op with just one winding system (HM) power unit system 2 | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Request motor operation with just one winding system (HM) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 05 | Request motor op with just one winding sys power unit system 1 | Inactive | Active | - |
| 06 | Request motor op with just one winding sys power unit system 2 | Inactive | Active | - |
| 07 | Request full motor operation | Inactive | Active | - |
| 08 | Full motor operation is again available. | Inactive | Active | - |
| 09 | Status: error sequence control | Inactive | Active | - |
| 10 | Status: converter without PU redundancy | Inactive | Inactive | - |
| 11 | Status: no-load operation | Inactive | Active | - |
| 12 | Status: full motor operation | Inactive | Active | - |
| 13 | Status: wait | Inactive | Active | - |
| 14 | Status: wait for inhibit operation | Inactive | Active | - |
| 15 | Status: wait for operating enable | Inactive | Active | - |
| 16 | Status: motor operation with just one winding system (HM) | Inactive | Active | - |
| 17 | Status: program error, should never be active | Inactive | Active | - |

**Note:**
  
Parameter r7007 is only relevant in series (crossover) topology with power unit redundancy.

### p7010 Current controller current asymmetry shutdown threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**Modulation | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10 [%] | 100 [%] | [ 0 ] 20 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the shutdown threshold to detect current asymmetry for several DC links.  
In this case, the smoothed difference of the highest and smallest smoothed DC link
current is evaluated.  
The specified value is referred to the rated DC link current.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6321, p7012  
Refer to:
A05052

### r7101[0...99] Par_circuit ring buffer data set number

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred from power units connected in
parallel (Motor Module, Active Line Infeed, Voltage Sensing Module).  
p7101 < 100:  
Displays the Power unit Data Set number (PDS).  
p7101 >= 100:  
Displays the Voltage Sensing Module Data Set number (VSMDS)

**Value:**
  
-1:
No fault/alarm  
0:
Total system  
1:
Subsystem 1  
2:
Subsystem 2  
10:
Overall system PSA1  
11:
Subsystem 1: PSA1  
12:
Subsystem 1: PSA2  
21:
Subsystem 2: PSA1  
22:
Subsystem 2: PSA2  
101:
VSM1  
102:
VSM2  
1001:
Subsystem 1  
1002:
Subsystem 2

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7100, r7102, r7103, r7104, r7105

**Note:**
  
The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

**Notice:**
  
The time comprises r7124 (days) and r7103 (milliseconds).

**Note:**
  
The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

### r7110[0...n] CO: CU redundancy tracking the passive CU

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**MSR, p0023 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Displays the tracking of the passive Control Unit (CU).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | CU is passive | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Switch on enable | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Switch on enable subsystem | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Control AUX tracked | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Control disconnector tracked | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Control LSS tracked | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Control excitation tracked | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Enable excitation tracked | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Speed controller tracked | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Enable LSC tracked | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Enable MSC tracked | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Line PLL ok | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Rotor position offset determined | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
The passive CU is only ready if all bits are set in the display.  
All bits are reset in the active state of the CU.

### p7112[0...9] BI: CU redundancy other CU state signal sources

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6950, 6953, 6955, 6956 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the feedback signal of the state of the other Control Unit
(CU).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
State active  
[
1]:
State active inverted  
[
2]:
State active clock cycle bit  
[
3]:
State ready  
[
4]:
State ready clock cycle bit  
[
5]:
State no stop response sys 1  
[
6]:
State no stop response sys 1 cycle bit  
[
7]:
State no stop response sys 2  
[
8]:
State no stop response sys 2 cycle bit  
[
9]:
State sign-of-life bit

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6222, r7005, p7120

**Note:**
  
The following indices are of significance for CU redundancy:  
0, 1.  
For CU hot standby redundancy (from firmware version 4.7 and higher) the following
indices are of significance:  
0, 2 ... 9.  
The parameter can only be changed when the pulses are inhibited and when the line-side
circuit breakers have been opened (r6222.8 = 0).

### p7114[0...4] CU redundancy clock cycle signal settings

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Parallel), VECTORGL, VECTOR3P (Parallel) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 20 | [ 0 ] 2   [ 1 ] 2   [ 2 ] 3   [ 3 ] 1   [ 4 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the times for the clock cycle signals for CU redundancy.  
For indices 0, 1 the following applies:  
The duration for 1-signal (T_on) is set in the computation clock cycles.  
The following applies for index 2:  
The duration for 0-signal (T_off) is set as ratio to T_on.  
T_off = p7114[2] x T_on  
The following applies for index 3:  
The duration of the monitoring window (MW) for received clock cycles is set as number
of time windows (TW).  
TW = p7114[1] x (1 + p7114[2])  
MW = p7114[3] x TW  
For p7114[3] = 0, the clock cycle signal is not evaluated. The state determined from
the clock cycle signal remains frozen.  
The following applies for index 4:  
The sampling time of the signal processing can only be set as an integer multiple
of the current controller sampling time (p0115[0]).  
The modified sampling time is not effective until the drive unit is switched on again.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Sign of life T_on  
[
1]:
Clock cycle signal T_on  
[
2]:
Clock cycle signal T_off (multiple of T_on)  
[
3]:
Time window number (multiple of T_on + T_off)  
[
4]:
Sampling time of the signal processing

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7005

**Note:**
  
The following minimum setting values apply for the individual indices:  
- p7114[0, 1, 2] = 1  
- p7114[3] = 2  
- p7114[4] = 8

### r7115.0...30 CO/BO: CU redundancy status word 2

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for status word 2 for redundant operation of the Control Unit
(CU).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | CU hot standby redundancy enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Pulse enable from the redundancy logic | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | CU OK | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Passive CU sys 1 can take over operation | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Passive CU sys 2 can take over operation | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Passive CU tracking OK | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Passive CU OK | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Other CU state active | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Other CU state active from clock cycle signal | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Other CU state active clock cycle signal set | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Other CU state active clock cycle signal OK | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Other CU state ready | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Other CU state ready from clock cycle signal | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Other CU state ready clock cycle signal set | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Other CU state ready clock cycle signal OK | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Other CU state no stop response sys 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | Other CU state no stop response sys 1 from clock cycle signal | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | Other CU state no stop response sys 1 clock cycle signal set | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | Other CU state no stop response sys 1 clock cycle signal OK | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | Other CU state no stop response sys 2 | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | Other CU state no stop response sys 2 from clock cycle signal | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | Other CU state no stop response sys 2 clock cycle signal set | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | Other CU state no stop response sys 2 clock cycle signal OK | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Other CU sign-of-life bit set | No | Yes | - |
| 25 | Other CU sign-of-life bit OK | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | Other CU sign-of-life bit via DI OK | No | Yes | - |
| 27 | Other CU is living | No | Yes | - |
| 28 | Data transfer error via communication coupling | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | Data transfer error via digital inputs | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | Data transfer error | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
For CU hot standby redundancy, this bit is set.  
This bit is set from the hardware description data.

### p7119[0...1] CU redundancy fault responses filter

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6957 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0011 1111 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting to filter the fault responses for CU redundancy. Only the selected fault responses
cause a switchover to the other CU.  
Example:  
Fault FXXXXX with stop response OFF1 has occurred. A switchover is only made to the
other CU if p7119[0].0 is set = 1.  
Exceptions:  
- faults, which are entered in p7117[0..15], never cause a switchover to the other
CU.  
- messages, which are entered in p7118[0..15], always cause a switchover to the other
CU.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
System active  
[
1]:
System passive

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Fault with response OFF1 active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Fault with response OFF2 active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Fault with response OFF3 active | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Fault with no response active | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Fault with response encoder active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Fault response SYS active | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7005, p7117, p7118

### p7120 BI: CU redundant operating state signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

Sets the signal source for the operating state for redundant operation of the Control
Unit (CU).  
For CU standby redundancy (r7115.0 = 0), the following applies:  
1 signal:  
CU active state is requested.  
For CU hot standby redundancy (r7115.0 = 1), the following applies:  
1 signal:  
CU state passive is requested (CU state not ready).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6222  
Refer to:
A05074

**Note:**
  
The parameter can only be changed when the pulses are inhibited and when the line-side
circuit breakers have been opened (r6222.8 = 0).

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Displays the relative system runtime in days when the fault/alarm occurred.  
Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred for power units connected in
parallel.

**Notice:**
  
The time comprises r7123 (days) and r7102 (milliseconds).  
The time display depends on the selected mode (p3100).

**Note:**
  
The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred for power units connected in
parallel.

**Notice:**
  
The time comprises r7124 (days) and r7103 (milliseconds).  
The time display depends on the selected mode (p3100).

The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

### r7760 Write protection/know-how protection status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORGL.md#sinamics-alarms-vectorgl) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the status for the write protection and know-how protection.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Write protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Know-how protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Know-how protection temporarily withdrawn | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Know-how protection cannot be deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Extended copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Basic copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Trace and measuring functions for diagnostic purposes active | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Reserved, Siemens-internal | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7761, p7765

**Note:**
  
KHP: Know-How Protection  
For bit 00:  
Write protection can be activated/deactivated via p7761 on the Control Unit.  
For bit 01:  
The know-how protection can be activated by entering a password (p7766 ... p7768).  
For bit 02:  
If it has already been activated, know-how protection can be temporarily deactivated
by entering the valid password in p7766. In this case, bit 1 = 0 and bit 2 = 1 offset.  
For bit 03:  
Know-how protection cannot be deactivated, as p7766 is not entered in the OEM exception
list (only the factory setting is possible). This bit is only set if know-how protection
is active (bit 1 = 1) and p7766 has not been entered in the OEM exception list.  
For bit 04:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards/Control
Units. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and in p7765.0 is set
= 1.  
For bit 05:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards.
This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.1 is set = 1 and p7765.0
is set = 0.  
For bit 06:  
When know-how protection is activated, the drive data can be traced using the device
trace function. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.2
is set = 1.  
For bit 12:  
Together with p7755, the bit is used to monitor write protection.  
Bit = 1, if p7755 is not equal to 0 and write protection is active (r7760.0 = 1).  
Bit = 0, if write protection was deactivated. p7755 is set to 0, and when write protection
is activated again, bit 12 remains at 0.
